NordicSemiconductor/Android-nRF-Connect#.body: **Describe the bug** While playing around with the Local Time Information Characteristic (UUID 0x2A0F) I noticed that the timezone is wrongly displayed. For a PST timezone and no daylight saving (which should be UTC-32:0, take into account that it should display -8hs * 4 = 32) it displays `UTC+56:0` per the attached screenshot **To Reproduce** To reproduce this behavior, run the `peripheral` example on an nrf52840 (or similar with BLE capabilities). Connect to the device and set the Local Time Information with value `0xEC00` (which is timezone -32 [0xE0] and daylight time saving to 0). Once configured, read that parameter and you will read the latter screenshot **Expected behavior** The correct timezone should be displayed, in this case `UTC-32`. I assume that the value is not correctly parsed and that's why it is shown as `+60` **Screenshots** **Versions (please complete the following information):** - Android version: 16 - Phone model Pixel 8a - App Version 4.29.1 **Additional context** This arised while testing the Local Time Information characteristic from this PR
NordicSemiconductor/Android-nRF-Connect#.title: Local time information is wrongly displayed

enjoy-digital/litex#1062.body:
### The issue
It appears that when LiteX switched from 8-bit CSR bus to 32-bit CSR bus, the Zephyr drivers were not updated. 
The riscv_litex Zephyr port is not really operable in this situation, as the Zephyr kernel isn't getting timing ticks via the Timer module Event, as the Timer register map and configuration code is wrong. 
LiteX seems to [no longer support CSR buses less than 32 bits](https://github.com/enjoy-digital/litex/commit/4ee9c53f185ac6dd9c4aa69a7547e9bc037acc25), so the CSR bus cannot be set back to 8 bits (Removing this assert it builds, but the BIOS hangs)



### Driver status
The Zephyr drivers seem fall into two categories, those using the HAL [soc/riscv/litex/soc.h:litex_write](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/soc/riscv/litex-vexriscv/soc.h), and those using the sys_write8. Those using the HAL may be easier to update, as they may only need register map updates

Drivers using the HAL:
- [gpio_litex.c](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/drivers/gpio/gpio_litex.c)
- [i2c_litex.c](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/drivers/i2c/i2c_litex.c)
- [i2s_litex.c](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/drivers/i2s/i2s_litex.c) and [.h](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/drivers/i2s/i2s_litex.h)

Drivers needing partial-re-writes to use HAL:
- [hwinfo_litex.c](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/drivers/hwinfo/hwinfo_litex.c)
- [eth_liteeth.c](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/drivers/ethernet/eth_liteeth.c)
- [litex_timer.c](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/drivers/timer/litex_timer.c)
- [pwm_litex.c](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/drivers/pwm/pwm_litex.c)
- [uart_liteuart.c](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/drivers/serial/uart_liteuart.c) - Works, but may want updates to HAL anyway

Not sure:
- [lock_control_litex.c](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/drivers/clock_control/clock_control_litex.c)/[h](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/drivers/clock_control/clock_control_litex.h)

### Path forward
I wish to be friendly and respectful, I am entirely new to this ecosystem and would like to contribute in the form of fixing these drivers.

Before I do this I think there needs to be a discussion:
- Have I diagnosed this issue correctly?
- Do the original authors intend to fix this (AntMicro?), or is this is a good candidate for me to attempt to fix?
- What is the best way to get questions about LiteX platform answered (IRC can be slow, but works!). This started as a discussion on IRC, Florent asked me to create an issue. 
- Come up with test methodology for platforms I do not have (hwinfo, i2c, i2s)
- I initially created an [issue on the Zephyr repo](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/39298) about the timer not working, before I understood the scope of the problem. Should I close this?
enjoy-digital/litex#1062.title: Zephyr: riscv_litex drivers broken by 32-bit CSR Bus

zephyrproject-rtos/sdk-ng#1038.body:
We are seeing the following:

```
west build -b nrf54l15dk/nrf54l15/cpuapp samples\hello_world -- -DCONFIG_LTO=y -DCONFIG_ISR_TABLES_LOCAL_DECLARATION=y
[snip]
zephyr-sdk/arm-zephyr-eabi/bin/../lib/gcc/arm-zephyr-eabi/12.2.0/../../../../arm-zephyr-eabi/bin/ld.bfd.exe: cannot find C:\Users\...\AppData\Local\Temp\ccqKSDAR.ltrans0.ltrans.o: No such file or directory
zephyr-sdk/arm-zephyr-eabi/bin/../lib/gcc/arm-zephyr-eabi/12.2.0/../../../../arm-zephyr-eabi/bin/ld.bfd.exe: cannot find C:\Users\...\AppData\Local\Temp\ccqKSDAR.ltrans1.ltrans.o: No such file or directory
```

Changing all instances of `-flto=auto` to `-flto=1` fixes the issue, so this is clearly a multithreading issue.
Also, this does not happen with GNU Arm Embedded, so perhaps we should take a look at how things are compiled there.

There's some precedent here:
https://github.com/Nuitka/Nuitka/issues/1768#issuecomment-1231659291

@stephanosio I see that there's a difference between building the toolchain with win32-threads or posix-threads, which one do we use currently?
zephyrproject-rtos/sdk-ng#1038.title: GCC: LTO: Windows builds fail with `-flto=auto` in some (slow) machines

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#27673.body:
**dual slot: execution in place**
[mcuboot direct execute-in-place (XIP) mode](https://github.com/JuulLabs-OSS/mcuboot/pull/739) was recently
introduced. This mode allow to run either image form slot_0 and slot_1. Mcuboot doesn't perform any swap operation - just discovery which image may and should to be run and run it.

- [x] Update DFU submodules for make them able to operate either on slot_0 and slot_1
- [ ] allow to build application either for slot_0 or slot_1
      Best if both at the same time.
- [x] mcumgr/SMP support for recognizing which image need to be upload (to be defined)
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#27673.title: DFU: support mcuboot direct execute-in-place (XIP)

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#48394.body:
`vsnprintfcb` will write to the given string buffer even if the pointer is `NULL`:

https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/3462a2a5912bea924ada551f514522083b5d66df/lib/os/cbprintf.c#L105-L109

Apparently having `str==NULL && size==0` is valid: https://stackoverflow.com/a/37173612

The code above obviously needs to be wrapped in an `if` condition, but what about `struct str_ctx`? Adding 0 to a NULL pointer is fine AFAIK, but `cbvprintf` may do something naughty with that.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#48394.title: vsnprintfcb writes to *str if it is NULL

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#49155.body:
**Is your enhancement proposal related to a problem? Please describe.**
It's currently not possible to relocate a zephyr heap (located in `noinit` section). 
Additionally, the `__noinit` and `__noinit_named` macros extend to a section containing `__FILE__` (for gcc) which contains characters that are incompatible for the `linker.cmd` to work.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Adding an additional target to the cmake `zephyr_code_relocate` extension for the `noinit` section.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I've submitted a proposal [here](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/39615), however this affects the build mechanism greatly, and doesn't work for generator expressions.

**Additional context**
A simple example to demonstrate relocating a heap to `DTCM`, which currently doesn't build/work.

```cmake
# CMakeLists.txt
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20.0)
find_package(Zephyr REQUIRED HINTS $ENV{ZEPHYR_BASE})
project(relocate)

# relocating everything to DTCM would also relocate instructions
zephyr_code_relocate(src/main.c DTCM_NOINIT)

target_sources(app PRIVATE src/main.c)
```

```c
/* src/main.c */
#include <zephyr/kernel.h>

K_HEAP_DEFINE(custom_heap, 1024);

void main(void)
{
	void *ptr = k_heap_alloc(&custom_heap, 64, K_FOREVER);
	k_heap_free(&custom_heap, ptr);
}
```

```ini
# prj.conf
CONFIG_CODE_DATA_RELOCATION=y
```
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#49155.title: Ability to relocate noinit section variables

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#53904.body:
**Is your enhancement proposal related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently it holds only single scenario where unaligned flash access is tested.
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/tree/main/tests/drivers/flash
Some parts of flash_simulator test could probably be reused for this
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/tree/main/tests/drivers/flash_simulator

**Describe the solution you'd like**
The test should be universal flash access test available to test flash drivers, independent from how they are connected to a device (SoC internal, SPI, QSPI, I2c, and so on).
Scenarios minimally should include:
 - page erase -- make sure only selected page gets erasded;
 - aligned/unaligned flash read -- configurable, some devices support it directly some not and some use software solution for that;
 - read across page boundaries
 - erase across page boundaries
 - aligned/unaligned write (same as with read)
 - write across page boundaries
 
Gradually configurations for all supported flash drivers should be added.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Current alternative is people using flash sample to test their devices and drivers.

**Additional context**
Original test:
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/tree/main/tests/drivers/flash
Flash simulator test:
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/tree/main/tests/drivers/flash_simulator
SPI flash access sample:
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/tree/main/samples/drivers/spi_flash
SoC flash access sample:
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/tree/main/samples/drivers/soc_flash_nrf
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#53904.title: tests/flash: Test is very incomplete and needs extending

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#55186.body:
**Describe the bug**
It appears as though the original `fnmatch()` implementation that was checked-in with 486b731b40717a16f619b07e5d58aaf7d5143e78 has bugs. This is not surprising since it was checked-in without any test coverage.

The actual failing cases are commented-out in `tests/lib/posix/common/src/fnmatch.c` (part of #55184)

Please also mention any information which could help others to understand
the problem you're facing:
 - What target platform are you using? N/A
 - What have you tried to diagnose or workaround this issue? Comment-out known failure modes in the test
 - Is this a regression? No, the bugs have always been present

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Uncomment failure modes in `tests/lib/posix/common/src/fnmatch.c`
2.`west build -p auto -b qemu_cortex_a53 -t run tests/posix/common/`
3. See error

**Expected behavior**
All test cases should pass, including known failure modes.

**Impact**
The shell has been using a buggy version of `fnmatch()` since approximately 2018.

**Logs and console output**
```
*** Booting Zephyr OS build zephyr-v3.3.0-475-gec19849fec26 ***
Running TESTSUITE posix_apis
===================================================================
START - fnmatch

    Assertion failed at WEST_TOPDIR/zephyr/tests/posix/common/src/fnmatch.c:35: posix_apis_fnmatch: fnmatch("[[?*\\]", "\\", 0) is non-zero

 FAIL - fnmatch in 0.001 seconds
===================================================================
```

**Environment (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Any
 - Toolchain (e.g Zephyr SDK, ...): Zephyr SDK v0.15.2
 - Commit SHA or Version used: b83cf1f1ee244e48a22844be181fd6ae2366e2c1 but goes as far back as 486b731b40717a16f619b07e5d58aaf7d5143e78

**Additional context**
Noticed in the context of #55185
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#55186.title: posix: fnmatch: fix known bugs

**Describe the bug**
If the peripheral host accepts a CIS request that then fails to be established, the host seemingly never receives a Disconnect event. 

**To Reproduce**
N/A

**Expected behavior**
The spec states:

> When the Controller receives the HCI_LE_Accept_CIS_Request command, the Controller sends the HCI_Command_Status event to the Host. An HCI_LE_CIS_Established event will be generated when the CIS is established or is considered lost before being established.

**Impact**
Can put the host into a non-working state, which requires a complete reset. 

**Logs and console output**N/A

**Environment (please complete the following information):**
 - Commit SHA or Version used: 1b7ef97c31f4b734cae9cfb2b251c1b86b40d109

**Additional context**
N/A

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#59922.body: **Is your enhancement proposal related to a problem? Please describe.** The existing workaround for `struct k_spinlock` occasionally being 0 bytes does not always work around things. In particular when you do pointer arithmetic, which is semi important in C, but triggers some warnings now that are showing up in gcc (not just g++). Is there a simple change that can be made so this just works better? Maybe some compiler experts know if it can be considered a size / runtime optimization? 🤷 **Describe the solution you'd like** Is there is another solution that works with like array indexing or one that does not require others to modify their source to make workarounds? Perhaps there are some Macrobatics. **Describe alternatives you've considered** Patching API consumers whenever this issue is hit in the future. **Additional context** While working on #59911
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#59922.title: kernel: spinlocks: clean up struct k_spinlock

**Describe the bug**
When using the new and old USB device stack ECM class on STM32H7 the assertion is raised due to mutex usage within ISR.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. build USB device with ECM class for STM32 MCU with enabled asserts (_`CONFIG_ASSERT=y`_)
3. run against linux

**Expected behavior**
~The existing stack doesn't have this issue. Assuming the new stack shall not fail as well.~
It should work fine with `CONFIG_ASSERT=y`.

**Impact**
The new stack stops working.

**Logs and console output**

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#61964.body: Hi, we're using the SMP to upload firmware images to the device, as well as uploading and downloading files (some of which are quite big). We have written our own implementation of an SMP client that is able to upload/download files much faster than `mcumgr`, but still I believe that the transfer rate can be improved if we skip the Base64 encoding step (which is not needed on any of the transports that we use). The SMP protocol has a "flags" field that is currently unused, that looks like a good place to add a "Skip Base64" bit. The idea is that if the client sets this bit, the server doesn't Base64 decode the incoming packet and also doesn't Base64 encode the response packet, everything else remains the same. If there's interest in this, I can start looking at what needs to change in order to make this happen.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#61964.title: Suggestion: consider making Base64 encoding optional in SMP packages

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#62791.body:
Reporting this on ESP32-S3 since that was what I had tested, but this likely applies to all ESP32-* Xtensa and RISC-V cores.

OpenOCD v0.12.0 added Zephyr OS awareness and also supports the ESP32-S3 chip directly.  However, setting `CONFIG_DEBUG_THREAD_INFO=y` results in a debug error:

```
Open On-Chip Debugger 0.12.0+dev-01324-gfb52ba4fa (2023-09-19-00:01)
Licensed under GNU GPL v2
For bug reports, read
	http://openocd.org/doc/doxygen/bugs.html
Info : only one transport option; autoselecting 'jtag'
Info : esp_usb_jtag: VID set to 0x303a and PID to 0x1001
Info : esp_usb_jtag: capabilities descriptor set to 0x2000
force hard breakpoints
can't read "_TARGETNAME": no such variable
```

Adding `set _TARGETNAME esp32s3.cpu1` to the openocd.cfg file to bypass probing shows the OpenOCD error
```
Info : Zephyr: looking for target: esp32s3
Error: Could not find target in Zephyr compatibility list
```

Looking at the v0.12 OpenOCD code, the Zephyr RTOS support shows that only arm and arcv2 architectures are supported.
```
static struct zephyr_params zephyr_params_list[] = {
	{
		.target_name = "cortex_m",
		.pointer_width = 4,
		.callee_saved_stacking = &arm_callee_saved_stacking,
		.cpu_saved_nofp_stacking = &arm_cpu_saved_nofp_stacking,
		.cpu_saved_fp_stacking = &arm_cpu_saved_fp_stacking,
		.get_cpu_state = &zephyr_get_arm_state,
	},
	{
		.target_name = "cortex_r4",
		.pointer_width = 4,
		.callee_saved_stacking = &arm_callee_saved_stacking,
		.cpu_saved_nofp_stacking = &arm_cpu_saved_nofp_stacking,
		.cpu_saved_fp_stacking = &arm_cpu_saved_fp_stacking,
		.get_cpu_state = &zephyr_get_arm_state,
	},
	{
		.target_name = "hla_target",
		.pointer_width = 4,
		.callee_saved_stacking = &arm_callee_saved_stacking,
		.cpu_saved_nofp_stacking = &arm_cpu_saved_nofp_stacking,
		.cpu_saved_fp_stacking = &arm_cpu_saved_fp_stacking,
		.get_cpu_state = &zephyr_get_arm_state,

	},
	{
		.target_name = "arcv2",
		.pointer_width = 4,
		.callee_saved_stacking = &arc_callee_saved_stacking,
		.cpu_saved_nofp_stacking = &arc_cpu_saved_stacking,
		.get_cpu_state = &zephyr_get_arc_state,
	},
	{
		.target_name = NULL
	}
};
```
See the following links for the code
 * Official OpenOCD - https://github.com/openocd-org/openocd/blob/master/src/rtos/zephyr.c#L335
 * EspressIf Branch of OpenOCD - https://github.com/espressif/openocd-esp32/blob/master/src/rtos/zephyr.c#L334


**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add Zephyr RTOS thread awareness to OpenOCD / GDB to enable multi-threaded debugging when running `west debug` for ESP32-S3 targets (this would likely work for ESP32 and ESP32-S2 targets as well).

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
No known alternatives to this for Zephyr RTOS other than using the tracing and logging frameworks for debugging.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#62791.title: Add ESP32-* OpenOCD / GDB Zephyr Thread Awareness

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#64954.body:
**Is your enhancement proposal related to a problem? Please describe.**
The current DTLS server socket implementation only supports a single client peer, because it has a single TLS context per socket file descriptor.
A side effect of this limitation is that, if the same host client initializes multiple handshakes on different ports, the server responds to all requests on the initial port.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
 An improvement would be to have a TLS context for each peer (`host:port` combination).

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
An idea I briefly looked into, is to split the `subsys/net/lib/sockets/sockets_tls.c` file into a TLS and a DTLS part, but nothing concrete.

**Additional context**
N/A
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#64954.title: DTLS server socket support multiple clients

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#68946.body:
**Bug description**

When running static analysis through CodeChecker support in the build system (`-DZEPHYR_SCA_VARIANT=codechecker`), [Cppcheck](https://cppcheck.sourceforge.io/) fails to analyze the code.

Found on 3.4, but also verified to fail in 3.5.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Introduce some undefined behaviour construct (for instance, zero-division) in for example `zephyr/samples/hello_world/src/main.c`
2. `west build -b qemu_x86 -- -DZEPHYR_SCA_VARIANT=codechecker`
3. The defect is detected
4. Include <zephyr/kernel.h> at the top of the source file
5. Build again (`west build`), and no defect is found for the source file containing the issue

**Expected behavior**
Native integration of static code analysis should work when including Zephyr headers. Otherwise, it offers no functionality for real projects

**Impact**
I can temporarily get static code analysis with a workaround using a script that removes `zephyr/include` from the list of include paths (`-I` parameter) for the call to `cppcheck` executable

**Environment (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux
 - Toolchain (Zephyr SDK, tested with both `arm-zephyr-eabi` and `x86_64-zephyr-elf`)
 - v3.5.0 (`a6eef0ba3755f2530c5ce93524e5ac4f5be30194`)

**Additional context**
I have tried running `CodeChecker analyze` with the same arguments set by the build system. Then, when I omit the Cppcheck option `--suppress=preprocessorErrorDirective`, error `#error "Invalid/unknown toolchain configuration"` is raising in `zephyr/include/zephyr/toolchain.h`. When `<zephyr/kernel.h>` is not included in sample source file, probably `zephyr/toolchain.h` gets never included, because even removing `--suppress=preprocessorErrorDirective`, this `#error` is not thrown, performing the static code analysis as expected.

Ccing @pdgendt
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#68946.title: CMake: SCA: Cppcheck finds no defects when including <zephyr/kernel.h>

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#69317.body:
For hwmv2, there should be a match between the names and values of SoCs, this should take the form of:
```
config SOC_SERIES
        default "nrf51" if SOC_SERIES_NRF51
        default "nrf52" if SOC_SERIES_NRF52
```

As it stands today without any exceptions, the following would be wrong:
```
config SOC_SERIES
        default "nrf51" if SOC_SERIES_NRF51X
        default "nrf52" if SOC_SERIES_NRF52X
```

The reason for needing alignment in SOC_SERIES and SOC names/values is so that CI checks can be added for spotting typos, as there currently is with boards in hwmv2 now. An example hwmv2 `Kconfig.<board>` file:
```
config BOARD_V2M_BEETLE
        select SOC_BEETLE_R0
```
and corresponding `board.yml` file:
```
board:
  name: v2m_beetle
  vendor: ARM
  socs:
  - name: beetle_r0
```
If there is a typo in a board name, the following illustrates how a mismatch is now detected in hwmv2 which would not be caught had the `bool` prompt be left:
```
config BOARD_V2M_BEETLE
        bool
        select SOC_BEETLE_R0

config BOARD_V8M_BEETLE
        bool
        select SOC_BEETLE_R0
```
There is similar checking which has been added for SOC_PART_NUMBER whereby issues have been found and corrected.

This checking can be expanded to SOC_SERIES and SOC, as of right now the aim is for the values of these to match the name without any rules or exceptions but this is not currently adhered to by all converted hwmv2 SoCs.

Boards/socs must be fixed so that the value in soc.yml matches the text string of the Kconfig which matches (in UPPER CASE) of the Kconfig symbol. E.g. if soc.yml has:
```
family:
- name: arm
  series:
  - name: mps2
    socs:
    - name: an383
```
then the Kconfigs must be:
```
config SOC_FAMILY_ARM
...

config SOC_FAMILY
        default "arm" if SOC_FAMILY_ARM

config SOC_SERIES_MPS2
...

config SOC_SERIES
        default "mps2" if SOC_SERIES_MPS2

config SOC_AN383
...

config SOC
        default "an383" if SOC_AN383
```

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#70597.body:
With HWMv2 the board.yml now defines board revisions like:
```
   revision:
     format: ["major.minor.patch", "letter", "number", "custom"]
     exact: [true, false]
     revisions:
      - name: <revA>
      - name: <revB>
      - name: <revC>
      - ...
```

this means it is now possible to extend `list_boards` and `west boards` with revision information for the known revision types.

Limitation will be with `custom` as this revision type is handled internally by CMake for full flexibility.
However, `custom` with a list of `revisions` names could still allow most to return revisions for some forms of custom revisions.

Even if custom revisions cannot be returned by list_boards in all cases, then returning known revisions when possible will be a big improvement.

Known revisions should then be used to facilitated auto completion of board names for `west` commands,  namely `west build -b <TAB>`
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#70597.title: Ability to autocomplete board revisions (`west build`)

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#71069.body:
**Is your enhancement proposal related to a problem? Please describe.**
1) The documentation does not make it obviously clear if using `malloc()` or `k_malloc()` is the preferred method for dynamic memory allocation.
2) There currently is no clean way of getting heap statistics for the _standard library_, such as with `mallinfo()`.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
1) Provide the user with a standard library function to understand how much memory is left for allocation via `malloc()`.
    - This could be completed through added support for `mallinfo()` for structure statistics and `malloc_stats()` for printed statistics ([newlib C, section 3.25](https://sourceware.org/newlib/libc.html#mallinfo)).
    - Completion by adding the `_mallinfo()` and `_malloc_stats()` stubs for the standard library.
    - For newlib, this could be achieved in */lib/libc/newlib/libc-hooks.c* by using existing variables: `HEAP_BASE` and `heap_sz`.

2) Provide *clear* documentation on *why* a user may want to use `malloc()` directly or stick to `k_malloc()`.
    - To my understanding...
        - `malloc()` is general purpose and supported no matter the standard C library chosen to build with. The memory sourced may go through a virtualization step where memory may no longer be continuous regions.
        - `k_malloc()` requires additional setup through the `CONFIG_HEAP_MEM_POOL_SIZE` option and is useful if memory should be allocated from a common memory region.
    - If this is correct, this should be stated in the documentation directly so others do not need to sift through multiple function calls to figure out what the difference is. Why one may be better for a task than the other.

3) Provide a memory summary documentation page.
Looking through several issues, conversations about the heap talk about the heap available to the standard c libraries (malloc) and the system heap (k_alloc) interchangeably when *they do not source their memory from the same heap*.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

- Currently no way to know how much memory is left for `malloc()` or other heap statistics available to the standard library.
- Previous issues of such discussions about stats for malloc:
    - #29735 
    - #23076
    - #57725 

**Additional context**

- Zephyr currently implements a heap size function for the user-defined system heap: `sys_heap_runtime_stats_get()` shown in this [sample](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/2c722f97b54f6479b176b2abb8b92fa8145277e9/samples/basic/sys_heap/src/main.c#L41). This function does not appear anywhere in the latest Zephyr documentation.
- The kernel shell service that uses aforementioned function above `cmd_kernel_heap()`, [code link](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/2c722f97b54f6479b176b2abb8b92fa8145277e9/subsys/shell/modules/kernel_service.c#L280C12-L280C27), also does not appear anywhere in the latest Zephyr documentation. It provides stats for the `_system_heap`, which is used for `k_malloc()`. This is useful for that single memory region, which is not the same memory region as `heap_base` where `malloc()` is sourcing its memory from (when using newlib).
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#71069.title: Heap & Memory Allocation Statistics: add mallinfo support & better dynamic memory documentation

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#71416.body:
**Describe the bug**
Compiling blinky with GCC works fine, but the same app compiled with ARM Compiler 6 fails with the below described error when linking.

```
C:\WINDOWS\system32\cmd.exe /C "cd . && C:\workspace\ArmCompilers\bin\armlink.exe  --cpu=cortex-m4   --via=CMakeFiles\zephyr_pre0.rsp -o zephyr\zephyr_pre0.elf && C:\WINDOWS\system32\cmd.exe /C "cd /D C:\workspace\gitlab\zephyrproject-rtos\zephyr\build\zephyr && "C:\Program Files\CMake\bin\cmake.exe" -E true""
Error: L6384E: No Load Execution Region of name bss seen yet at line 11.
Error: L6384E: No Load Execution Region of name bss seen yet at line 15.
```

The errors seems to be from the below lines (not able to find `__bss_start` symbol) in the cmake\linker_script\arm\linker.cmake. I am able to work-around the errors by hardcoding instead of using `__bss_start`

```
zephyr_linker_symbol(SYMBOL __kernel_ram_start EXPR "(@__bss_start@)")
zephyr_linker_symbol(SYMBOL __kernel_ram_end  EXPR "(${RAM_ADDR} + ${RAM_SIZE})")
zephyr_linker_symbol(SYMBOL __kernel_ram_size EXPR "(@__kernel_ram_end@ - @__bss_start@)")
```

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. `$Env:ZEPHYR_TOOLCHAIN_VARIANT="armclang"`
2. `west build -p always -b cy8cproto_063_ble .\samples\basic\blinky\`

**Expected behavior**
blinky should compile and link successfully with ARM compiler as well

**Impact**
Unable to use ARM Compiler for our platforms

**Logs and console output**
```
west build -p always -b cy8cproto_063_ble .\samples\basic\blinky\
-- west build: making build dir C:\workspace\gitlab\zephyrproject-rtos\zephyr\build pristine
-- west build: generating a build system
Loading Zephyr default modules (Zephyr base).
-- Application: C:/workspace/gitlab/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/samples/basic/blinky
-- CMake version: 3.29.0
-- Found Python3: C:/workspace/gitlab/zephyrproject-rtos/.venv/Scripts/python.exe (found suitable version "3.11.0", minimum required is "3.8") found components: Interpreter
-- Cache files will be written to: C:/workspace/gitlab/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/.cache
-- Zephyr version: 3.6.99 (C:/workspace/gitlab/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr)
-- Found west (found suitable version "1.2.0", minimum required is "0.14.0")
```

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#73679.body:
**Is your enhancement proposal related to a problem? Please describe.**

On `esp32s3`, logging is fairly slow. The default level of logging during the boot process adds about 100ms to the boot time.  That's a showstopper for applications like mine where the time from power up to application code running is critical.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

I want to be able to disable all non-error logging prior to the application `main` via Kconfig, similar to the `CONFIG_BOOTLOADER_LOG_LEVEL_WARN` option in ESP-IDF. Ideally it should only take one setting to do so.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

I suppose if the boot time impact of logging could be reduced below 1 ms that would also be fine, but that seems somewhere between impossible and much harder than turning it off.

Since my application logs only to the USB Serial JTAG controller and not UART0, it would also be fine for the boot code to detect whether a USB host is connected and disable logging entirely if not.

**Additional context**

I've found a few locations which are contributing to log messages not being controlled by any Kconfig option:
- [`modules/hal/espressif/zephyr/port/include/boot/mcuboot_config/mcuboot_logging.h`](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/hal_espressif/blob/5191505f915c0b1c706222f4709925a453e0e858/zephyr/port/include/boot/mcuboot_config/mcuboot_logging.h#L32-L34) defaults the `MCUBOOT_LOG_LEVEL` macro to `MCUBOOT_LOG_LEVEL_INFO`. Nothing else that I can find sets that macro, so it's effectively hard-coded to that level.
- [`modules/hal/espressif/zephyr/esp32s3/include/sdkconfig.h`](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/hal_espressif/blob/5191505f915c0b1c706222f4709925a453e0e858/zephyr/esp32s3/include/sdkconfig.h#L16) hard-codes the `CONFIG_BOOTLOADER_LOG_LEVEL` macro to `3` (info).
- [`zephyr/soc/espressif/common/loader.c`](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/4fc6506a8af33e8025e9119fdddc96e007acb3ca/soc/espressif/common/loader.c#L39-L42) has its own definition for `BOOT_LOG_INF` which prints the message unconditionally.

That list probably isn't exhaustive for all configurations, but for my particular application changing those locations gets rid of all the boot-time messages.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#73679.title: esp32s3: disable verbose logging during boot

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#78724.body:
**Describe the bug**
The checksum offloading capabilities of an Ethernet interface are not respected for virtual network interfaces as the function `need_calc_checksum()` in "net_if.c" always returns true for the virtual interfaces. This increases CPU utilization as checksums are calculated in software even though they would be automatically calculated by the Ethernet MAC. Even worse, some Ethernet MACs require the checksum to be cleared to zero for TX checksum offloading as otherwise an incorrect checksum will be generated by the Ethernet MAC (for example [NXP MIMXRT1060-EVK](https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/boards/nxp/mimxrt1060_evk/doc/index.html)).

**To Reproduce**
Use the [Virtual LAN](https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/samples/net/vlan/README.html) sample
1. disable IPv6 as IPv6 will disable TX/RX checksum offloading on i.MX RT1060 (`CONFIG_NET_IPV6=n`)
2. west build --pristine -b mimxrt1060_evk samples/net/vlan
3. west flash
4. From PC, ping 192.0.2.1 (untagged, working)
5. From PC, ping 198.51.100.1 (VLAN 100, request timed out)
6. Incorrect checksum (0xffff) for IP header and ICMP message will be shown in Wireshark for the Echo reply

**Expected behavior**
TX/RX checksum offloading should also work on virtual network interfaces used for Virtual LAN (VLAN).

**Impact**
TX/RX checksum offloading can be disabled for i.MX RT1060 by setting `CONFIG_ETH_NXP_ENET_HW_ACCELERATION=n`. Therefore, impact is mainly the increased CPU utilization.

**Environment:**
 - OS: Windows
 - Zephyr SDK 0.16.8
 - Zephyr 3.7.0
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#78724.title: net: checksum offloading not working for virtual network interfaces used for Virtual LAN (VLAN)

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#78813.body:
<!--
A clear and concise description of what the problem is.
-->

The [basic button example](https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/samples/basic/button/README.html) shows the use of [`gpio-keys`](https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/build/dts/api/bindings/input/gpio-keys.html) but does not use the [input subsys](https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/services/input/index.html) to read the button events, instead it initializes the gpio pin again and reads it with GPIO API.

<!--
A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.
-->
It has to be decided if this example wants to show how to use GPIO API or how to use `gpio-keys`. If the answer is GPIO API, `zephyr-user` can be used;

```devicetree
#include <zephyr/dt-bindings/gpio/gpio.h>

/ {
     zephyr,user {
             signal-gpios = < &gpio0 PIN FLAGS >;
     };
};
``` 

<!--
A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.
-->

Alternatively, `gpio-keys` can be used fully and input subsystem can be showcased.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#78813.title: Button example is not using `gpio-keys` driver

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#79069.body:
**Is your enhancement proposal related to a problem? Please describe.**
Some benchmarks shows Zephyr behind in context swap performance compared to ThreadX.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Avoid any branch-link (function call) operations in PendSV handling for Arm, likely other archs could have the same idea implemented.

Every bl op is a potential pipeline flush, certainly some lost context, we almost immediately call out to a C function handler for PendSV handling (used for Arm context swap). There's several other bl ops involved depending on which options are involved.

ThreadX avoids almost all bl ops except a hook for swap in/swap out that is opt in. Otherwise has ~80 asm instructions for PendSV handling. Clearly has some performance implications somewhere here, maybe partially due to the branch out of inline asm. Perhaps other things, needs investigating.

https://github.com/eclipse-threadx/threadx/blob/master/ports_arch/ARMv7-M/threadx/gnu/src/tx_thread_schedule.S#L131

https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/arch/arm/core/cortex_m/swap_helper.S#L56

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Not doing anything

**Additional context**

Benchmark report showing difference in context swap performance, on a cortex-m4 
https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/opimwfbvkd9coeprc7d5h/Beningo_RtosPerformance_2024_Report.pdf?rlkey=s3n007s6hgubnj37ovto88bs2&e=3&dl=0

In large part the difference is due to our MPU usage for hw stack protection by default, but this isn't the *only* thing playing a part.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#79069.title: Improve Arm (And other Arch) Context Swap Performance

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#84934.body:
**Describe the bug**
This change to `/drivers/usb/device/usb_dc_stm32.c` in 3.7.0 has broken USB for the SOC_STM32F723XX.
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/70850/commits/9587b7e369e129df1da4c2077d2bae1eb051624b

In the previous implementation, the STMF723XX would make the following calls:
```
LL_AHB1_GRP1_EnableClock(LL_AHB1_GRP1_PERIPH_OTGHSULPI);
LL_APB2_GRP1_EnableClock(LL_APB2_GRP1_PERIPH_OTGPHYC);
```
In the 3.7.0 implementation, the STMF723XX makes these call:
```
LL_AHB1_GRP1_DisableClockLowPower(LL_AHB1_GRP1_PERIPH_OTGHSULPI);
LL_APB2_GRP1_EnableClock(LL_APB2_GRP1_PERIPH_OTGPHYC);
```
This ultimately causes `USB_CoreReset` in `modules/hal/stm32/stm32cube/stm32f7xx/drivers/src/stm32f7xx_ll_usb.c` to return a timeout which gets bubbled up and caused `usb_enable()` to return -EIO.  The USB devices are not enumerated/discovered by the host system.

With the previous implementation, no errors are returned and the USB devices function as expected.

**To Reproduce**

**Expected behavior**
`usb_enable()` should not return an error and USB devices should enumerate on the host.

**Impact**
Showstopper: USB devices are unusable on STM32F723XX MCUs.

**Logs and console output**

**Environment (please complete the following information):**
Built against the Zephyr 3.7.0 release branch.
Tested on a Fedora Linux host system.

**Additional context**
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#84934.title: USB devices broken on STM32F723XX MCUs after clock enable changes in Zephyr v3.7.0

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#85662.body:
<!--
**Notes**
Github Discussions (https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/discussions)
are available to first verify that the issue is a genuine Zephyr bug and not a
consequence of Zephyr services misuse.

This issue list is only for bugs in the main Zephyr code base
(https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/). If the bug is for a project
fork (such as NCS) specific feature, please open an issue in the fork project
instead.
-->

**Describe the bug**

While tests are batched run and the system's under heavy load, `tests/misc/llext-edk/misc.edk.pytest` fails with the following error:


```
-------------------------------- live log call ---------------------------------
INFO: -- west build: running target llext-edk
[1/1] Generating llext-edk.tar.xz
-- Found Python3: /usr/bin/python (found suitable version "3.10.12", minimum required is "3.10") found components: Interpreter
-- Cache files will be written to: /root/.cache/zephyr
DEBUG: Copying llext-edk.tar.xz to /tmp/tmps7cievp7
DEBUG: Extracting llext-edk.tar.xz to /tmp/tmps7cievp7
INFO:
DEBUG: Copying extension to /tmp/tmprpbpnrf2
DEBUG: Copying file2hex.py to /tmp/tmprpbpnrf2
DEBUG: Building extension in /tmp/tmprpbpnrf2 - cmake
INFO: -- The C compiler identification is GNU 12.2.0
-- The CXX compiler identification is GNU 12.2.0
-- Configuring done
-- Generating done
-- Build files have been written to: /tmp/tmprpbpnrf2/build
DEBUG: Building extension in /tmp/tmprpbpnrf2 - make
make: warning: jobserver unavailable: using -j1.  Add '+' to parent make rule.
/tmp/tmprpbpnrf2/src/main.c:7:10: fatal error: zephyr/kernel.h: No such file or directory
7 | #include <zephyr/kernel.h>
|          ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
compilation terminated.
make[2]: *** [CMakeFiles/extension.dir/build.make:73: extension.llext] Error 1
make[1]: *** [CMakeFiles/Makefile2:83: CMakeFiles/extension.dir/all] Error 2
make: *** [Makefile:91: all] Error 2
FAILED
```

but if we run this test alone (and on a fresh out directory)
`west twister -p qemu_cortex_r5/zynqmp_rpu -s tests/misc/llext-edk/misc.edk.pytest` it passes.

If the test has failed, trying to re-run whist preserving the output directory will keep on failing. The output directory (for that specific test is enough) needs to be removed in order to get the tests to build from fresh & pass.

 - What target platform are you using?
   Was noticed with `qemu_cortex_r5/zynqmp_rpu`. I don't know if there are other platforms running this specific test.
 - What have you tried to diagnose or workaround this issue?
   Clearing the tests output directory and running this test alone. It passed.
 - Is this a regression? If yes, have you been able to "git bisect" it to a
   specific commit?
   It might be but considering how long it takes to run the suites and trigger the issue, a bisect hasn't be an option.

**To Reproduce**

Run `west twister -p qemu_cortex_r5/zynqmp_rpu`

**Expected behavior**

The test should build and pass on the first run.

**Impact**

It is pretty significant as it prevents local & CI run to pass.

**Logs and console output**

If applicable, add console logs or other types of debug information
e.g Wireshark capture or Logic analyzer capture (upload in zip archive).
copy-and-paste text and put a code fence (\`\`\`) before and after, to help
explain the issue. (if unable to obtain text log, add a screenshot)
-->

**Environment (please complete the following information):**

 - OS: Linux (the CI docker container 0.27.4)
 - Commit SHA: caa284e0355
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#85662.title: `tests/misc/llext-edk/misc.edk.pytest` build failure when running under load

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#86073.body:
**Is your enhancement proposal related to a problem? Please describe.**
Run smoketest for each BTP service in BSIM. Several services may run at the same time, e.g. BAP may run BAP, PACS and ASCS and thus no need to run ASCS separately. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Run smoke test for the following services:

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#86223.body:
**Is your enhancement proposal related to a problem? Please describe.**
The current description for Kconfig symbol `CONFIG_WDT_DISABLE_AT_BOOT` is:
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/9b2fdace1d4c9686d43fbd8fad87b9620f576671/drivers/watchdog/Kconfig#L17-L21

Expected behavior when `CONFIG_WDT_DISABLE_AT_BOOT=y` can be easily understood: WDT drivers __must disable__ the timer.
However, the expected behavior when `CONFIG_WDT_DISABLE_AT_BOOT=n` is not specified and could be either of:
* _Do not disable the WDT during boot_ [if it is enabled by default]
  * i.e., do nothing
* _Enable the WDT during boot_

The currently implemented behavior varies from driver to driver.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
The expected driver behavior when `CONFIG_WDT_DISABLE_AT_BOOT=n` should be agreed upon and documented.
Non-conforming drivers should be updated to implemented the agreed upon and documented behavior.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
1. Keep expected behavior when `CONFIG_WDT_DISABLE_AT_BOOT=n` undocumented.

2. Replace `WDT_DISABLE_AT_BOOT` with more generic and explicit `choice WDT_BOOT_BEHAVIOR` such as:
* `WDT_HARDWARE_DEFAULT`
  * Driver should configure the WDT (e.g., reload counter, prescaler, etc)
  * Driver __must not__ disable or enable the WDT
  * After boot, the WDT status will depend on default hardware behavior
    * If WDT is enabled by default on SoC, it will be enabled
    * If WDT is disabled by default on SoC, it will be disabled
* `WDT_DISABLE_AT_BOOT`
  * Driver should not configure the WDT, and __must__ explicitly disable it
  * This option can be gated behind `HAS_WDT_DISABLE_AT_BOOT`
* `WDT_ENABLE_AT_BOOT`
  * Driver should configure and __must__ explicitly enable (if applicable) the WDT

Unanswered question: where should the default configuration come from? (STM32 has platform-specific Kconfig for this)

**Additional context**
The `samples/drivers/watchdog` and `tests/drivers/watchdog/wdt_basic_api` applications are setting `CONFIG_WDT_DISABLE_AT_BOOT=n`.
Since commit 2509de7d3e134f25eba4c9e132e13892f3898df5, these tests will "fail" on STM32 platforms, because the STM32 IWDG timer driver interprets `CONFIG_WDT_DISABLE_AT_BOOT=n` as _enable the WDT during boot_.
Since the timer can be configured only once, `wdt_install_timeout` returns `-ENOMEM` and a test failure is reported... despite the board getting reset by WDT after a little while. Replacing with `CONFIG_WDT_DISABLE_AT_BOOT=y` allows the tests to succeed.

In these two applications, `CONFIG_WDT_DISABLE_AT_BOOT=n` seems to have been set under the interpretation "_do not disable the WDT if enabled by default_", which breaks when the underlying implementation understands it as "_enable and configure the WDT_".
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#86223.title: Clarify expected behavior when `CONFIG_WDT_DISABLE_AT_BOOT=n`

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#86444.body: **Describe the bug** With latest Zephyr Downstream release, we found one critical issue that BLE didn’t work after erasing board then flash BT SHELL project, it can be reproduced with any RW612 board once erasing the board before test. - What target platform are you using? NXP RW610/RW612 - What have you tried to diagnose or workaround this issue? After debug further, we found issue was caused by sysworkq which excuted the bt_ready() function got blocked during the ECDH operations through long work queue. Details: sysworkq task will call bt_init() -> bt_smp_int()->bt_pub_key_gen()->bt_long_wq task to generate BT public key, but during generating the random data through long work queue in z_impl_sys_csrand_get(), bt_ready() was also called by sysworkq to settings_load() ---->generate random data via same interface, although the task priority of sysworkq was higher than bt_long_wq task, bt_long_wq still completes the operation in z_impl_sys_csrand_get() due to mutex protection, sysworkq was in blocked state during this period. After bt_long_wq task done and released Mutex, it should be sysworkq turn to acquire the ctr_lock mutex per our understanding, But issue was the sysworkq task just blocked there forever and never run into the z_impl_sys_csrand_get() again to complete the bt_ready() call trace. Attached few pictures for your reference, you can see after issue hit, the call stack suddenly changed to main task which is unexpected. - Is this a regression? If yes, have you been able to "git bisect" it to a specific commit? - yes, it's regression. Previous our v4.1 release at Jan/20 doesn’t have this issue as there is no ECDH enabled nor longwq called from bt_init(). **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. west build -p always -b rd_rw612_bga zephyr/tests/bluetooth/shell -d ble_build/bt_shell 2. flash zephyr.elf to RW612 board 3. power on board 4. bt init, bt scan on 5. see error **Expected behavior** bt init and bt scan on should all success **Impact** BLE can't be used on any board which erasing flash before using **Logs and console output** *** Booting Zephyr OS build nxp-v4.0.0-13647-g0c6e38f51f3c *** Type "help" for supported commands.Before any Bluetooth commands you must `bt init` to initialize the stack. uart:~$ bt init Bluetooth initialized [00:00:02.652,169] fs_nvs: 8 Sectors of 4096 bytes [00:00:02.652,185] fs_nvs: alloc wra: 0, fd0 [00:00:02.652,189] fs_nvs: data wra: 0, 13 [00:00:02.905,481] bt_hci_core: No ID address. App must call settings_load() uart:~$ uart:~$ uart:~$ bt scan on Bluetooth set active scan failed (err -11) uart:~$ **Environment (please complete the following information):** - OS: Linux - Toolchain: Zephyr SDK - Commit SHA or Version used: 9cc8301dfaa759490af638876938c5113805dc77 **Additional context** We're not able to locate the root cause yet why sysworkq task was blocked during bt init, but refer to this commit : https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/commit/c7f3ad6307a034beb486077899440cb6a2eeeca5 We switched to use the system workq to perform any ECDH operations other than via long workq, so we add CONFIG_BT_LONG_WQ=n to prj.conf and compile bt_Shell app, issue was gone, BLE can work normally after every erasing board. It's just the workaround we made to unblock the BT SHELL using. Could you please help to fix this issue from root cause? Thanks!
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#86444.title: BLE can't be used after erasing board and flash BT SHELL app

#### As soon as possible
- [x] Ensure there is a draft `doc/releases/release-notes-4.2.rst` and `doc/releases/migration-guide-4.2.rst` with feature summary from the previous one and if not create one (previous #69418)
- [x] Ensure the draft release_notes and migration guide for this release (4.2) are included in the toc (https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/86866)
- [x] Ensure MAINTAINERS.yml "Release Notes"'s maintainers to point at the current release owners (https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/88675)
- [x] Set the current release managers as assignee's in this issue and ensure it is linked from the [release plan](https://github.com/orgs/zephyrproject-rtos/projects/13).

#### From day one but with extra focus as we approach RC1 and after:
- [x] Check the CI [weekly](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/actions/workflows/twister.yaml?query=event%3Aschedule) run for build failures that should be fixed and report or fix them.
- [x] Make sure the release plan is kept up to date (https://github.com/orgs/zephyrproject-rtos/projects/13)
- [x] Ensure the [release management page](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/wiki/Release-Management) has an up-to-date schedule for the release

#### One month before RC1:
- [x] Ping west project maintainers to remind them of the RC1/freeze deadline and to consider if their modules should be updated (you may want to extract all west projects' maintainers names from the MAINTAINERS files and create an issue like) (https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/91788)
- [x] Start giving heads ups on the TSC meeting about the incoming freeze.
- [x] Check on the status for features to be deprecated as listed in https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/77070
- [x] Ensure that the latest Zephyr SDK contains all the changes necessary to support the new features included in, or planned for this release; if not, ping the SDK maintainer (@stephanosio) to release a new SDK version.
    - [x] Check if any toolchain-related enhancements and bug fixes are required for any of the pull requests targeting this release. If any of these enhancements and bug fixes are not part of the latest Zephyr SDK, a new SDK version must be released.
    - [ ] Check that the Picolibc sha in the latest SDK (check [sdk-ng](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/sdk-ng) for the sha of the picolibc submodule for the current tag) matches the `picolibc` module revision in [`west.yml`](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/west.yml). If they do not match, a new SDK version must be released. (ping @stephanosio and @keith-packard )

#### Two weeks before RC1:
- [x] Send a heads ups about the incoming freeze to dev-mailing list and Discord General channel

#### Pre RC1
- [x] Create next release (v4.2.0) milestone so that features that don't make it to the release can be assigned a milestone
- [x] Verify that all security-critical external components and external dependencies are up to date
  - (4.1) https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/85522
  - (4.1) https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/85523
  - (4.2) https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/92149
- [x] Check known vulnerabilities, and fix exploitable vulnerabilities or verify them as un-exploitable (ask Security working group).
- [x] Make sure there's no pending ["Release Blocker"](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+label%3A%22Release+Blocker%22) issues
- [x] Create a "Deprecated APIs/options to be removed by x.y" issue for 4.3 (N+3), linking to it from & to the 4.2 one (https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/77070) . Remember to set the `deprecation_tracker` label.

See https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/project/release_process.html#tagging for tagging details, release specific filters under https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/wiki/Filters to find pending PRs.

- [x] Update Release Notes and Migration Guide
  - Be sure to remind maintainers to update the release notes, at the very least in the rc1, rc2 and rc3 emails.

#### After RC3, before final release

- [x] Create `backport v4.2-branch` label, use it for fixes that are not bad enough to go in RC3
- [x] Finalize Release Notes and Migration Guide: (previous: #76113, #76295 )
  - [x] Review `doc/releases/release-notes-4.2.rst` overview summary and details sections
- [x] Add new version in `doc/conf.py`, `doc/releases/index.rst`, and `doc/releases/eol_releases.rst` (previous  #76047)
- [x] Remove EOL version(s) in `doc/conf.py`, `doc/releases/index.rst`, and `doc/releases/eol_releases.rst`  : (previous: #69404) .

- [x] Tag v4.2.0 (see https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/project/release_process.html#tagging)
- [x] Update `doc/LICENSING.rst` for new components that do not have a license: (previous: #69392) (ping @nashif to check)
- [x] Update and publish the [draft GitHub release](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/releases) created by the CI.

### Directly After Tagging Release

### After Release

  - [x] Close v4.2.0 milestone
  - [x] Create checklist issue for next release
  - [x] Add a comment to this issue linking to the next release checklist issue

  - [ ] If you're a first time release manager, you may contact Tracey (or [info@zephyrproject.org](mailto:info@zephyrproject.org)) to claim your Release Manager badge.

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#88385.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
There are several ESP32-S3 Dev boards with Parallel LCDs. It would be helpful to incorporate support for this display interface on the ESP32-S3 within Zephyr so that LVGL can be used to drive more immersive graphics on this platform.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Ideally driver support is added for parallel displays with the ESP32-S3. Board support already exists for the other Espressif Dev Kits [here](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/tree/main/boards/espressif). It would be reasonable to also add support for the [ESP32-S3-LCD-EV-Board](https://docs.espressif.com/projects/esp-dev-kits/en/latest/esp32s3/esp32-s3-lcd-ev-board/index.html#esp32-s3-lcd-ev-board). In addition, other ESP32-S3 kits that implement a Parallel display could also serve as a point of reference. For example, the [Waveshare ESP32-S3 5" Parallel LCD touch kit](https://www.waveshare.com/product/esp32-s3-touch-lcd-5.htm?sku=28117) could be supported. Other Waveshare ESP32-S3 boards are already supported, but [only with a SPI display](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/tree/main/boards/waveshare/esp32s3_touch_lcd_1_28). Makerfabs is another good example of a readily available dev board for this - you can see on [this page](https://www.makerfabs.com/esp32-s3-parallel-tft-with-touch-ili9488.html?srsltid=AfmBOoozKYbKCzHtVHX8HQb2ntBbMlEZ54-3xFeDRjfTDABTZTOgnNry) the difference between driving a display over SPI vs Parallel RGB.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Currently, it is only possible to run LVGL graphical applications over parallel RGB on the ESP32-S3 by using ESP-IDF. It would be nice to be able to achieve the same graphical capabilities with Zephyr instead. SPI display support exists, but the framerate is too slow to build more complex graphical applications.

**Additional context**
Would be great to have something like this on zephyr+LVGL! instead of ESP-IDF+LVGL!
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZWtTmmne6Bo&t=99s
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#88385.title: Add support for Parallel LCDs on ESP32-S3 Platform

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#88404.body:
**Is your enhancement proposal related to a problem? Please describe.**
Current STM32 Q/O/X-SPI bindings is not following device tree specification and since https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/76735 a warning is generated when building platforms enabling Q/O/X-SPI nodes:
```
CMake Warning at /local/home/frq07517/zephyrproject/zephyr/cmake/modules/dts.cmake:425 (message):
  dtc raised one or more warnings:


  /local/home/frq07517/zephyrproject/zephyr/build/b_u585i_iot02a/zephyr/zephyr.dts:820.26-848.5:
  Warning (spi_bus_bridge): /soc/spi@420d2400: incorrect #size-cells for SPI
  bus

  <stdout>: Warning (spi_bus_reg): Failed prerequisite 'spi_bus_bridge'
```

This is because we don't respect a device tree (dtc check which is the de facto specification) which states that reg and sizes of SPI devices (nodes sitting on a SPI bus), should be defined as:
```
      reg = <FOO>;
      size = <BAR>;
```
ie `#size-cells = <0>;`

On STM32 SoCs, we're defining them as:
```
           reg = <FOO BAR>;
```
ie `#size-cells = <1>;`. This design was adopted in https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/68274 which was approved by subsytem maintainer at that time.


**Describe the solution you'd like**
Review STM32 Q/O/X-SPI devices binding to comply with dts spec and do the same as others socs description vendors, where address is defined in bus node:
```
           reg = <0x500c8000 0x1000>, <0x90000000 DT_SIZE_M(8)>;
```
and first two cells are address/size of the controller and last two cells are addressable address/size
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#88404.title: stm32: q/o/x-spi bindings generate dtc warnings

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#88460.body:
In some Kconfig files (for example: "zephyr/drivers/lora/Kconfig.sx12xx") there are line that look like this:
```
depends on DT_HAS_SEMTECH_SX1272_ENABLED || DT_HAS_SEMTECH_SX1276_ENABLED
```
It isn't clear how the symbol DT_HAS_SEMTECH_SX1262_ENABLED was constructed.
I assume it depends on a line like 'compatible = "semtech, sx1262";' existing.

This isn't documented anywhere and should be.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#88460.title: No documentation for DT_HAS_[compatible]_ENABLED

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#88499.body:
**Is your enhancement proposal related to a problem? Please describe.**
<!--
A clear and concise description of what the problem is.
-->
Currently, zbus only have synchronous listeners, allowing developers to execute a function during the zbus publication process. There is no simple way of running listeners (or callback-like functions) out of the publishing context.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
<!--
A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.
-->
The solution is to add the "async listeners." These functions will run in the work queue context rather than the publishing thread's. 

For example:
```c
static void async_listener_bar_callback(const struct zbus_channel *chan, const void *message)
{
	if (chan != &chan_foo) {
		LOG_ERR("Unexpected channel");
		return;
	}

	const struct msg_bar *msg = message;

	LOG_INF("field %d", msg->field);
}

ZBUS_ASYNC_LISTENER_DEFINE(alis_bar, async_listener_bar_callback);

ZBUS_CHAN_ADD_OBS(chan_foo, alis_bar, 5);
```

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
<!--
A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.
-->
An alternative to that is using the work queue directly. However, it would require some effort/rework from the developer every time they need it.

**Additional context**
<!--
Add any other context or graphics (drag-and-drop an image) about the feature request here.
-->
Zbus currently has the following observers: listeners, subscribers, and message subscribers. The `async listeners` seem to be missing.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#88499.title: ZBus Async Listeners

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#88601.body:
**Describe the bug**
k_poll on a pipe no longer waits for data to be available. This results in k_poll always returning immediately on a pipe. This was introduced in 4.1.0 in the following commit:

https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/commit/a9ab8cb7797d9b2783ce273f022161aea66f52a0

See the change in the `is_condition_met()` function.

**To Reproduce**
k_poll on an empty pipe

**Expected behavior**
The k_poll waits until data is available to return

**Impact**
Can cause unexpected behaviour like tasks consuming much more cpu than designed for or not allowing the cpu to sleep and save power.

**Logs and console output**
n/a

**Environment (please complete the following information):**
Zephyr 4.1.0

**Additional context**
n/a
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#88601.title: k_poll on a pipe does not wait for data to become available

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#90272.body:
## Introduction
<!--
This section targets end users, TSC members, maintainers and anyone else that might
need a quick explanation of your proposed change.
-->

### Problem description
<!--
Why do we want this change and what problem are we trying to address?
-->
The [pyKwalify](https://pypi.org/project/pykwalify/) project hasn't received updates since 2020 and the highest supported Python version is 3.9.

### Proposed change
<!--
A brief summary of the proposed change - the 10,000 ft view on what it will
change once this change is implemented.
-->
As an alternative, I propose moving to [JSON schema](https://json-schema.org/)

- An active project
- [YAML as a superset of JSON](https://json-schema-everywhere.github.io/yaml)
- Clear [specifications](https://json-schema.org/specification)
- Backed by large companies

## Detailed RFC
<!--
In this section of the document the target audience is the dev team. Upon
reading this section each engineer should have a rather clear picture of what
needs to be done in order to implement the described feature.
-->
Similar efforts have been proposed to convert our current bindings files from `pyKwalify` to [`dt-schema`](https://github.com/devicetree-org/dt-schema), which also uses `jsonschema` under the hood.
Others parts of the project should follow suite, unifying schema validation with the same vocabulary.

### Proposed change (Detailed)
<!--
This section is freeform - you should describe your change in as much detail
as possible. Please also ensure to include any context or background info here.
For example, do we have existing components which can be reused or altered.

By reading this section, each team member should be able to know what exactly
you're planning to change and how.
-->
Converting `pyKwalify` schemas to `jsonschema` is trivial for most basic types, but needs to be evaluated case-by-case.
See #90261 for an example.

### Dependencies
<!--
Highlight how the change may affect the rest of the project (new components,
modifications in other areas), or other teams/projects.
-->


### Concerns and Unresolved Questions
<!--
List any concerns, unknowns, and generally unresolved questions etc.
-->
- ~~Our current schemas could be [included](https://pykwalify.readthedocs.io/en/unstable/partial-schemas.html#include) downstream.~~ EDIT: Inclusion is to re-use schemas within files

## Alternatives
<!--
List any alternatives considered, and the reasons for choosing this option
over them.
-->
- Keep supporting `pyKwalify` schemas, and adopt the project if higher versions of Python breaks current usage.
- [pydantic](https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/) data validation

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#90614.body:
<!--
**Notes**
Github Discussions (https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/discussions)
are available to first verify that the issue is a genuine Zephyr bug and not a
consequence of Zephyr services misuse.

This issue list is only for bugs in the main Zephyr code base
(https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/). If the bug is for a project
fork (such as NCS) specific feature, please open an issue in the fork project
instead.
-->

**Describe the bug**
<!--
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

Please also mention any information which could help others to understand
the problem you're facing:
 - What target platform are you using?
 - What have you tried to diagnose or workaround this issue?
 - Is this a regression? If yes, have you been able to "git bisect" it to a
   specific commit?
 - ...
-->

On MIMXRT1062, using `spi_transceive` on an LPSPI peripheral, one word more is clocked out than requested.
This leads to issues with peripherals that are very picky about transmission lengths, e.g. TMC5041 in our case.

**To Reproduce**
<!--
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. mkdir build; cd build
2. cmake -DBOARD=board\_xyz
3. make
4. See error
-->

- Build a shell module e.g. for MIMXRT1062-EVKB with `CONFIG_SPI_SHELL=y` and `CONFIG_SPI=y`
- Use LPSPI "CPU driver" (not DMA)
- Record traces of MISO, MOSI, SCK, CS
- Run the shell commands `spi conf lpspi4 400000` and `spi transceive 00 00 00 00 00`
- Traces show one word more than requested (see **Logs and console output**)

**Expected behavior**
<!--
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.
-->

`spi_transceive` only clocks exactly the number of words requested.

**Impact**
<!--
What impact does this issue have on your progress (e.g., annoyance, showstopper)
-->

Currently not a showstopper as a patch is known:
After bisecting the issue to https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/commit/e086678d863f069ce930e2cebcbe7f825d53fd16 and some trying around, I found that `spi_transceive` worked normally again when, in https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/21b20de1eb346ab6f5264f6596b3d93ff93c65e5/drivers/spi/spi_nxp_lpspi/spi_nxp_lpspi.c#L264C1-L282C3 , the "stalling behavior end xfer workaround" is moved **before** the FIFO queue fill, or the FIFO queue fill is removed entirely. The fact that the VERID check on main is currently bugged is a separate issue which is already addressed in https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/90182 .

**Logs and console output**
<!--
If applicable, add console logs or other types of debug information
e.g Wireshark capture or Logic analyzer capture (upload in zip archive).
copy-and-paste text and put a code fence (\`\`\`) before and after, to help
explain the issue. (if unable to obtain text log, add a screenshot)
-->

1. On https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/commit/21b20de1eb346ab6f5264f6596b3d93ff93c65e5
Shell output:
```
uart:~$ spi transceive 04 00 00 00 00
TX:
00000000: 04 00 00 00 00                                   |.....            |
RX:
00000000: 00 00 00 02 00                                   |.....            |
```
Logic analyzer trace:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2e59e0c3-9cbe-4c51-80d2-a1196e127cc3)

2. With "change of order" patch described above

Shell:
```
uart:~$ spi transceive 04 00 00 00 00
TX:
00000000: 04 00 00 00 00                                   |.....            |
RX:
00000000: 00 10 00 00 05                                   |.....            |
```
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/93bab6dc-c9e0-4c64-813b-f2425250265f)

(this is the correct response to the SPI command)

**Environment (please complete the following information):**

 - OS: Linux
 - Zephyr SDK 0.17.0
 - Commit https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/commit/21b20de1eb346ab6f5264f6596b3d93ff93c65e5

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#91042.body: ### Describe the bug I'm using the USB DFU class with MCUboot. When both slots are defined in the DTS, the DFU implementation currently allows: - Upload from both alt=0 (slot-0) and alt=1 (slot-1) - Download only to alt=1 (slot-1) Attempts to download to alt=0 are explicitly rejected in the DFU handler, resulting in a transfer failure: ``` >dfu-util.exe --alt 0 --download ....\build\zephyr\zephyr.signed.bin dfu-util 0.11 Copyright 2005-2009 Weston Schmidt, Harald Welte and OpenMoko Inc. Copyright 2010-2021 Tormod Volden and Stefan Schmidt This program is Free Software and has ABSOLUTELY NO WARRANTY Please report bugs to http://sourceforge.net/p/dfu-util/tickets/ Warning: Invalid DFU suffix signature A valid DFU suffix will be required in a future dfu-util release Opening DFU capable USB device... Device ID 1fc9:ffff Device DFU version 0110 Claiming USB DFU Interface... Setting Alternate Interface #0 ... Determining device status... DFU state(2) = dfuIDLE, status(0) = No error condition is present DFU mode device DFU version 0110 Device returned transfer size 128 Copying data from PC to DFU device Download [ ] 0% 0 bytes failed! DFU state(10) = dfuERROR, status(3) = Device is unable to write memory ``` It seems that USB DFU permits downloads to only one slot, which appears logical since enabling both slots results in slot-1 being selected as the default target. This restriction becomes a problem when slot-0 contains no valid image. In such cases, MCUboot fails with: ``` E: Unable to find bootable image FIH_PANIC ``` ### Impact Prevents USB-based recovery in cases where slot-0 is invalid, requiring physical access to reflash the device. Zephyr version: v4.1
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#91042.title: USB DFU class restricts downloads to slot-1 even when recovery via slot-0 is required

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#91061.body:
### Problem Description

We have 10s of optional modules/projects that are maintained in a submanifest, those are not being pulled by default, however in CI we pull them and CI is blocked if any of those modules fails, i.e. through in-tree tests or through tests provided as part of the module.

Such optional modules SHALL never block CI.

We have issues on two levels: 
1) We have too many optional modules that zephyr does not depend on, they work with zephyr, they have samples in zephyr, but nothing in core zephyr depend on them, we pull those in every CI run to build 1 or 2 samples, we have dependencies of those in our docker images, increasing the size of the docker and then there are random python packages we pull everywhere to support optional modules that we directly do not use or depend on. 
This one is easy to fix, remove from manifest, make them external modules that someone can add to their west if they need them, figure out compatibility checking using  some CI that is not tied to our PRs or day to day development.

2) modules with cyclic dependency that have their own life and CI, zephyr does not depend on them, but they do, i.e. you change something in zephyr and they break very fast, because they depend on zephyr primitives. Those are just a few. We need to figure out a better integration mode for those and remove the cyclic dependency. 


### Proposed Change (Summary)

Review all "optional" modules in the default manifest, make them "external" where it makes sense, and if something really needed, it probably needs to be promoted to something we have in the default manifest. Then move all the in-tree tests and put them in the modules themselves, i.e. protoc and thrift, create a repo where we can have those listed and have some CI run on them on a regular basis and test against latest zephyr, but then if something fails, it does not block zephyr development and owners will have to deal with those somewhere else.


Create a repo where such moduels can be tested verified, but outside of the mainline development , see https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/external-modules for a repo that can do that (very initial setup, needs more feedback).

CI also runs on a regular basis, i.e. https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/external-modules/actions/runs/15442862785/job/43464685793

### Proposed Change (Detailed)

see above.

### Dependencies

Removal and reclassification of optional modules, document them as such and add references to the external-moduels repo.

### Concerns and Unresolved Questions

NA

### Alternatives Considered

No alternatives to the provided solution considered
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#91061.title: Review optional modules and make them external

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#91269.body:
### Describe the bug

If we try to build a program with a Flash QPSI chip that has instruction based quad enable and using nordic QSPI nor, the build fails.

In the Zephyr Flash driver for NRF QSPI Nor, there is a function call that fails compilation if the quad enable requirements are set to None.

Link to the function call:
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/98ba754013b0553a3eb96d95f3d1b13300d1b4f3/drivers/flash/nrf_qspi_nor.c#L615

This function `qspi_wait_while_writing` is being called inside the `qspi_erase` function in zephyr/drivers/flash/nrf_qspi_nor.c. 

The issue is that the function `qspi_wait_while_writing` is defined inside a preprocessor guard section `#if !IS_EQUAL(INST_0_QER, JESD216_DW15_QER_VAL_NONE)` (link : https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/98ba754013b0553a3eb96d95f3d1b13300d1b4f3/drivers/flash/nrf_qspi_nor.c#L458C1-L458C53), which does not contain the earlier mentioned function call. Ultimately, if the user builds with a QSPI Flash device with compatible "nordic,qspi-nor" and with quad-enable-requirements = "NONE", then they get compilation errors due to the  `qspi_wait_while_writing` function being called while undefined.


### Regression

- [x] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Make a simple program with a Flash QSPI chip included
2. Set the devicetree entry to for the Flash device to: `compatible = "nordic,qspi-nor"` and `quad-enable-requirements = "NONE"`
3. Try to build the program, see it fail due to compilation errors originating in zephyr/drivers/flash/nrf_qspi_nor.c.

This bug can be spotted by inspection of the zephyr/drivers/flash/nrf_qspi_nor.c file.
1. The "if quad requirements are not None" compilation guard section starts here on line 458 https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/98ba754013b0553a3eb96d95f3d1b13300d1b4f3/drivers/flash/nrf_qspi_nor.c#L458
2. The "if quad requirements are not None" compilation guard section ends here on line https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/98ba754013b0553a3eb96d95f3d1b13300d1b4f3/drivers/flash/nrf_qspi_nor.c#L570
3. The `qspi_wait_while_writing` function is defined on line 485, inside the compilation guard section between lines 458 and 570: https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/98ba754013b0553a3eb96d95f3d1b13300d1b4f3/drivers/flash/nrf_qspi_nor.c#L485
4. The `qspi_wait_while_writing` function called on line 615, outside the compilation guard section between lines 458 and 570: https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/98ba754013b0553a3eb96d95f3d1b13300d1b4f3/drivers/flash/nrf_qspi_nor.c#L615
5. Unless the `qspi_wait_while_writing` function is defined somewhere else when the guard section excludes that code, the program will fail to build. It appears to me that `qspi_wait_while_writing` taking two arguments is a statically defined function that only exists in this file.

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Major – Severely degrades functionality; workaround is difficult or unavailable.

### Environment

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#91269.title: Flash/nrf-qspi-nor : Function qspi_wait_while_writing can be called without that function being compiled

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#91373.body:
### Problem Description

This issue started from discussions in the following two PRs:

- https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/88141
- https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/90755

Which introduce similar but subtly different API types:

- #88141 Introduces a completely vendor-specific API for a hardware function that is not yet abstracted as a common driver class in Zephyr
- #90755 introduces SoC-specific APIs that the application and drivers can use to access functionality over IPC. Those will likely never be generalized at all

### Proposed Change (Summary)

The options we have for the different vendor-specific APIs are:

a. Keep the headers in `include/zephyr/drivers/soc/<vendor-function>.h` and the implementation in `drivers/soc/<vendor-function>.c`
b. Keep both headers and implementations in `soc/<vendor>/<function>`
c. Start new driver classes every time a new proposal like this comes. For example, in the case of the Renesas ELC above, a new `include/zephyr/drivers/interconnect/` could have been started that tries to generalize the API to a certain extent

### Proposed Change (Detailed)

Not available yet.

### Dependencies

_No response_

### Concerns and Unresolved Questions

_No response_

### Alternatives Considered

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#91373.title: Vendor-specific APIs in Zephyr
