commit 03684e8572b84148cedbd82d5eaf9f2773e1f451
Author: Mike J. Chen <mjchen@google.com>
Date:   Wed Jun 11 18:39:03 2025 -0700

    drivers: spi: mcux_flexcomm: make async mode with dma work

    The current driver implementation would block even when the async
    API was invoked, so it wasn't really async.

    This CL also fully chains the DMA transfer using multiple dma blocks
    and makes the number of dma blocks available a config value. The
    increase in number of dma blocks is needed so that a spi_buf_set
    that has many entries can be converted into chained dma transfers
    with the last transfer in a separate block that will set the EOT flag.

    Also make some improvements:
    1) When doing single cnt transfer, don't use the SDK driver but
       use a new fucntion spi_mcux_transfer_single_word().
       It's much more efficient and does not use an ISR
       like the SDK function does just to send one word.

    2) Fix calls to spi_context_update_tx/rx() so that the
       correct word size is passed in, instead of previously
       being hardcoded to 1. This only matters when word size
       is two. Evaluate the word_size_bytes and word_size_bits
       once and store the values in data instead of computing
       it multiple times in various parts of the driver

    3) When CONFIG_SPI_MCUX_FLEXCOMM_DMA is defined, we
       do not use the IRQ handler, so add #ifdefs to compile
       that code out. This reduces code size.

    Signed-off-by: Mike J. Chen <mjchen@google.com>

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98344.body:
## Bug Description

**Issue**: TLB invalidation is not being broadcast to all CPUs on ARM64 SMP systems

On multi-core ARM64 systems with SMP enabled, the TLB (Translation Lookaside
Buffer) invalidation operations is only affecting the current CPU. The code 
is using `TLBI VMALLE1` i.e. invalidates TLBs only on the local CPU.

**Impact**:
- Other CPUs retained stale TLB entries pointing to outdated page table mappings
- This cause memory management inconsistencies when page tables are modified
- Results in many SMP test failures with userspace enabled on Arm's FVP (Fixed Virtual Platform)

**Root Cause**:
The non-shareable TLB invalidation instructions don't broadcast to other
cores in an SMP system. On multi-core systems, when one CPU modifies page
tables,  all CPUs need to invalidate their cached translations to ensure
memory coherency.

**Fix**:
1. Use Inner Shareable (IS) variants on SMP configurations:
   - `TLBI VMALLE1IS` instead of `VMALLE1` - broadcasts full TLB invalidation
   - `TLBI VAE1IS` for page-specific invalidation - broadcasts single-page invalidation
2. These IS instructions ensure TLB invalidations reach all CPUs in the Inner Shareable domain

The fix is implemented in PR #97887.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98344.title: ARM64: Bad SMP TLB invalidation on SMP systems

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98351.body:
The code performs data cache invalidation operations after every MMU mapping 
change, which causes multiple issues on ARM64 systems.

**Problems**:
1. **Deadlock on memory mapping operations**:
   - `arch_mem_map()` called with `K_MEM_MAP_UNPAGED` triggers page faults
     when accessing unmapped regions for cache operations
   - The page fault handler in `do_page_fault()` tries to reacquire
     `z_mm_lock` which is already held by the caller of `arch_mem_map()`
   - This results in a deadlock that causes CI test failures on Arm FVP SMP
     configurations

2. **Unnecessary cache operations**:
   - Cache invalidation is being performed on _every_ mapping change
   - ARM64 implementations use Physically Indexed, Physically Tagged (PIPT)
     data caches where cache maintenance is not needed during MMU
     modifications
   - These operations are unnecessary and add performance overhead

3. **Ineffective workaround**:
   - The cache operations were originally added to work around Xen not
     virtualizing set/way cache instructions (commit c9b534c4ebca "arch:
     arm64: mmu: avoid using of set/way cache instructions")
   - However, a subsequent commit (c4ffadb0b61f "arch: arm64: avoid
     invalidating of RO mem after mem map") disabled cache operations for
     read-only mappings, making the workaround inoperative on half the
     mappings
   - This renders the performance cost unjustifiable since it doesn't
     actually solve a problem as invalidating the cache before enabling the
     MMU is not strictly needed.

**Impact**:
- Performance degradation from unnecessary cache invalidations
- System hangs when demand paging or memory mapping operations are involved
- Deadlock-induced CI test failures on Arm FVP SMP configurations

**Fix**:
Revert the problematic commits and simply dispense with cache invalidation
before enabling the MMU:
- ARM64's PIPT data cache architecture means cache maintenance is simply
  not needed during MMU operations
- The original `sys_cache_data_invd_all()` wasn't strictly necessary either
  and should have just been removed as its replacement makes things worse
  and not uniformly applied anyway (i.e. RO mappings are ignored).

The fix is implemented in PR #97886.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98351.title: ARM64: Cache invalidation operations during MMU mapping changes cause deadlocks and unnecessary performance overhead

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98353.body:
The MPSC test consumer calls `spsc_acquire()` without checking the return 
value, then unconditionally calls `spsc_produce()`. This leads to silent 
node loss when the SPSC free queue is full.

**Problems**:
1. **Unchecked return value**:
   - `spsc_acquire()` is called without checking if it returns NULL
   - When the SPSC free queue is full, `spsc_acquire()` returns NULL and 
    does not increment the acquire counter

2. **Silent failure in produce**:
   - `spsc_produce()` only produces if the acquire counter is greater than 0
   - When `spsc_acquire()` fails, `spsc_produce()` silently does nothing
   - This results in nodes being lost instead of returned to producer queues

3. **Race condition under load**:
   - The bug manifests on 4-CPU configurations (at least on QEMU)
   - Producers put all nodes in flight simultaneously while the single
     consumer cannot keep up
   - This causes frequent SPSC queue full conditions, triggering the bug 
     repeatedly

**Impact**:
- Lost nodes in the MPSC lockfree data structure test
- Test failures on multi-CPU configurations (4 or more CPUs)
- The test fails to properly validate the lockfree implementation under 
  high contention

**Fix**:
Loop on `spsc_acquire()` until it succeeds before calling `spsc_produce()`:
- Add a while loop that retries `spsc_acquire()` when it returns NULL
- Call `k_yield()` between retries to allow other threads to make progress
- This ensures nodes are always successfully returned to producer queues

The fix is implemented in PR #97740.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98353.title: MPSC test consumer incorrectly handles node returns to producer free queues, causing lost nodes

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98378.body: ### Describe the bug When running [west spdx](https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/develop/west/zephyr-cmds.html#software-bill-of-materials-west-spdx), the copyright information is not properly parsed from the source code. The error is the following: The issue relates to a change in API of REUSE. The following code in the zspdx scanner is faulty: https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/0ffef90a01f3881777942c569ed6a0c3e6e39dd7/scripts/west_commands/zspdx/scanner.py#L196-L198 The `copyright_lines` field does not exist anymore. In its place, there is a list of copyright notices accessible through `info.copyright_notices`. Each notice being an object of type [CopyrightNotice](https://reuse.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api/reuse.copyright.html#reuse.copyright.CopyrightNotice) . Suggested fix: ```python for info in infos: for notice in info.copyright_notices: copyrights.extend([notice.original]) ``` ### Regression - [ ] This is a regression. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Set the `CONFIG_BUILD_OUTPUT_META=y` in the hello world sample. 2. west spdx --init -d build 3. west build -d build -p always -b frdm_rw612 zephyr/samples/hello_world 4. west spdx -d build 5. Check the output warnings and the result in `app.spdx` ### Relevant log output ```shell walking through targets walking through pending sources files walking through pending relationships scanning files in package app-sources in document app-sources WARNING: Error getting copyright info for /home/user/zephyr-workspace/zephyr/samples/hello_world/src/main.c: 'ReuseInfo' object has no attribute 'copyright_lines' ``` ### Impact Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality. ### Environment - Commit: `ec3bcd39307abbb9c5df0b2d100706ba27ffe9e4` ### Additional Context _No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98378.title: west:spdx: Broken copyright identification with REUSE

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98410.body:
The url specified in the module.yml for the WBA6_LinkLayer15_4_Zephyr.a and WBA6_LinkLayer_Thread_lib_Zephyr.a libraries takes the main branch as reference. 
This url format generates a fragility about the association of zephyr and hal_stm32 to a specific historical version of the libraries. Only the last pushed version could be fetched.

In order to fix this fragility, the 'main' in the url should be replaced by the SHA-1 of the commit corresponding to the version of the libraries.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98410.title: hal_stm32 : stm32wbax: Link Layer url versioning fragility reference

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98425.body:
### Describe the bug

The TX result after a `stm32wba_802154_ral_transmit()` function call, in the ieee802154 driver for the stm32wbax, indicates a failure status (-35)

The issue comes from the `stm32wba_802154_ral_transmit()` implementation in which the parameters 'maximimum frame retries' and 'maximum number of CSMA backoffs' are not taken in account correctly in case of the link layer library is configured for MAC only without Openthread over the function `stm32wba_802154_ral_set_config_lib_params()` called by `stm32wba_802154_driver_init()` in zephyr/drivers/ieee802154/ieee802154_stm32wba.c

@asm5878 @etienne-lms

### Steps to reproduce

run echo_client and echo_server sample application with overlay 802154 with following command lines:
* echo-client side:
```
west build -b nucleo_wba65ri samples/net/sockets/echo_client -DEXTRA_CONF_FILE=overlay-802154.conf -p
west flash -d build/nucleo_wba65ri/echo_client
```
* echo-server side:
```
west build -b nucleo_wba65ri samples/net/sockets/echo_server -DEXTRA_CONF_FILE=overlay-802154.conf -p
west flash -d build/nucleo_wba65ri/echo_server
```
We can observe too many "send failure status -35" logs on the echo server side

### Relevant log output

[00:06:50.307,000] <wrn> net_if: iface 1 pkt 0x200120a8 send failure status -35

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98491.body:
### Describe the bug

While building `qemu_riscv64` with lld, and code model large, I get undefined symbol `z_stack_space_get`.
Taking a look this actually seems to be **true**. 
Since: 
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/05899583fdcd1d2a10e0ef3ca619fe5c57367041/kernel/thread.c#L869
introduced in https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/22520 guards the definition of `z_stack_space_get`, but not the usage in `arch_thread_priv_stack_space_get`
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/f0da1d79ff7e003050f91164ae3939e75d3b50ad/arch/riscv/core/thread.c#L216

which is guarded by 

https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/f0da1d79ff7e003050f91164ae3939e75d3b50ad/arch/riscv/core/thread.c#L127

there is also `CONFIG_ARCH_HAS_THREAD_PRIV_STACK_SPACE_GET` introduced in https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/77425 guarding the definition of `arch_thread_priv_stack_space_get` in the headers, but not the compilation unit itself, which is unconditionally compiled.

I have a workaround currently, but I get the feeling that someone more knowledgable of this should take a look and decide how it SHOULD work. There are multiple paths towards a solution
* a separate function definition 
* making the Kconfig variable actually conditionally compile this properly
* syncing up whether to check for USERSPACE and/or INIT_STACKS

```
diff --git a/arch/riscv/core/thread.c b/arch/riscv/core/thread.c
index 773bf51479a..0da917191ab 100644
--- a/arch/riscv/core/thread.c
+++ b/arch/riscv/core/thread.c
@@ -207,6 +207,9 @@ FUNC_NORETURN void arch_user_mode_enter(k_thread_entry_t user_entry,
 int arch_thread_priv_stack_space_get(const struct k_thread *thread, size_t *stack_size,
                                     size_t *unused_ptr)
 {
+#if !defined(CONFIG_INIT_STACKS) || !defined(CONFIG_THREAD_STACK_INFO)
+  return -EINVAL;
+#endif
        if ((thread->base.user_options & K_USER) != K_USER) {
                return -EINVAL;
        }
```



### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

`LLVM_TOOLCHAIN_PATH=/workdir/llvm/build/ ZEPHYR_TOOLCHAIN_VARIANT=llvm west build -p -b qemu_riscv64 samples/userspace/hello_world_user/ -DCONFIG_LLVM_USE_LLD=y -DCONFIG_COMPILER_RT_RTLIB=y`

### Relevant log output

```shell
ld.lld: error: undefined symbol: z_stack_space_get
>>> referenced by thread.c
>>>               thread.c.obj:(.data.rel.ro+0x58) in archive zephyr/arch/arch/riscv/core/libarch__riscv__core.a
clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
ninja: build stopped: subcommand failed.
```

### Impact

Major – Severely degrades functionality; workaround is difficult or unavailable.

### Environment

f39f9bd38f2

### Additional Context

Why is this only being discovered in some special cases? 
I guess because linker will cull symbols not being used, is there some way to detect all undefined symbols?

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98523.body:
### Describe the bug

I was upgrading an application from v3.7.0 to v4.2.1. The application compiles fine, but when the kernel initiailzes the shell_uart backend, it triggers an assertion. 

https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/0f5e03f1fcba4326baf4507c343f3609bf32c524/include/zephyr/sys/ring_buffer.h#L173-L182

This is kinda like a regression, where the same Kconfigs that used to worked in Zephyr v3.7.0 but triggers a runtime fatal error in v4.2.0

### Regression

- [x] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configures `SHELL_BACKEND_SERIAL_TX_RING_BUFFER_SIZE=65536`
2. Compiles the application in v3.7.0 and run - works fine
3. Compiles the same application in v4.2.0 and run - triggers assert on init

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.

### Environment

- Custom toolchain
- Zephyr v4.2.1 tagged release

### Additional Context

The fix is simple as the assert message was obvious enough to lead user to the source code and realize that they have to enable `CONFIG_RING_BUFFER_LARGE`, but the upgrade should ideally be seamless especially for in-tree kernel modules such as the shell uart.

See also [#85344](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/85344#issuecomment-3462718672)

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98568.body:
### Background:
* Zephyr getopt module was introduced in https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/31356 to be used by the shell.
* Zephyr's getopt is not [the standard one](https://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/009696799/functions/getopt.html). It has multiple APIs which make it more suited for a system like Zephyr where different components may want to use it for different purposes. Including APIs to [init it, get and set its internal state](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/lib/posix/c_lib_ext/getopt/getopt.h#L56).
* Several Zephyr modules (shell, net, wifi, ztest) use this getopt with these special APIs.
* The getopt module is nowadays bundled in the POSIX compatibility API subsystem (`POSIX_C_LIB_EXT`).

### Problem Description:
1. As it is not the standard `getopt()` no C library can possibly provide a Zephyr compatible version (even if such C library were to provide a standard `getopt()`)
2. As it is bundled in Zephyr's POSIX API (in POSIX_C_LIB_EXT), multiple components that depend on it are selecting `CONFIG_POSIX_C_LIB_EXT`. Zephyr core components should not depend on the POSIX API in this way.
3. `TC_PROVIDES_POSIX_C_LIB_EXT` is meant for C libraries to indicate they provide the APIs `CONFIG_POSIX_C_LIB_EXT` provides. But due to 1. no C library can possibly provide it. And therefore no C library can safely set this even if they provide fully standard compliant versions of the APIs provided by `CONFIG_POSIX_C_LIB_EXT`.

### Proposed Change

* Rename the `getopt*()` APIs `sys_getopt*()` (or whatever) and move them into a module under lib/ with its own kconfig option to enable it.
* Zephyr's users are changed to use this new component.
* The POSIX subsystem can continue providing `getopt()` by calling thru into `sys_get_opt()` and in that way retain backwards compatibility for external users. While `TC_PROVIDES_POSIX_C_LIB_EXT` is limited to promise the standard APIs.

### Dependencies

POSIX API
shell, networking, wifi, ztest (users)

### Additional info

Relates to:
* https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/95477
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98568.title: Zephyr's "getopt" should become a separate component

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98641.body:
### Summary

On STM32U585 (custom board), migrating to Zephyr v4.3.0-rc1 and the USB device stack “next”, as the current stack is flagged as deprecated. My application runs at HCLK = 40 MHz for power savings. The STM32U5 clock driver currently sets VOS3 whenever HCLK is below ~55 MHz. The new STM32 UDC driver checks the regulator voltage scaling and refuses to initialize the USB device peripheral unless VOS ≥ 2. As a result, USBD init fails, even though the same hardware/clock configuration worked with the old USB device stack.

### Current behavior

- With HCLK = 40 MHz, clock driver selects VOS3.
- During USBD bring-up, UDC checks VOS and fails with an error similar to:

```
/* Check that power range is 1 or 2 */
if (LL_PWR_GetRegulVoltageScaling() < LL_PWR_REGU_VOLTAGE_SCALE2) {
	LOG_ERR("Wrong Power range to use USB OTG HS");
	return -EIO;
}
```


### Describe the solution you'd like

Provide a supported way to run STM32U5 USB device (FS) at low HCLK (e.g., 40 MHz) while selecting VOS2, at least when USB is active, without patching Zephyr source locally and without having to raise HCLK above 55 MHz.

### Why this matters

- We have devices in the field that are already configured for 40 MHz HCLK for power consumption reasons, and I would be also scared a bit to do an OTA that affects the clock in the app, considering that bootloader will still be with old clock
- The legacy USB stack is being deprecated; we need to adopt the “next” stack 


A potential solution can be introducing a small runtime hook so peripherals that require higher VOS (e.g., USB device) can request VOS2 during activation and release it on suspend/disconnect (reference-counted, like the pm runtime).

### Alternatives

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98641.title: clock_stm32_u5: Voltage regulator high ranges cannot be selected when running at low clock frequency

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98793.body:
## Describe the bug

The zbus benchmark sample tests (`samples/subsys/zbus/benchmark`) have a
race condition on SMP systems that causes incorrect results. The tests use a
producer-consumer pattern where a producer thread publishes messages and 
consumer threads process them, updating an atomic counter. On SMP systems, 
the producer thread can complete and check the final count before all 
consumer threads finish processing, resulting in spurious test failures.

**Affected test variants:**
- `sample.zbus.benchmark_sync` (SUBSCRIBERS) - Currently filtered in
  PR #97827 (commit d93561da682e)
- `sample.zbus.benchmark_async_msg_sub` (MSG_SUBSCRIBERS) - **Still affected,
  needs filtering**

**Not affected:**
- `sample.zbus.benchmark_async` (LISTENERS) - Uses synchronous callbacks,
  no race condition

**Target platforms:**
While any SMP-capable platform could potentially be affected by this race,
it manifests on FVP Base RevC AEMv8 SMP configurations. QEMU SMP does not
exhibit the issue, likely due to different scheduling behavior.

**Root cause:**
The test uses mismatched thread priorities where consumers have higher
priority (3) than the producer (5). On uniprocessor systems, this ensures
consumers preempt the producer and complete processing before the final 
count check. On SMP systems, threads run in parallel on different CPUs, so
 the priority difference doesn't prevent the race.

**Current workaround:**
Commit d93561da682e filtered `CONFIG_SMP` from the SUBSCRIBERS variant,
but MSG_SUBSCRIBERS has identical threading and needs the same filter.

## Regression
- [ ] This is a regression.

This is a test design issue that has always existed, not a regression.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Build the MSG_SUBSCRIBERS variant for FVP SMP and run it:
```bash
west build -p -b fvp_base_revc_2xaemv8a/fvp_base_revc_2xaemv8a/smp/ns samples/subsys/zbus/benchmark -T sample.zbus.benchmark_async_msg_sub
west build -t run
```

2. Observe incorrect byte counts (non-deterministic due to race):
```
I: Bytes sent = 262144, received = 261888
```

3. Expected: received = 262144

Relevant log output

```
I: Benchmark 1 to 8 using MSG_SUBSCRIBERS to transmit with message size: 256 bytes
I: Bytes sent = 262144, received = 261888
I: Average data rate: 5.37MB/s
I: Duration: 0.046501230s
```

The received byte count is incorrect (missing 256 bytes = 1 message).

Impact

Functional Limitation – Test produces spurious failures on SMP systems, 
but the underlying zbus functionality works correctly. This is a test design
issue, not a bug in production code.

Environment

- OS: Linux
- Toolchain: Zephyr SDK 0.17.2
- Commit SHA: a3aa51393b65 (current main branch)
- Affected platforms: FVP Base RevC AEMv8 with SMP configurations

Additional Context

Technical Details:

Thread configuration:
- Producer thread: priority 5 (lower priority, runs later)
- Consumer threads (8): priority 3 (higher priority, runs first)

On SMP, the race occurs as follows:
```
CPU 0 (Producer):              CPU 1-3 (Consumers):
-----------------              ----------------------
Publish message 1
  k_fifo_put(sub1) ✓
                                 k_fifo_get() → processing
Publish message 2
  k_fifo_put(sub2) ✓
                                 k_fifo_get() → processing
...continues...
Publish message 128 ✓
atomic_get(&count)               Still processing messages...
→ Reports incomplete count!      atomic_add() comes later
```

Code references:
- Producer logic: samples/subsys/zbus/benchmark/src/benchmark.c:57-73
- MSG_SUBSCRIBER consumer: samples/subsys/zbus/benchmark/src/msg_sub.c:32-50
- Zbus notification: subsys/zbus/zbus.c:138-148 (async fifo delivery)

Why LISTENERS doesn't have this issue:

LISTENERS use synchronous callbacks that execute within `zbus_chan_pub()`
(see subsys/zbus/zbus.c:130-132):
```
case ZBUS_OBSERVER_LISTENER_TYPE: {
    obs->callback(chan);  /* Synchronous! */
    break;
}
```

All `atomic_add()` operations complete before the loop continues, so 
there's no race.

Fix:

PR #98792 contains the fix: Add not CONFIG_SMP filter to
sample.zbus.benchmark_async_msg_sub in samples/subsys/zbus/benchmark/sample.yaml,
matching the fix already applied to sample.zbus.benchmark_sync.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98793.title: Incomplete SMP filtering in samples/subsys/zbus/benchmark

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98796.body:
## Describe the bug

An assertion failure occurs in `z_get_next_switch_handle()` at 
kernel/sched.c:865 when running tests/kernel/smp_abort on SMP systems with
3 or more CPUs.

The assertion `old_thread->switch_handle == NULL` fails due to a race
condition where multiple CPUs simultaneously abort threads and use the 
shared dummy thread structure. While one CPU performs a context switch from 
the dummy thread (setting its `switch_handle`), another CPU may check the 
assertion expecting `switch_handle` to be NULL, causing it to fail.

- Target platform: ARM64 FVP (Fixed Virtual Platform) with 3+ CPUs
- The issue is timing-dependent and hardware-specific: it reproduces on 
  FVP but not on QEMU
- Adding debug traces can make the issue disappear due to timing changes
- This is a regression introduced by commit d4d51dc062bf ("kernel: Replace
  redundant switch_handle assignment with assertion")

## Regression

- [x] This is a regression.
- Introduced by: d4d51dc062bf ("kernel: Replace redundant switch_handle
  assignment with assertion")

## Steps to reproduce

1. Configure for an ARM64 FVP board with at least 3 CPUs
2. Build and run tests/kernel/smp_abort
3. The race condition requires precise timing where 3 CPUs simultaneously:
   - CPU0 aborts thread B
   - CPU1 aborts thread C
   - CPU2 aborts thread A
   - Multiple CPUs enter dummy thread state and perform context switches concurrently

## Relevant log output

```
ASSERTION FAIL [old_thread->switch_handle == NULL] @ kernel/sched.c:865
    old thread handle should be null.
```

## Impact

Intermittent – Occurs occasionally; hard to reproduce.
Causes CI to fail.

## Environment

- OS: Linux
- Toolchain: Zephyr SDK
- Platform: ARM64 FVP (Fixed Virtual Platform) with 3+ CPUs
- Commit: d4d51dc062bf or later

## Additional Context

The root cause is that the dummy thread structure is shared among all CPUs
in SMP systems. When multiple CPUs concurrently abort threads, they all use
the same dummy thread instance. The narrow timing window required for the
race makes it hardware-dependent and difficult to reproduce consistently.

The fix involves relaxing the assertion to allow `switch_handle` to be non-NULL when the old thread is the dummy thread: `old_thread->switch_handle ==
NULL || is_thread_dummy(old_thread)`, since the dummy thread can be legitimately clobbered by concurrent CPUs.

Fix available in PR #98682.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98796.title: Assertion race in z_get_next_switch_handle()

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98819.body:
## Describe the bug

The `tests/kernel/smp_abort` test fails on ARMv8-R FVP 
(`fvp_baser_aemv8r/fvp_aemv8r_aarch64/smp`) with threads continuing
execution after they should have been aborted. The test expects all
threads to be aborted through a circular dependency mechanism, but
one or more threads escape abortion and trigger assertion failures.

**Target platform:** ARM FVP Base RevC AEMv8-R (fvp_baser_aemv8r/fvp_aemv8r_aarch64/smp)

**Root cause:** The test has a timing assumption that doesn't hold on 
platforms with large emulator/simulator quantum sizes. The test creates a 
circular abort scenario where each thread (in its ISR) aborts the next 
thread in sequence. However, if the quantum is large enough, one thread's
ISR may complete and return before the "previous" thread in the chain 
even calls `k_thread_abort()` on it, preventing the circular dependency 
from forming.

**What was tried:**
- Initially suspected kernel scheduler bugs
- Investigated memory barrier issues (ARMv8-R weak memory ordering)
- Examined shared `_thread_dummy` structure race conditions
- Analyzed `__attribute_const__` optimization on `z_smp_current_get()`
- All led to discovering this was a test timing issue, not a kernel bug

## Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

No - test has always had this latent timing assumption

## Steps to reproduce

```bash
west build -p -b fvp_baser_aemv8r/fvp_aemv8r_aarch64/smp tests/kernel/smp_abort/
west build -t run
```

Relevant log output

```
START - test_smp_thread_abort_deadlock
Thread 0 started
Thread 1 started
Thread 2 started
Thread 3 started

    Assertion failed at WEST_TOPDIR/zephyr/tests/kernel/smp_abort/src/main.c:53: thread_entry: (false is false)
Thread 0 did not abort!
```

Impact

Functional Limitation – Test fails on ARMv8-R FVP, but the kernel scheduler 
code is correct. The test works on QEMU and ARMv9-A FVP due to different
timing characteristics.

Environment

- OS: Linux
- Toolchain: Zephyr SDK
- Board: fvp_baser_aemv8r/fvp_aemv8r_aarch64/smp
- Commit: a3aa51393b65

Additional Context

PR #98682 must be applied to get rid of the following:

```
ASSERTION FAIL [old_thread->switch_handle == ((void *)0)] @ WEST_TOPDIR/zephyr/kernel/sched.c:865
        old thread handle should be null.
```

This is however a separate issue unrelated to this one.

Fix: PR #98817 fixes this issue.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98819.title: tests: kernel: smp_abort timing race on ARMv8-R FVP

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98876.body: ### Describe the bug A timing issue can cause some LCD-PAR-S035 shields to be unresponsive to touch events. Writing to the display is not impacted. Because this issue is caused by marginal timing, it is not seen on all shields. Specific shields suffer from the issue more than others, and on some shields the issue may be intermittent. This is a software issue, and is not related to the revision of the shield. The issue can also be seen on multiple base-boards, and has been reported on the FRDM-MCXN947 and FRDM-RW612. When a shield is stuck in this state, the GT911 touch controller does not ACK its target address on the I2C bus. The shield I tested showing this issue is so sensitive to the timing that adding a test probe to the INT pin avoided the issue and touch started working. The issue was originally reported to me on v4.2.0, but I still see the issue with v4.3.0-rc2 The root cause is related to two signals on the GT911 touch controller: INT and Reset. Normally, INT is an output from the touch IC to the host to interrupt and indicate it detected a touch event. But during reset, the GT911 uses INT as an input to configure the I2C target address. To use the expected I2C target address on this shield, the INT signal must remain low before and after the Reset signal is deasserted. But this Reset signal is also shared with the display controller on the shield. Both the GT911 and ST7796S drivers drive this Reset signal. And when the ST7796S driver deasserts Reset, the required INT timing is not always achieved. The result is GT911 no longer ACKs the I2C target address. This captures the signals on a bad shield that does not respond to touch. The INT signal is not included here because probing the INT signal hides the issue. The GT911 touch controller is initialized first. The first Reset pulse is driven by the GT911 driver. Then the GT911 driver has multiple I2C transfers to read the GT911 firmware and write it back. Those transfers all succeed. The GT911 driver init completes, and next the ST7796S display driver initializes. It asserts Reset again for 100ms, which is what causes this issue. Afterwards, the GT911 NAKs all read/writes to its I2C address. The GT911 driver attempts to read the GT911 every 10ms and repeats this forever. This capture zooms in on a single I2C write command after the ST7796S init, and the GT911 no longer ACKs the host. Adding a probe to the INT signal on J5 pin12 hides the issue, and touch starts working. This capture shows that the INT signal is hight when the ST7796S driver asserts Reset. And the timing required for the INT signal with Reset is not met. ### Regression - [ ] This is a regression. ### Steps to reproduce Not every shield shows the issue. I tested with the FRDM-MCXN947 base-board and the LVGL demo on v4.3.0-rc2 with Zephyr SDK v0.17.4 using this build command: ``` west build -b frdm_mcxn947//cpu0 --shield lcd_par_s035_8080 samples/modules/lvgl/demos/ --pristine ``` ### Relevant log output ```shell ``` ### Impact Intermittent – Occurs occasionally; hard to reproduce. ### Environment _No response_ ### Additional Context I have a hack that prevents the issue, but it is a quick fix to demonstrate how to manage the INT signal on this shield. With this [touch_LCD-PAR-S035_hack.patch](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23344665/touch_LCD-PAR-S035_hack.patch) the INT signal remains as an output driving low through both Reset pulses. The touch works as expected. This patch was applied to v4.3.0-rc2.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98876.title: shields: NXP: LCD-PAR-S035: touchscreen may not respond

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98935.body:
### Describe the bug

The LWM2M message handling code in `subsys/net/lib/lwm2m/lwm2m_message_handling.c` uses incorrect format specifiers for `size_t` variables in the
`lwm2m_read_cached_data()` function. Specifically:

- Line 1401: `LOG_DBG("Read cached data size %u", length);` uses `%u` instead of `%zu`
- Line 1411: `LOG_DBG("Limited number of read %d", length);` uses `%d` instead of `%zu`

This causes CI build failures on ARM64 platforms where `size_t` is `long unsigned int`, triggering `-Werror=format` compilation warnings that are treated
as errors.

**Target platform**: ARM64 platforms
**Issue**: This prevents successful compilation with `-Werror=format` enabled
**Fix**: PR #98794 corrects these format specifiers to use `%zu` for proper `size_t` printing

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure a build for an ARM64 target platform
2. Build with `-Werror=format` enabled (CI configuration)
3. Compile `subsys/net/lib/lwm2m/lwm2m_message_handling.c`
4. Observe compilation error: `format '%u'/'%d' expects argument of type 'unsigned int'/'int', but argument has type 'size_t' {aka 'long unsigned int'}`

### Relevant log output

error: format '%u' expects argument of type 'unsigned int', but argument has type 'size_t' {aka 'long unsigned int'} [-Werror=format]
error: format '%d' expects argument of type 'int', but argument has type 'size_t' {aka 'long unsigned int'} [-Werror=format]

### Impact

(Prevents builds on ARM64 platforms with strict format checking.

### Environment

- OS: Linux (CI environment)
- Toolchain: Zephyr SDK
- Platform: ARM64
- Commit SHA: (current main branch before PR #98794)

### Additional Context

The bug is in function `lwm2m_read_cached_data()` at lines 1401 and 1411 in `subsys/net/lib/lwm2m/lwm2m_message_handling.c`. The `size_t` type varies
between architectures (unsigned int on 32-bit, long unsigned int on 64-bit), so the correct format specifier is always `%zu` for portable code.

Fixed by PR #98794
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98935.title: incorrect format specifiers in `lwm2m_read_cached_data()`

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98953.body:
### Describe the bug

This issue reports a critical **buffer overflow** vulnerability in the `mcux_adc16` driver's read function when handling **multi-channel sequences** that use `adc_sequence.options` (for extra samplings or continuous conversions).

- **Validation Flaw:** The driver's internal buffer size validation logic erroneously calculated the required size by considering only a **single channel** multiplied by the total number of samplings: `sizeof(uint16_t) * (extra_samplings + 1)`.
  
- **Neglected Channels:** This calculation **failed to account for the total number of channels** enabled in the `adc_sequence.channels` bitmask.
  
- **Impact:** If a sequence included multiple channels, the validation would incorrectly pass, and the subsequent ADC data read would attempt to write more data than the validated size, leading to a **buffer overflow** in the user-provided buffer.
  

**Area:** Drivers, ADC, NXP, MCUX

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

The bug is a logic error in buffer size calculation.

> **Write:**
> 
> 1. Build an application using the `mcux_adc16` driver (e.g., on an NXP platform).
>   
> 2. Configure an `adc_sequence` to include **multiple channels** (e.g., bits 0 and 1 set in `sequence.channels`).
>   
> 3. Set `sequence.options` to enable **extra samplings** (e.g., `sequence.options.extra_samplings = 1`).
>   
> 4. Call `adc_read()` with a buffer sized only for a single channel's total samples.
>   
> 5. The driver's validation will **incorrectly pass**, but the subsequent ADC transfer will overflow the buffer because the actual required size includes the total number of channels (which was neglected in the validation check). The fix involves using `POPCOUNT(sequence->channels)` for correct calculation.
>

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Not sure

### Environment

- **OS:** Windows WSL
  
- **Toolchain:** Zephyr SDK
  
- **Driver:** MCUX ADC16 Driver

### Additional Context

Additional Context: Fix Proposal is presented in the following PR: #98611
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98953.title: Drivers: MCUX ADC16 buffer overflow risk with multi-channel sequences and options

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#98955.body:
### Describe the bug

This issue reports a lack of robustness in the **airoc_mgmt_connect** function when a user attempts to connect using a Pre-Shared Key (PSK) but provides an explicit security type of **`WIFI_SECURITY_TYPE_NONE`**.

- **Problematic Behavior:** When `WIFI_SECURITY_TYPE_NONE` is provided with a PSK, the driver correctly attempts to **scan for the Access Point (AP)** to automatically determine the correct, specific security protocol (e.g., WPA2/WPA3).
  
- **Logic Flaw:** If this background scan **fails or does not find the AP**, the driver previously failed the entire connection attempt.
  
- **Impact:** The driver failed to fall back to the security parameters **originally provided by the user** (the PSK and `WIFI_SECURITY_TYPE_NONE`, which often implies the security is PSK-based and relies on the provided key). This results in an **unnecessary connection failure** due to a transient scan issue, even though the necessary credentials were provided.
  

**Area:** Drivers, Wi-Fi, AIROC, Network Management

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

The bug is a logic error related to error handling after a security-type resolution attempt.

> **Write:**
> 
> 1. Configure an application to use the AIROC Wi-Fi driver.
>   
> 2. Attempt a connection using `airoc_mgmt_connect` where:
>   
>   - A **valid PSK** is provided.
>   - The security type is explicitly set to **`WIFI_SECURITY_TYPE_NONE`**.
> 3. **Manually induce a scan failure** (e.g., by temporarily disabling the AP or introducing interference) during the driver's internal security type resolution phase.
>   
> 4. The original code logic would return an error, failing the connection attempt, instead of proceeding to connect using the provided PSK. The fix ensures that if the resolved security remains **`WHD_SECURITY_UNKNOWN`**, the driver uses the user-provided parameters.
>

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Not sure

### Environment

- **OS:** Windows WSL
  
- **Toolchain:** Zephyr SDK
  
- **Driver:** AIROC Wi-Fi Management Driver

### Additional Context

Fix Proposal is presented in the following PR: #98731

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99234.body:
### Describe the bug

The eventfd implementation works nicely with the non-standard `eventfd_read` and `eventfd_write` functions.

However, when trying to use the POSIX API `read(2)` or `write(2)`, in **blocking mode**, it will end up in a deadlock if we call `read(2)` before `write(2)` (or `zvfs_eventfd_write`).

I am testing this on a Raspberry Pico 2, but I think the problem will be there in any platforms (that are not `native_posix` based).

I added a proof of concept test here: https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/99231. The added test will deadlock. I believe this test (and another one for testing POSIX write) should be included in the main development tree (once we fix the bug and the tests pass).

I investigated a bit and it looks like when we use `read(2)`, the mutex is locked inside the fdtable code [here](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/lib/os/fdtable.c#L332), so the `zvfs_eventfd_rw_op` [function](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/lib/os/zvfs/zvfs_eventfd.c#L300) gets called with the [mutex](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/lib/os/zvfs/zvfs_eventfd.c#L356) already locked. On the other hand, if we use the non-standard [function](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/lib/os/zvfs/zvfs_eventfd.c#L449) `eventfd_read`, it "bypasses" the fdtable and the mechanism works correctly.

I have hacked a workaround: do not take the mutex in the `zvfs_eventfd_rw_op` function and instead take it in `zvfs_eventfd_read`, but I am not sure if this is the correct fix. Maybe we do not want to lock the mutex in the fdtable in the first place? Or unlock the mutex before we [call the op function](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/lib/os/fdtable.c#L360)? Can anyone please clarify what is the internal expected behavior for this mutex?

Disclaimer to the POSIX police: I know `eventfd` is not POSIX specification, so we do not have a reference documentation where to get the expected behavior from, however I have tested the same usage of eventfd in Linux and FreeBSD, and we do not get a deadlock there. I am yet to test the code on NuttX, and I will come back with the results, but I bet we do not get a deadlock there either.

Thank you.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Run the test in https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/99231

### Relevant log output

```
START - test_posix_read_then_write_block
D: 0x200013b8 took mutex 0x20000cb0, count: 1, orig prio: -1
D: mutex 0x20000cb0 lock_count: 1
D: new owner of mutex 0x20000cb0: 0 (prio: -1000)
D: 0x20000d80 took mutex 0x20000298, count: 1, orig prio: 0
D: 0x20000d80 took mutex 0x20000298, count: 2, orig prio: 0
D: mutex 0x20000298 lock_count: 2
D: adjusting prio up on mutex 0x20000298
D: 0x20000d80 (ready (y/n): n) prio changed to -1 (was 0)
*** deadlock ***
```

### Impact

eventfd with POSIX API `read(2)` and `write(2)` does not work as expected 

### Environment

Any non posix_native platform

### Additional Context

See https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/99231

Tentative fix: https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/99241

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99435.body:
### Describe the bug

In the `struct dma_block_config`, the field `block_size` is described as `Number of bytes to be transferred for this block`.

The `dma_rpi_pico` driver simply passes this value on to the pico's `dma_channel_configure` function.
This function explicitly states that this value does not encode the number of bytes:
```c
/* (...)
 * \if rp2040_specific
 * On RP2040 this is just the number of transfers (NOT bytes, see \ref channel_config_set_transfer_data_size) from 0 -> 2^32 - 1.
 * \endif
 *
 * \if rp2350_specific
 * On RP2350 the low 28 bits are used to encode a number of transfers (NOT bytes, see \ref channel_config_set_transfer_data_size) and non-zero values of the top 4 bits are used to specify other options.
 * \endif
 *
 * (...)
 */
```

In my example, this has lead to the DMA sending the same buffer of u32 values 4 times until I noticed this.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

### Environment

- OS: Arch Linux (dev container), Fedora OS Silverblue (host)
- Toolchain: zephyr-sdk 0.17.4
- Zephyr version: 4.3.99


### Additional Context

_No response_

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99453.body:
### Describe the bug

#87988 introduced a zero-current-offset, useful for hall sensors.
#95588 then rewrote the conversion algorithm from milliamps to microamps and forgot to include said zero-current-offset, breaking all peripherals that use said offset.

For my device, I use a CC6920BSO-5A, which [has a sensitivity of 264 mA/V](https://www.lcsc.com/datasheet/C2880430.pdf).

I use the following device tree node:
```dts
    curr_sens: curr_sens {
        compatible = "current-sense-amplifier";
        io-channels = <&adc1 0>;
        sense-resistor-milli-ohms = <1>;
        sense-gain-mult = <264>;
        zero-current-voltage-mv = <1650>;
    };
```

I consume about 1.1A and get the following debug output:
```none
[00:00:05.420,000] <inf> current_amp: 1667/4095, 1343041 uV, current:5087276 uA
u
```

I then patched `current_sense_amplifier_scale_ua_dt` and added:
```c
temp = temp - 1000 * (int64_t)(spec->zero_current_voltage_mv);
```

... and now it works:
```none
[00:00:21.368,000] <inf> current_amp: 1664/4095, 1340625 uV, current:-1171875 uA
```

### Regression

- [x] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Not that easy, I don't know of a dev board with a built in hall sensor. It's very trivial to reproduce logically, the offset calculation in `current_sense_amplifier_scale_dt` is simply missing in `current_sense_amplifier_scale_ua_dt`.

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

### Environment

Zephyr v4.3.0

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99453.title: sensor: current-amp: zero-current-offset no longer works

Author: Carles Cufi <carles.cufi@nordicsemi.no>
Date:   Tue Nov 11 14:13:24 2025 +0100

    include: sys: atomic: Check for ATOMIC_OPERATIONS_BUILTIN

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99472.body:
### Summary

There is currently no alignment constraint at all on the buffers allocated besides the default from `net_buf`.

USB device drivers, aligned buffers:

https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/91b1b84eee8159ce9706e8e0a3826cea04a09170/drivers/usb/udc/udc_common.c#L56-L58

USB host drivers, unaligned buffers:

https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/91b1b84eee8159ce9706e8e0a3826cea04a09170/drivers/usb/uhc/uhc_common.c#L17-L19

---

The USB Device stack has the ability to put allocate data in a `nocache` memory region, but not the USB Host stack yet

https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/3cf7b202780d788976e4bf3e46724e63d23f6bcc/include/zephyr/drivers/usb/udc_buf.h#L38-L45

(no `uhc_buf.h`)

### Describe the solution you'd like

Merge the USB device and host macros into something unified, assuming the USB host and device controllers of a same system will typically share the same requirements (i.e. host/device mode of a same controller).

### Alternatives

Develop an identical/similar infrastructure for the host side.

### Additional Context

This was investigated as a possible source of bugs encountered in some USB host drivers (more detailed report about each driver can follow in discussions).
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99472.title: USB Host Drivers: support non-cached and aligned allocation

### Describe the bug

Although the DTS assigns `clk_ibro` as the system clock, the system still uses the default clock.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. In DTS of MAX32650FTHR board, set the assigned-clock for the system clock to `clk_ibro`.
2. Build any Zephyr application for the target board.
3. Flash the firmware and boot the system.
4. Observe that the system still uses the default clock instead of `clk_ibro`.

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99496.body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

The current `compatible = "nxp,imx-flexspi-nor";` driver already supports the IS25WX01G flash driver, but not in Octal mode. Adding support would result in much higher read/write speeds.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Add the custom bits to the driver to enable the octal mode for this particular controller. I fail to grasp the custom/JEDEC handling in the current driver to add the necessary updates myself.

_An ideal situation would be something like what is described in https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/46399_

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99496.title: flash_mcux_flexspi_nor: Support Octal mode for IS25WX01G

### Describe the bug

There is [task ](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/91061) to clear optional modules from manifest.
After [PR97946 ](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/97946) there is build failure in all INTEL_ADSP platforms. SOF was moved to [extrenal-modules](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/external-modules) repo. zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99505.body:
### Describe the bug

To better understand the issues surrounding #99164 and #98866, I created a function that outputs the current MPU region configuration and then compared these. Attached are the outputs of the following MPU configurations:

* [mpu_regions_0_mcuboot.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23583362/mpu_regions_0_mcuboot.txt): Bootloader
* [mpu_regions_1_with_mcuboot.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23583359/mpu_regions_1_with_mcuboot.txt): Sample with_mcuboot original
* [mpu_regions_2_with_mcuboot_ethernet.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23583360/mpu_regions_2_with_mcuboot_ethernet.txt): Sample with_mcuboot with enabled ethernet (like in #98866)
* [mpu_regions_2_with_mcuboot_ethernet_sram2.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23583361/mpu_regions_2_with_mcuboot_ethernet_sram2.txt): Sample with_mcuboot with enabled ethernet and sram2 `zephyr,memory-attr = <DT_MEM_ARM(ATTR_MPU_RAM)>` set in dt (like proposed in #99164)

The first regions come from `mpu_regions.c`, then from the nodes with `zephyr,memory-attr`-property, and the last one is set dynamically and protects some memory (comes from `CONFIG_HW_STACK_PROTECTION`).

I noticed the following:
1. Output 0 (mcuboot): Region 2 points out that ~~[mcuboot/blob/main/boot/zephyr/boards/nucleo_h7s3l8.conf#L1](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/mcuboot/blob/main/boot/zephyr/boards/nucleo_h7s3l8.conf#L1) sets CONFIG_BOOT_DIRECT_XIP=y. This is the only board in mcuboot's board folder with this configuration set and I think this configuration should not be active for the bootloader.~~ `CONFIG_XIP` is set for mcuboot also. I think it shouldn't be set, but I don't know where it comes from. However, if we remove this, the MPU region for the internal flash `0x0800 0000` is not created. It should be created either via `zephyr, memory-attr` in the `flash0`-node of the SoC, or in `mpu_regions.c` as always RO.

2. Output 1 (with_mcuboot): Region 2 is the external flash @ `0x7000 000` with 32MB, but its size is set to the 64k of the internal flash. The entry comes from [mpu_regions. c#L23](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/5973b553ed94bc9ffea35319e08ee618bd9ba7e6/soc/st/stm32/stm32h7rsx/mpu_regions.c#L23) and ~~I don't understand the reason, since `CONFIG_FLASH_SIZE` is set to 32768 (kB) in `autoconf.h`~~ this is caused by `CONFIG_FLASH_SIZE` beeing set to 0. Not absolutely sure why, but has to do with the size/reg properties of the external flash node. I found out that with the changes from PR #99647 this is fixed.
But this region gets overlayed by another region, see my point 4 and the code could be removed from `mpu_regions. c` at all.

3. Output 0 / 1: Region 4 (read-only area in flash with unique device ID, ADC calibration data, etc.) is marked as RW in mcuboot, but correctly as RO in the sample. The reason is that CONFIG_MPU_ALLOW_FLASH_WRITE is set in mcuboot. ~~Would it be better to set (MPU_RASR_C_Msk | REGION_2K | P_RO_U_RO_Msk) here instead of REGION_FLASH_ATTR(REGION_2K)?~~
**_Edit:_** The type should be changed to `REGION_IO_ATTR` (same as region 1 "PERIPH") and size can be reduced to 512 bytes (see RM0477 table 28 and chapter 5.3.12)
This gets fixed in #99745

4. Output 1: Region 6 is the external flash @ `0x7000 000` ~~the correct size of 32MB coming from the `zephyr, memory-attr` of the ext_memory-node.~~ properties like `zephyr,memory-region` and `zephyr,memory-attr` are set in the [soc](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/dts/arm/st/h7rs/stm32h7rs.dtsi#L91) and the boards ([nucleo_h7s3l8](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/boards/st/nucleo_h7s3l8/nucleo_h7s3l8.dts#L64), [stm32h7s78_dk](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/boards/st/stm32h7s78_dk/stm32h7s78_dk-common.dtsi#L74)) - I think we can remove it from the SoC and have it only in the boards soc (same as with the sram config).
This gets fixed in #99668

5. Output 2 / 3: This is to show why we disabled sram2 per default to not overlay the ethernet dma memory regions defined earlier. I still don't know why ethernet is working if we do it anyway.

6. Output 0-3: Region 6 is configured as device memory (TEX2 S0 C0 B0) which means unaligned access is not allowed. But this happens quite often. @bklaric1 [reported](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/99164#issuecomment-3521188655) and @mariopaja [reported](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/98866#issuecomment-3491079042) such a behaviour and I get it when not building with `CONFIG_DEBUG_OPTIMIZATIONS=y`. In the mem fault prints you can see register values starting with 0x7000xxxx and having an odd LSB most likely pointing to a unaligned memory in the external flash. The external XiP flash should be configured as Normal, Write-back, no write allocate memory (TEX0 S0 C1 B1) which corresponds to `<DT_MEM_ARM(ATTR_MPU_FLASH)>`. 
This gets fixed in #99668

7. The comment [`/* The ATTR_MPU_EXTMEM attribute causing a MPU FAULT */`](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/dts/arm/st/h7rs/stm32h7rs.dtsi#L95) in the `ext_memory`-node of the SoC is definitley correct, because `ATTR_MPU_EXTMEM` results in: [`STRONGLY_ORDERED_SHAREABLE | size | NO_ACCESS_Msk`](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/include/zephyr/arch/arm/mpu/arm_mpu_v7m.h#L132) which means to no access at all - whats the purpose of this define?


### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Use the my branch https://github.com/JB-Lighting/zephyr/tree/jb_mpu_print and apply [mcuboot-extra.diff.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23583726/mcuboot-extra.diff.txt) to your mcuboot repo.

This will give you 2 complete MPU region configuration debug prints, one from mcuboot and one frome the chain-loaded application. See the output `called from` to check if it is coming from mcuboot (0x08000 xxxx) or the app (0x7000 xxxx)

I used the nucleo_h7s3l8 board, but there are only a few changes needed to run it on the stm32h7s78-dk.

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

### Environment

- OS: Windows
- Toolchain: VScode with Zephyr IDE extension, SDK 17.3
- Zephyr 4.3, see branch url above

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99634.body:
### Describe the bug

When resync is done `sntp_simple` is called in system work queue context.
The function blocks for `NET_CONFIG_SNTP_INIT_TIMEOUT` ms if SNTP server does not respond.
The system work queue is block for `NET_CONFIG_SNTP_INIT_TIMEOUT` (default `3000`) ms on timeout which is unacceptable.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Add the following code:
```diff
diff --git a/samples/net/sockets/sntp_client/src/main.c b/samples/net/sockets/sntp_client/src/main.c
index 3725f46d91a..0d4a55c416f 100644
--- a/samples/net/sockets/sntp_client/src/main.c
+++ b/samples/net/sockets/sntp_client/src/main.c
@@ -127,8 +127,20 @@ end:
        sntp_close(&ctx);
 }

+void work_cb(struct k_work __unused *w)
+{
+}
+
 int main(void)
 {
+       K_WORK_DEFINE(work, work_cb);
+       while (1) {
+               int ret = k_work_submit(&work);
+               if (ret != 1) {
+                       LOG_WRN("Queue failed: %d", ret);
+               }
+               sleep(1);
+       }
        wait_for_network();

        do_sntp(AF_INET);
```

Build the sample:

```bash
west build -p auto -b nucleo_h563zi samples/net/sockets/sntp_client/ -- -DCONFIG_NET_CONFIG_CLOCK_SNTP_INIT=y -DCONFIG_NET_CONFIG_SNTP_INIT_SERVER=\"1.2.3.4\" -DCONFIG_NET_CONFIG_SNTP_INIT_RESYNC=y -DCONFIG_NET_CONFIG_SNTP_INIT_RESYNC_ON_FAILURE_INTERVAL=15 -DCONFIG_SYSTEM_WORKQUEUE_STACK_SIZE=4096 -DCONFIG_NET_ARP_LOG_LEVEL_OFF=y
```

Flash the board:

```bash
west flash
```



### Relevant log output

```shell
$
```

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99674.body:
### Describe the bug

After [#79371](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/79371), the RX buffer is flushed after the prompt is printed. There's a possibility that the thread gets preempted after printing the prompt and the shell command came in. When the thread gets scheduled again, it clears the shell command on its way out.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

How it might happen:
1. Shell init thread set the state as `SHELL_STATE_ACTIVE` and print the prompt [here](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/613d228002ac134ab432d3d4f9280ef58c00bec4/subsys/shell/shell.c#L1455C16-L1455C34)
2. After printing the prompt, an interrupt occurs and cpu switches context
3. Host sees that there's a prompt, and send shell commands to device
4. Shell init thread get scheduled again and execute [`z_shell_backend_rx_buffer_flush(sh);`](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/613d228002ac134ab432d3d4f9280ef58c00bec4/subsys/shell/shell.c#L1463)
5. Shell command from host is flushed

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Intermittent – Occurs occasionally; hard to reproduce.

### Environment

- Zephyr v4.3.0

### Additional Context

_No response_

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99711.body:
### Describe the bug

There is an issue in the net_context / net_sockaddr_ptr logic for ieee802154 stack. The bug is cause by a wrong handling of the ieee802154 iface link address length, that can either be a 16bits short address, or a 64bits extended address.

On startup, the device is initialized with an extended address (64 bits). Any socket bind will get a struct `net_sockaddr_ptr` net_context.local reference to the iface link address with:
```
sll_addr = iface->if_dev->link_addr (with 64 bits address content)
sll_halen= 8
```
However, in case of association with a short address (16bits), only the link_addr is changed in the net_context:
```
sll_addr = iface->if_dev->link_addr (with 16 bits address content)
sll_halen= 8 (should be 2, can't be updated, local copy different from ` net_if_get_link_addr(iface)->len`.
```

After the association, the socket can't transmit or receive anymore as it's matching packet addresses with a buffer containing 2 bytes, with a size of 8.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

the sequence is:
- net_if_up(iface)
- zsock_socket(AF_PACKET, SOCK_DGRAM, htons(ETH_P_IEEE802154)) -> net_context_get()
- zsock_bind(socket, iface, AF_PACKET, htons(ETH_P_IEEE802154)) -> net_context_bind()

The bind logic populates the net_context.local with iface.link information:
```
        struct net_sockaddr_ll *ll_addr = (struct net_sockaddr_ll *)addr;
        iface = net_if_get_by_index(ll_addr->sll_ifindex);

        net_context_set_iface(context, iface);
        // from iface->if_dev->link_addr
        net_sll_ptr(&context->local)->sll_addr = net_if_get_link_addr(iface)->addr;
        net_sll_ptr(&context->local)->sll_halen = net_if_get_link_addr(iface)->len;
```

at this point, the socket has a valid context->local populated with:
```
sll_addr = net_if_get_link_addr(iface)->addr
sll_halen: initialized to 8 (extended address)
```

- ieee802154 association: successful with short_addr: set_association() is called, leading to iface.link update:
```
          struct ieee802154_context *ctx
          ctx->linkaddr.len = IEEE802154_SHORT_ADDR_LENGTH;
          short_addr_be = net_htons(short_addr);
          memcpy(ctx->linkaddr.addr, &short_addr_be, IEEE802154_SHORT_ADDR_LENGTH);
          update_net_if_link_addr(iface, ctx);
            -> net_if_set_link_addr(iface, ctx->linkaddr.addr, ctx->linkaddr.len, ctx->linkaddr.type);
```

at this point:
```
net_if_get_link_addr(iface)->addr set to new 16 bits address
net_if_get_link_addr(iface)->len = IEEE802154_SHORT_ADDR_LENGTH (2 bytes)

socket context.local:
sll_addr = net_if_get_link_addr(iface)->addr: correctly tracking the new 16 bits address
sll_halen: still 8 as local copy. len was only changed in net_if_get_link_addr(iface)->addr, but not in the socket context.local net_sockaddr_ptr
```

This issue is very hard to workaround. Binding the socket after association is not an option as it would open a short window where all the received packets would be dropped.

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Major – Severely degrades functionality; workaround is difficult or unavailable.

### Environment

- OS: linux
- Toolchain: zephyr SDK
- tested on: main branch f1c2ce4f4026bd5b212c6e4cc7d754aba69b189e

### Additional Context

The fix proposal is to deprecate the halen copy in the `net_sockaddr_ptr` structure and always use the iface link information, for ingress and egress:
```
diff --git a/subsys/net/ip/net_context.c b/subsys/net/ip/net_context.c
index f8049d6d719..a0988b22887 100644
--- a/subsys/net/ip/net_context.c
+++ b/subsys/net/ip/net_context.c
@@ -3251,6 +3251,16 @@ int net_context_recv(struct net_context *context,
                                net_sll_ptr(&context->local)->sll_halen;
 
                        if (net_sll_ptr(&context->local)->sll_addr != NULL) {
+                               /* FIXME it's not reliable to use the local sockaddr_ll_ptr here.
+                                * Although the addr pointer links to the net_linkaddr, the len
+                                * is not and can change along with the link address (ie. with
+                                * 802.15.4 extended (8) vs short (2) addresses).
+                                */
+                               struct net_linkaddr *link_addr = CONTAINER_OF(
+                                               (uint8_t(*)[NET_LINK_ADDR_MAX_LENGTH])net_sll_ptr(&context->local)->sll_addr,
+                                               struct net_linkaddr, addr);
+
+                               addr.sll_halen = link_addr->len;
                                memcpy(addr.sll_addr,
                                       net_sll_ptr(&context->local)->sll_addr,
                                       MIN(addr.sll_halen, sizeof(addr.sll_addr)));
diff --git a/subsys/net/l2/ieee802154/ieee802154.c b/subsys/net/l2/ieee802154/ieee802154.c
index 10e639cec42..0b8f0d81398 100644
--- a/subsys/net/l2/ieee802154/ieee802154.c
+++ b/subsys/net/l2/ieee802154/ieee802154.c
@@ -509,16 +509,20 @@ static int ieee802154_send(struct net_if *iface, struct net_pkt *pkt)
                           socket_type == NET_SOCK_DGRAM) {
                        struct net_sockaddr_ll *dst_addr =
                                (struct net_sockaddr_ll *)&context->remote;
-                       struct net_sockaddr_ll_ptr *src_addr =
-                               (struct net_sockaddr_ll_ptr *)&context->local;
 
                        (void)net_linkaddr_set(net_pkt_lladdr_dst(pkt),
                                               dst_addr->sll_addr,
                                               dst_addr->sll_halen);
 
+                       /* FIXME it's not reliable to use the local sockaddr_ll_ptr here.
+                        * Although the addr pointer links to the net_linkaddr, the len
+                        * is not and can change along with the link address (ie. with
+                        * 802.15.4 extended (8) vs short (2) addresses).
+                        */
+
                        (void)net_linkaddr_set(net_pkt_lladdr_src(pkt),
-                                              src_addr->sll_addr,
-                                              src_addr->sll_halen);
+                                              iface->if_dev->link_addr.addr,
+                                              iface->if_dev->link_addr.len);
                } else {
                        return -EINVAL;
                }
```
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99711.title: ieee802154: bound sockets impaired after network association

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99722.body:
### Describe the bug

When using the Zephyr HTTP server, I’ve observed that for dynamic resources, the response status line is sent as HTTP/1.1 200 without a reason-phrase (e.g., "OK"). For static resources, the correct format HTTP/1.1 200 OK is used.

Expected behavior:
According to RFC 9110, the reason-phrase in the status line is optional. However, many HTTP clients, proxies, and test suites expect the traditional, human-readable reason-phrase to be present for compatibility. For best interoperability and consistency, it would be preferable if all Zephyr HTTP responses included the reason-phrase.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

This can be easily seen on Wireshark capture of HTTP transaction.

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.

### Environment

-OS: Linux
Version: 3.7

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99722.title: net: http_server: No support for reason-phrase for chunked encoded response

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99737.body:
## Problem

I tested the [http_server](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/tree/main/samples/net/sockets/http_server) sample on stm32h7s78dk (after enabling Ethernet like here #99296) without any issues.
I then wanted to test the same sample with [overlay-tls.conf](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/samples/net/sockets/http_server/overlay-tls.conf) (https variant). But I can't get it to work.
The sample builds with addition of [overlay-tls.conf](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/samples/net/sockets/http_server/overlay-tls.conf) and I can flash it to the board without issues.
After flashing, I can ping the board and open the http website in browser (`http://<ip_address>`). That part works without any problems.
Trying to open `https://<ip_address>` doesn't work. The website doesn't load and/or says that the website can't be trusted.
The https website is also not reachable via openssl s_client. Relevant command logs are listed below:

## Setup

- **src:** I used the sample's code, without changing anything, except the two .sh scripts used to generate the certificates. 
- **certs:** since I am developing on Windows, I rewrote the [gen_ca_cert.sh](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/samples/net/sockets/http_server/src/certs/gen_ca_cert.sh) and [gen_server_cert.sh](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/samples/net/sockets/http_server/src/certs/gen_server_cert.sh) to a Powershell script:
  <details>
  <summary>Powershell script</summary>

    ```powershell
    # Cleanup
    Remove-Item src\certs\* -Exclude *.gitignore*
    
    # Generate a root CA private key
    & openssl ecparam -name prime256v1 -genkey -out src\certs\ca_privkey.pem
    
    # Generate a root CA certificate using private key
    & openssl req -new -x509 -days 1 -key src\certs\ca_privkey.pem -out src\certs\ca_cert.pem -subj "/O=Zephyrproject/CN=Zephyrproject Sample Development CA" -config "<path_to>\openssl-3.6.0\ssl\openssl.cnf"
    
    # Create DER encoded version of CA certificate
    & openssl x509 -outform der -in src\certs\ca_cert.pem -out src\certs\ca_cert.der
    
    # Generate a server private key
    & openssl ecparam -name prime256v1 -genkey -out src\certs\server_privkey.pem
    
    # Generate a certificate signing request using server key
    & openssl req -new -sha256 -key src\certs\server_privkey.pem -out src\certs\server_csr.pem -subj "/O=Zephyrproject/CN=zephyr" -config "C:\Users\111987\openssl-3.6.0\ssl\openssl.cnf"
    
    # Create a file containing server CSR extensions in UTF-8 encoding
    & Add-Content -Path src\certs\server_csr.ext -Value "subjectKeyIdentifier = hash" -Encoding Default
    & Add-Content -Path src\certs\server_csr.ext -Value "authorityKeyIdentifier = keyid,issuer" -Encoding Default
    & Add-Content -Path src\certs\server_csr.ext -Value "basicConstraints = critical,CA:FALSE" -Encoding Default
    & Add-Content -Path src\certs\server_csr.ext -Value "keyUsage = critical,digitalSignature" -Encoding Default
    & Add-Content -Path src\certs\server_csr.ext -Value "extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth" -Encoding Default
    & Add-Content -Path src\certs\server_csr.ext -Value "subjectAltName = DNS:zephyr.local,IP:10.21.20.147" -Encoding Default
    
    # Create a server certificate by signing the server CSR using the CA cert/key
    & openssl x509 -req -sha256 -CA src\certs\ca_cert.pem -CAkey src\certs\ca_privkey.pem -days 1 -CAcreateserial -CAserial src\certs\ca.srl -in src\certs\server_csr.pem -out src\certs\server_cert.pem -extfile src\certs\server_csr.ext
    
    # Create DER encoded versions of server certificate and private key
    & openssl ec -outform der -in src\certs\server_privkey.pem -out src\certs\server_privkey.der
    
    & openssl x509 -outform der -in src\certs\server_cert.pem -out src\certs\server_cert.der
    
    Write-Output "Finished generating certificates and keys"
    
    ```
    </details>

  Note that I tried getting this to work using the certificates and keys already provided in the sample, but got the same result.
  
- **config:** I used the sample's config with the addition of [overlay-tls.conf](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/samples/net/sockets/http_server/overlay-tls.conf)

Everything else stayed exactly the same.

## What I already tried

- Increasing the stack and heap size
- Changing the used SHA algorithm: I tried switching to SHA384 (note that I changed this in my Powershell script and in overlay-tls.conf: `CONFIG_PSA_WANT_ALG_SHA_384=y`)
- Increasing inactivity timeout via `CONFIG_HTTP_SERVER_CLIENT_INACTIVITY_TIMEOUT=60`
- Increasing network buffers count in `overlay-tls.conf`
- Installing certificates in Windows, so that they are trusted
- Building with `-DCONFIG_NET_SAMPLE_HTTPS_USE_ALPN`
- Connecting via curl using --insecure:
  ```
  PS <path>> curl.exe -v --compressed https://10.21.20.147 --insecure
  * Trying 10.21.20.147:443...
  * schannel: disabled automatic use of client certificate
  * schannel: using IP address, SNI is not supported by OS.
  * ALPN: curl offers http/1.1
  * schannel: failed to receive handshake, SSL/TLS connection failed
  * closing connection #0
  curl: (35) schannel: failed to receive handshake, SSL/TLS connection failed
  PS <path>>
  ```

Am I missing something obvious here? I have been at this for 2 days now and can't get it to work no matter what I try.
Also, this might not be a bug but rather a problem in my implementation. Since I don't know definitively, I will mark it as a bug.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Steps to reproduce:

1. Use an STM32H7S78-DK board
2. Enable Ethernet like in #99296
3. Build and flash the http_server sample with overlay-tls.conf.
(Optional: generate certificates and keys using the provided scripts or the PowerShell script shared above.)
4. Try to connect to the board via HTTPS (e.g., using a browser, openssl s_client, or curl).

### Relevant log output

```
Listed above in collapsible sections
```

### Impact

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

### Environment

- OS: Windows 10
- Latest commit: f1c2ce4f4026bd5b212c6e4cc7d754aba69b189e
- OpenSSL version: 3.6.0

commit 0c4abcdd29fcb828bba7a82fee9e60f0ec377a67
Author: William Tang <william.tang@nxp.com>
Date:   Mon Oct 27 20:02:20 2025 +0800

    drivers: can: mcux: flexcan: support Enhanced CAN Bit Timing registers

    Some FlexCAN devices have enhanced bit timing registers (EPRS ENCBT EDCBT)
    with extended timing configuration ranges and different behavior compared
    to standard bit timing registers.

    This change adds proper support for these enhanced registers, utilize full
    bit timing capabilities.

    Key differences for enhanced bit timing registers:
    - Extended timing ranges with larger maximum values for sjw, phase_seg1,
      phase_seg2, and prescaler fields
    - No propagation segment configuration support, so prop_seg must be 0
    - prop_seg value is used directly without the typical 'minus 1' adjustment

    The implementation adds conditional compilation based on the
    `FSL_FEATURE_FLEXCAN_HAS_ENHANCED_BIT_TIMING_REG` feature flag.
    For Enhanced registers, use extended timing limits and prop_seg directly.

    Test this commit on mimxrt1180_evk/mimxrt1189/cm33 drivers.can.timing
    test case, and enable `TEST_ALL_BITRATES` Kconfig symbol.

    Signed-off-by: William Tang <william.tang@nxp.com>

### Describe the bug

in `PM_DEVICE_ACTION_RESUME`, the spi context gets unconditionally unlocked (`spi_context_unlock_unconditionally()`) at the end of the action.
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/0f9932a9aec2d9cc9ef1f13237d5bd3ef2ac2304/drivers/spi/spi_ll_stm32.c#L1593-L1612

this looks very strange. furthermore, the context semaphor is initialized with 0 which then gets incremented here on boot, but this does not work anymore once `zephyr,pm-device-runtime-auto` has been set for the device as the resume action is only called after the look has been acquired.

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99752.body:
### Summary

The STM32H7S78-DK board under Zephyr currently doesn't support FDCAN or better said doesn't have it enabled by default.

This is due to FDCAN1 and FDCAN2 pins already being used by something else.

In the table bellow I will list all the pins that can be used for FDCAN1 and FDCAN2 and what already uses them.
Resources for information this are [stm32h7s78_dk-common.dtsi](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/boards/st/stm32h7s78_dk/stm32h7s78_dk-common.dtsi), [stm32h7s7l8hxh-pinctrl.dtsi](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/hal_stm32/blob/main/dts/st/h7/stm32h7s7l8hxh-pinctrl.dtsi) and [board's datasheet](https://www.st.com/resource/en/datasheet/stm32h7s3a8.pdf) (Table 22, Pages 132-137).

**FDCAN1:**
| Pin            | Functionality   | State                  |
| :---------- | :--------------- | :----------------- |
| PA11         | RX                   | Taken by `ltdc`   |
| PB8           | RX                   | *free*                 |
| PD0          | RX                   | Taken by `uart4` |
| PA12        | TX                    | Taken by `ltdc`   |
| PB9          | TX                    | Taken by `i2c1`   |
| PB1          | TX                    | Taken by `uart4` |

**FDCAN2:**
| Pin            | Functionality   | State                           |
| :---------- | :--------------- | :----------------------- |
| PB5          | RX                   | Taken by `timer3`        |
| PB12        | RX                   | Taken by `ltdc`             |
| PB1          | TX                    | *free*<sup>*</sup> |
| PB6          | TX                    | Taken by `i2c1`           |
| PB13        | TX                    | Taken by `ltdc`           |

**Taken by `mac` in #99296*

### Describe the solution you'd like

I propose an addition of something like this (of course adapted to this board):
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/db0c34d836aeb79ddbdff0b219499009eebc59d9/boards/waveshare/open103z/waveshare_open103z.dts#L160-L169
to the [stm32h7s78_dk-common.dtsi](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/boards/st/stm32h7s78_dk/stm32h7s78_dk-common.dtsi).

This way the user can enable FDCAN without needing to first find the pins in datasheet or pinctrl file, which makes it easier and less complex. The user can add FDCAN support with an overlay like this:
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/f1c2ce4f4026bd5b212c6e4cc7d754aba69b189e/samples/drivers/can/counter/boards/waveshare_open103z.overlay#L7-L15

The main question is what to disable, aka which pins to select for FDCAN1 or FDCAN2.

- `ltdc` pins are used for display.
- `i2c1` pins are used for touch, for the display.
- `uart4` is used for console and shell.

Note that I already successfully tested the [can/counter](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/tree/main/samples/drivers/can/counter) sample with FDCAN1 using `pa11` and `pa12` pins, with display not in use.

Would an addition like this be good? Or does it indirectly introduce more complexity into [stm32h7s78_dk-common.dtsi](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/boards/st/stm32h7s78_dk/stm32h7s78_dk-common.dtsi)?

If this addition is welcome, I would be happy to open a PR for this, with specified pins.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99752.title: boards: st: stm32h7s78_dk: add optional FDCAN support

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99762.body:
### Describe the bug

When MCUboot is configured with `slot0_partition` and `slot1_partition` on different flash devices (e.g., slot 1 on external flash), mcumgr image management cannot correctly identify the active application partition. This results in uploading new firmware to the wrong slot, corrupting the currently running image and making the device unbootable.

### Regression

- [x] This is a regression.

### Impact

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

### Additional Context

The `FIXED_PARTITION_IS_RUNNING_APP_PARTITION` macro used in `img_mgmt_active_slot()` only compares the partition offset against `CONFIG_FLASH_LOAD_OFFSET` without considering which flash device the partition resides on.

https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/32615695ad1b96670be8f2a38a8a9fd27e2d8ae5/subsys/mgmt/mcumgr/grp/img_mgmt/src/img_mgmt.c#L51-L53

When two partitions on different flash devices have overlapping offset ranges that include `CONFIG_FLASH_LOAD_OFFSET`, the macro can incorrectly identify a partition on external flash as the running partition.

In Zephyr versions prior to v4.3.0, the implementation used exact equality, so this could accidentally work in multi-device configurations when the slot offsets differed between flash devices.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99762.title: mcumgr: Image management incorrectly identifies active slot when slots are on different flash devices

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99797.body:
### Describe the bug

The [official docs](https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/services/device_mgmt/smp_groups/smp_group_0.html#date-time-command) say it takes the date/time format `“yyyy-MM-dd’T’HH:mm:ss.SSSSSSZZZZZ”`.

In reality (through code review of [`os_mgmt.c`](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/subsys/mgmt/mcumgr/grp/os_mgmt/src/os_mgmt.c)), it seems that it just parses everything after the `.` as a raw integer that represents milliseconds.

Meaning, the following stands:

`.000012` => 12ms
`.00012` => 12ms
`.0012` => 12ms
`.001200` => 1200ms, overflow

The **only** format that the parser takes is
`.SSS`, without `ZZ` and specifically **not** the `.SSSSSS` that the documentation claims.


### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Code review of https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/subsys/mgmt/mcumgr/grp/os_mgmt/src/os_mgmt.c.

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#99797.title: MCUmgr: OS: DateTime: Millisecond parsing erroneous
