zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#101226.body:
### Describe the bug

Maybe I'm doing something wrong, or this behavior is intended. But I noticed that if `CONF_FILE` is set before `configuration_files.cmake:39` checks it, Kconfig overlays in the `boards/` directory (and several other Kconfig files) of the test will be ignored. This can happen when `extra_conf_files` are supplied in the `testcase.yaml`, as they are for the LLEXT subsys test.

This causes a situation where a test will pass when ran with West by a dev, but fail with Twister in CI, because board-specific Kconfig configuration is missing.

(See my first comment below this for printouts of the `CONF_FILE` var.)

`tests/subsys/llext/testcase.yaml`:

```
common:
  tags: llext
  ...
  extra_conf_files:
     - prj.conf
...
tests:
...
  llext.writable:
    ...
    extra_conf_files: ['no_mem_protection.conf']
```

Reproducible with Twister and West with a Twister target:

```
$ west -vv build -p -b qemu_arc/qemu_arc_em tests/subsys/llext -T llext.writable
...
Parsing /home/laurenmu/zephyrproject/zephyr/tests/subsys/llext/Kconfig
Loaded configuration '/home/laurenmu/zephyrproject/zephyr/boards/qemu/arc/qemu_arc_qemu_arc_em_defconfig'
Merged configuration '/home/laurenmu/zephyrproject/zephyr/tests/subsys/llext/prj.conf'
Merged configuration '/home/laurenmu/zephyrproject/zephyr/tests/subsys/llext/no_mem_protection.conf'
Merged configuration '/home/laurenmu/zephyrproject/zephyr/build/zephyr/misc/generated/extra_kconfig_options.conf'
Configuration saved to '/home/laurenmu/zephyrproject/zephyr/build/zephyr/.config'
Kconfig header saved to '/home/laurenmu/zephyrproject/zephyr/build/zephyr/include/generated/zephyr/autoconf.h'
```

Note the missing `tests/subsys/llext/boards/qemu_arc_qemu_arc_em.conf`. After checking Kconfig with menuconfig, we can confirm the board Kconfig overlay was not applied.

When the `extra_conf_file`s are removed from the testcase.yaml or when building with West without a Twister testcase, `CONF_FILE` is empty, and the board overlay `qemu_arc_qemu_arc_em.conf` is selected:

```
Parsing /home/laurenmu/zephyrproject/zephyr/tests/subsys/llext/Kconfig
Loaded configuration '/home/laurenmu/zephyrproject/zephyr/boards/qemu/arc/qemu_arc_qemu_arc_em_defconfig'
Merged configuration '/home/laurenmu/zephyrproject/zephyr/tests/subsys/llext/prj.conf'
Merged configuration '/home/laurenmu/zephyrproject/zephyr/tests/subsys/llext/boards/qemu_arc_qemu_arc_em.conf'
Merged configuration '/home/laurenmu/zephyrproject/zephyr/build/zephyr/misc/generated/extra_kconfig_options.conf'
Configuration saved to '/home/laurenmu/zephyrproject/zephyr/build/zephyr/.config'
Kconfig header saved to '/home/laurenmu/zephyrproject/zephyr/build/zephyr/include/generated/zephyr/autoconf.h'
```

`configuration_files.cmake:39:`
```
if(NOT DEFINED CONF_FILE)
  zephyr_file(CONF_FILES ${APPLICATION_CONFIG_DIR} KCONF CONF_FILE NAMES "prj.conf" SUFFIX ${FILE_SUFFIX} REQUIRED)
  zephyr_file(CONF_FILES ${APPLICATION_CONFIG_DIR}/socs KCONF CONF_FILE QUALIFIERS SUFFIX ${FILE_SUFFIX})
  zephyr_file(CONF_FILES ${APPLICATION_CONFIG_DIR}/boards KCONF CONF_FILE SUFFIX ${FILE_SUFFIX})
else()
  string(CONFIGURE "${CONF_FILE}" CONF_FILE_EXPANDED)
  string(REPLACE " " ";" CONF_FILE_AS_LIST "${CONF_FILE_EXPANDED}")
  list(LENGTH CONF_FILE_AS_LIST CONF_FILE_LENGTH)
  if(${CONF_FILE_LENGTH} EQUAL 1)
    get_filename_component(CONF_FILE_NAME ${CONF_FILE} NAME)
    if(${CONF_FILE_NAME} MATCHES "prj_(.*).conf")
      set(CONF_FILE_FORCE_CACHE FORCE)
    endif()
  endif()
endif()
```

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

```
$ west -vv build -p -b qemu_arc/qemu_arc_em tests/subsys/llext -T llext.writable
$ twister --build-only -p qemu_arc/qemu_arc_em -s llext.writable
```

Check build output for "merged configuration" messages, and check Kconfigs.

### Impact

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu 24.04
- Toolchain: Zephyr SDK / Xtensa
- 7c39469e1757bde345e20124f33fca6600f49dd5

### Additional Context

It seems like the only test to use `extra_conf_files` in the tree is LLEXT, but it's still a useful feature provided by testcase.yaml, so other people may be using it.

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#101259.body:
### Describe the bug

I run the uvc sample `samples/subsys/usb/uvc` and test failed on Windows.

Zephyr hash: e02a1b742ae
Build command:
```
west -v build \
-b numaker_pfm_m467 \
--snippet video-sw-generator \
zephyr/samples/subsys/usb/uvc
```

Per my check, I find two points which cause my failure.
First, `uvc_compare_frmival_desc` cannot handle the case like `ia=0x28b0a, ib=0xff676980`
 https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/235aa1aa7f7cff394fbbb217ff1eb924e228ff09/subsys/usb/device_next/class/usbd_uvc.c#L1499
Where the large `ib` comes from:
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/235aa1aa7f7cff394fbbb217ff1eb924e228ff09/drivers/video/video_sw_generator.c#L435

Maybe this code can change to like:
```
return (ia > ib) ? 1 : (ia < ib) ? -1 : 0;
```

Second, there is description dependency conflict between `init_configuration_inst` and `usbd_class_init`:
  https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/235aa1aa7f7cff394fbbb217ff1eb924e228ff09/subsys/usb/device_next/usbd_init.c#L218-L229

`init_configuration_inst` relies on description being ready and does adjustment, for example, re-assign endpoint address. But for uvc, description is not yet ready until:
 https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/235aa1aa7f7cff394fbbb217ff1eb924e228ff09/subsys/usb/device_next/class/usbd_uvc.c#L1725-L1742

After fixing above two issue, I can run the uvc sample on Windows.

N/A

```

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#101296.body:
### Describe the bug

From a discussion in #99349.

> Heads up that commit https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/commit/5dd36854fde7e6497124b791243c2e8fbe747210 from this PR seems to break silabs bluetooth HCI stack, which uses a Meta-IRQ priority:
> https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/drivers/bluetooth/hci/hci_silabs_efr32.c#L250
> 
> Reverting just this commit fixes the issue, so is there a path we missed here?

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

west build -p -b xg24_dk2601b samples/bluetooth/peripheral_hr

### Relevant log output

```shell
@asmellby reported ...

I had a look at this, and I'm observing the following:

The device crashes in sys_dlist_dequeue due to a null pointer dereference

The list node in question is bt_tx_processor_workq
Both the prev and next pointers are NULL, so the dereferencing in prev->next and next->prev cause the crash
The call stack is
sys_list_dequeue
z_priq_simple_yield
z_impl_k_yield
work_queue_main
Starting from the changed logic in next_up before the crash:

thread is 0 (idle thread) and mirqp is bt_tx_processor_workq
the workqueue thread is not ready
So it seems like the new logic chooses to return to the workqueue despite it not being ready, while the old logic goes to the idle thread in this case. I don't know the kernel well to understand the implications of selecting a not-ready thread and if that directly causes the issue when that thread later tries to yield.

I reproduced this by doing the following:

Build a BT sample for a Silicon Labs board, I used west build -p -b xg24_dk2601b samples/bluetooth/peripheral_hr
Flash the sample
Use a mobile app such as Si Connect or nRF Connect to connect to the device
The connection will immediately drop due to the crash
```

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.

### Environment

_No response_

### Additional Context

The problem has been traced to the following lines in the identified commit ...

```
-		if (z_is_thread_ready(mirqp)) {
-			thread = mirqp;
-		} else {
-			_current_cpu->metairq_preempted = NULL;
-		}
+		thread = mirqp;
```

Recommendation: Revert this part of the commit.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#101296.title: kernel: Recent metaIRQ change broke bluetooth sample on Silabs

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#101339.body:
### Describe the bug

Allocation at https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/c3190d34aa912d39cd082db822f6ebb332806a2b/arch/xtensa/core/ptables.c#L1081 can fail, which then leads to an exception. The result should be checked and handled instead, at least with an assertion.

Further, if a new domain with a new L1 page table has been allocated with no mappings, then memory gets mapped so that that new domain is also updated, this line https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/c3190d34aa912d39cd082db822f6ebb332806a2b/arch/xtensa/core/ptables.c#L1072 will return `NULL` which then also will lead to an exception. When that is fixed by
```
@@ -1136,6 +1136,8 @@ static void dup_l2_table_if_needed(uint32_t *l1_table, uint32_t l1_pos)
 	uint32_t *l2_table, *src_l2_table;
 
 	src_l2_table = (uint32_t *)PTE_PPN_GET(l1_table[l1_pos]);
+	if (!src_l2_table)
+		return;
 
 	if (l2_page_tables_counter[l2_table_to_counter_pos(src_l2_table)] == 1) {
 		/* Only one user of L2 table, no need to duplicate. */
```
another exception occurs later when unmapping that memory

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Major – Severely degrades functionality; workaround is difficult or unavailable.

### Environment

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#101357.body:
### Describe the bug

I noticed when doing some development with the `frdm_mcxn236` board that my threads would be scheduled sooner/later depending on what cpu frequency scaling the board was running with.

The actual UART output is fine, so that clock is remaining stable, but the system timer used for Kernel events seems to get scaled, which is against the requirements of cpu_freq integration. 

This could just be a matter of choosing the kernel clock to be on a stable source for the SoC. But it must be fixed for the board to be usable with this subsystem.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

1. west build -p -b frdm_mcxn236 ./samples/subsys/cpu_freq/on_demand
2. Run on board
3. The logging shows timestamps from the sample, but the actual wall-clock time of the sample goes faster/slower depending on what pstate the SoC has chosen
4. UART output remains at the same Baud throughout this

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

### Environment

frdm_mxcn236

_No response_

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#101429.body:
### Describe the bug

I have an issue using Zephyr RTOS's HTTP server:

```C
static struct http_resource_detail_static_fs frontendFsDetail = {
    .common =
        {
            .bitmask_of_supported_http_methods = BIT(HTTP_GET),
            .type = HTTP_RESOURCE_TYPE_STATIC_FS,
        },
    .fs_path = "/lfs",
};

static struct http_resource_detail_dynamic statusJsonDetail = {
    .common =
        {
            .bitmask_of_supported_http_methods = BIT(HTTP_GET),
            .type = HTTP_RESOURCE_TYPE_DYNAMIC,
            .content_type = "application/json",
        },
    .cb = httpHandlerStatusJson,
    .user_data = nullptr,
};

HTTP_RESOURCE_DEFINE(frontendStatusResource, httpService, "/status", &frontendFsDetail);
HTTP_RESOURCE_DEFINE(statusJsonResource, httpService, "/status/json", &statusJsonDetail);
```

I have a static filesystem resource at "/status" and a dynamic JSON resource at "/status/json".
However, when I try to access "/status/json", I receive an error `net_http_server: fs_stat /lfs/status/json: -2` (unexpected) and my `httpHandlerStatusJson` handler is never invoked.

I believe this is a bug since I'm not using a wildcard resource and the static resource should not match "/status/json".

I have verified that the handler is not called using LOGs. I have also verified that my handler works properly by changing `/status/json` to `/status2/json`.

Zephyr v4.3.0

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Set up the HTTP server with the above resource definitions.
2. Attempt to access "/status/json" via a web client or curl.
3. Observe the `net_http_server: fs_stat /lfs/status/json: -2` error and that the handler is not triggered.

### Relevant log output

```shell
net_http_server: fs_stat /lfs/status/json: -2
```

### Impact

Not sure

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

We are using LPADC on the MCXA156 and have found that LPADC currently only supports interrupt-based data acquisition. In multi-channel/high sampling rate scenarios, DMA mode would be more efficient. According to the MCXA156 reference manual, LPADC does support DMA transfer of conversion results. We would like to know if the community/NXP has plans to add this feature.

Add DMA transfer functionality to LPADC

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#101592.body: ### Problem Description The configuration step is slow. By configuration, I mean the time spent on discovering tests, parsing yaml files, etc. before the runner starts doing its thing. ### Proposed Change (Summary) Change how the YAML schema's are handled when discovering tests. I noticed that when each YAML file is processed, the Python scripts are loading, and constructing validation YAML schema every single time, even though it never changes. In most cases the validation schema is a "constant". So my idea is to always begin by loading the validation YAML schema and store it in memory/variable. Then when we want to process and validate new YAML files, we *don't* reconstruct the YAML validator as it is an expensive process (takes around 1ms on my Linux machine). I have a working prototype locally, and for a single test run (with just one test) I am seeing a reduction in execution time of around 3 seconds. ### Proposed Change (Detailed) In `scripts/pylib/twister/scl.py` I propose to add a new function, `make_yaml_validator`. Which a class instance calls only once to create a validator from a YAML schema. ```python def make_yaml_validator(schema_path: str) -> Callable[[dict[str, Any]], None]: """ Create a reusable validator function for a fixed schema. The returned callable validates a loaded YAML document and returns None. """ if not schema_path: return lambda _data: None schema = yaml_load(schema_path) try: import pykwalify.core logging.getLogger("pykwalify.core").setLevel(50) core = pykwalify.core.Core(source_data={}, schema_data=schema) def _validate(data): core.source = data core.validate(raise_exception=True) return functools.partial(_validate) except ImportError as e: log.warning("can't import pykwalify; won't validate YAML (%s)", e) return lambda _data: None ``` Also in `scripts/pylib/twister/scl.py`, we would change `yaml_load_verify` to this: ```python def yaml_load_verify(filename: str, validate: Callable[[dict[str, Any]], None]): """ Safely load a testcase/sample yaml document and validate it against the YAML schema, returning in case of success the YAML data. :param str filename: name of the file to load and process :param dict schema: loaded YAML schema (can load with :func:`yaml_load`) # 'document.yaml' contains a single YAML document. :raises yaml.scanner.ScannerError: on YAML parsing error :raises pykwalify.errors.SchemaError: on Schema violation error """ # 'document.yaml' contains a single YAML document. y = yaml_load(filename) if not y: raise EmptyYamlFileException('No data in YAML file: %s' % filename) validate(y) return y ``` In `scripts/pylib/twister/twisterlib/config_parser.py` we need to change the `__init__ `method in class `TwisterConfigParser`: ### Change in workflow Before (Current main branch) we always load the YAML validator schema once. But the YAML validator is then reconstructed in `yaml_load_verify` again and again in every loop iteration. #### Before ```python # This code runs once schema = scl.yaml_load(os.path.join(ZEPHYR_BASE, "scripts", "schemas", "twister", "schema.yaml")) # A loop is entered, which executed code multiple times for (): # yaml_load_verify also reconstruct the YAML validator data = scl.yaml_load_verify(file, schema) # do stuff with data ``` #### After ```python # This code runs once schema_path = os.path.join(ZEPHYR_BASE, "scripts", "schemas", "twister", "schema.yaml") schema_validator = scl.make_yaml_validator(schema_path) # A loop is entered, which executed code multiple times for (): data = scl.yaml_load_verify(file, schema_validator) # do stuff with data ``` ### Profiling I am currently using this method to run profiling: `viztracer --min_duration 0.1ms -o twister.trace.json -- west twister -j 1 -s utilities.lists` ### Dependencies No change in dependencies. ### Concerns and Unresolved Questions Do I need to follow deprecation procedure for any of these changes? ### Alternatives Considered _No response_

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#101748.body:
### Describe the bug In tcp_in(), there is an assertion that assumes whenever there is pending send data (conn->send_data_total > 0), the send_data_timer must be pending: ``` NET_ASSERT((conn->send_data_total == 0) || k_work_delayable_is_pending(&conn->send_data_timer), "conn: %p, Missing a subscription " "of the send_data queue timer", conn); ``` This assumption is not valid when the peer advertises a zero send window. ### Regression - [ ] This is a regression. ### Steps to reproduce A valid TCP sequence can trigger this assertion: 1. `send_data_timer` expires and invokes `tcp_resend_data()`. 2. `tcp_resend_data()` calls `tcp_send_data()`. 3. `tcp_unsent_len()` returns `0` because `conn->send_win == 0`, even though `conn->send_data_total > 0`. 4. No data is transmitted and `send_data_timer` is not rescheduled. 5. When a packet is later received and processed by `tcp_in()`, the assertion fails because: - `conn->send_data_total > 0` - `send_data_timer` is not pending The assertion incorrectly treats this condition as an internal error.

Workaround this issue

Treat a zero send window as a valid exception to the assertion, for example: ``` NET_ASSERT((conn->send_data_total == 0) || (conn->send_win == 0) || k_work_delayable_is_pending(&conn->send_data_timer), "conn: %p, Missing a subscription " "of the send_data queue timer", conn); ``` ### Relevant log output ```shell ``` ### Impact Intermittent – Occurs occasionally; hard to reproduce. ### Environment _No response_ ### Additional Context _No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#101748.title: net: tcp: Incorrect assertion in tcp_in() when peer advertises zero send window

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#101761.title: ESP32 Wi-Fi networking not working on a specific phone

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#101936.body:
### Describe the bug

- imx93_evk/mimx9352/m33

commit e7d24c80884fc4fda99c378b7b57551196de73e5
Author: Zhaoxiang Jin <Zhaoxiang.Jin_1@nxp.com>
Date:   Fri Dec 26 13:28:16 2025 +0800

    drivers: adc: add clock frequency selection for SAR ADC
    
    This commit enhances the SAR ADC driver by adding
    support for clock frequency selection. The clock
    frequency can now be configured via the devicetree,
    allowing for better optimization of ADC performance
    based on application requirements.
    
    Signed-off-by: Zhaoxiang Jin <Zhaoxiang.Jin_1@nxp.com>

 drivers/adc/adc_nxp_sar_adc.c     | 11 ++++++++---
 dts/bindings/adc/nxp,sar-adc.yaml | 12 ++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 20 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

### Regression

- [x] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

west build -b imx93_evk/mimx9352/m33 tests/drivers/adc/adc_api -T drivers.adc.b_u585i_iot02a_adc4

### Relevant log output

```shell
/home/jenkins/agent/workspace/bitbucket_build_platform/zephyr/drivers/adc/adc_nxp_sar_adc.c:468:23: error: 'ADC_MCR_ADCLKSEL_MASK' undeclared (first use in this function); did you mean 'ADC_MCR_ADCLKSE_MASK'?
  468 |                       ADC_MCR_ADCLKSEL_MASK)) | ADC_MCR_OWREN(config->overwrite ? 1U : 0U) |
      |                       ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |                       ADC_MCR_ADCLKSE_MASK
/home/jenkins/agent/workspace/bitbucket_build_platform/zephyr/drivers/adc/adc_nxp_sar_adc.c:468:23: note: each undeclared identifier is reported only once for each function it appears in
/home/jenkins/agent/workspace/bitbucket_build_platform/zephyr/drivers/adc/adc_nxp_sar_adc.c:470:23: error: implicit declaration of function 'ADC_MCR_ADCLKSEL'; did you mean 'ADC_MCR_ADCLKSE'? [-Werror=implicit-function-declaration]
  470 |                       ADC_MCR_ADCLKSEL(config->conv_clk_freq_div_factor));
      |                       ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |                       ADC_MCR_ADCLKSE
cc1: all warnings being treated as errors
```

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.

### Environment

- OS: ubuntu
- toolchain: zephyr
- version: v4.3.0-3362-g1428bc537a39

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102001.body:
### Describe the bug

Race condition in uart_mcux_lpuart between ISR and Work Queue causes RX length underflow

I have identified a concurrency issue in the uart_mcux_lpuart driver that leads to a system crash due to an integer underflow.

Symptoms: The system crashes after proccossing the underflowed len value (which yield to FFFF FFBC size len is size_t which is unsigned)
```
[00:12:11.098,000] <dbg> uart_mcux_lpuart: RX Ready: (len: -68 off: 68 buf: 20200140)
```
Root Cause Analysis: The issue stems from a race condition between the System Work Queue (sysworkq) and the DMA Interrupt Service Routine (ISR).
- Context A: sysworkq executes mcux_lpuart_async_rx_timeout.
- Context B: The ISR preempts the work queue to execute dma_callback.

Both contexts access and modify data->async.rx_dma_params without any synchronization or locking mechanism. Specifically, the ISR modifies the DMA parameters while the timeout function is in the middle of calculating the RX length:
```c
.data.rx.len = dma_params->counter - dma_params->offset
```

If the ISR updates counter or offset mid-calculation, this subtraction results in an underflow. Access to rx_dma_params should be protected by a critical section or lock.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Take an UART async application that utilizing DMA, let it run for 10-60 minutes eventually len would underflow which could escalate to to a busfault.

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Showstopper – Prevents release or major functionality; system unusable.

### Environment

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102001.title: uart_mcux_lpuart: DMA race condition with sysworkq and isr

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102005.body:
## Summary

This is the roadmap to prepare the ground for bumping Mbed TLS version from the current 3.6 revision to 4.0. See corresponding [RFC](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/97660).

### 1. Remove remaining usage of legacy crypto

In the following I will list all the dependencies on legacy Mbed TLS crypto that need to be transitioned to PSA Crypto API in order to allow this bump to happen smoothly. The list only includes dependencies in Zephyr repo, modules are not checked.

I ran the following command on the `main` branch at 9334cedb3de831ab654bce4ae8a47dbfc95d50d8 in order to find all legacy dependencies:

`rg -g '!modules/mbedtls/*' -g '!doc/releases/*' -ie 'mbedtls_(ecp|rsa|cipher|ecdh|ecdsa|aes|gcm|ccm|mpi|des|dhm|sha|entropy|ctr_drbg|hmac_drbg|hmac|hkdf|cmac)_' | grep -v _ROM_TABLES | cut -d: -f1 | sort -u`

and I got:

- [x] drivers/bluetooth/hci/Kconfig.esp32
- [x] drivers/wifi/esp32/Kconfig.esp32
- [x] modules/hostap/Kconfig [1]
- [x] modules/openthread/Kconfig https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/98088
- [x] samples/net/lwm2m_client/overlay-dtls-cert.conf https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/101419
- [x] samples/net/wifi/shell/nxp/* https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/102716
- [x] subsys/net/lib/http/http_server_ws.c https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/102037
- [x] subsys/net/lib/sockets/Kconfig https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/101419
- [x] subsys/net/lib/websocket/* https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/102037
- [x] subsys/random/* https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/100048
- [x] subsys/storage/flash_map/* https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/102022
- [x] tests/benchmarks/mbedtls/prj.conf https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/102042
- [x] tests/net/socket/tls_configurations/prj.conf https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/101419

### 2. Following steps

- [x] Remove Kconfigs related to legacy crypto features
- [x] Start integrating Mbed TLS 4.0 https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/104031

## Describe the solution you'd like

Ideally all the above usages of legacy Mbed TLS crypto should be transitioned to PSA Crypto API.

[1] For the specific case of HostAP we know it relies on some legacy modules (ex: bignum) to implement some algorithm that's not yet supported by PSA Crypto API (ex: WPA3). This lack of support from PSA API means that some sort of support for legacy crypto mechanisms need to stay.
Ideally one could claim that the approach might apply to all other legacy dependencies mentioned above. However it should be noted that all legacy crypto modules are now internal to Mbed TLS so they are not granted to have stable interfaces over minor releases. HostAP is an exception and once the PSA Crypto API will implement all the algorithms that HostAP requires, HostAP will need to drop usage of legacy Mbed TLS APIs and move to PSA API.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102005.title: Roadmap for bumping Mbed TLS 3.6 to Mbed TLS 4.0/TF-PSA-Crypto 1.0

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102057.body:
### Describe the bug

- mimxrt700_evk/mimxrt798s/cm33_cpu1/mimxrt700_evk/mimxrt798s/cm33_cpu0
- mimxrt1180_evk/mimxrt1189/cm7

commit a206401647752c576e5ca9844995f38695f2f11e
Author: Ben Levinsky <ben.levinsky@amd.com>
Date:   Tue Jan 6 06:40:49 2026 -0800

    drivers: ipm: ipm_mbox: handle mailbox notifications without payload
    
    Some mailbox backends may deliver notifications without an associated
    data payload. This can occur when the mailbox is used as a signal-only
    mechanism, where struct mbox_msg is NULL, data is NULL, or size is zero.
    
    The IPM mailbox callback currently assumes a valid data payload and
    unconditionally dereferences data->data, which can lead to a NULL
    pointer dereference for such mailbox implementations.
    
    Update the IPM mailbox callback to tolerate empty mailbox messages and
    invoke the IPM callback with a NULL payload pointer when no data is
    present. This preserves notification semantics while preventing
    runtime faults.
    
    Signed-off-by: Ben Levinsky <ben.levinsky@amd.com>

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102099.body:
### Describe the bug

Probe-rs runner always selects elf_file, ignoring hex_file and bin_file from RunnerConfig. This breaks MCUBoot support since the bootloader requires hex/bin format and the build system sets hex_file to signed images (zephyr.signed.hex).

It was supposed to be implemented in https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/commit/3c7d9732e8f5ebe25b9de201cc526b5f73cf4f34 / #93670 but it never worked. (cc: @jaredwolff )


### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Steps to reproduce:
1. Build any app with MCUBoot
2. Use signing key for app
3. Flash MCUBoot and app
4. App will fail to boot due to not being signed

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102129.body:
### Describe the bug

The MAX32657 NS target doesn't support any access to the flash peripheral, but the `max32657evkit/max32657/ns` exposes a `storage_partition` in error, resulting in various tests, etc. attempting to access the storage partition for flash operations.

To fix, we need to not have the flash controller enabled for that target, nor expose a `storage_partition` node to be safe.

This was fixed in `main` but needs to be back ported to v4.3 as well. See #99842

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102133.body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Hello,

I would like to do math with timeouts, which seems as easy as operating with the underlying ticks, but since `k_timeout_t` is an opaque pointer, accessing this field is not supported. For example, how do we implement exponential backoff when we have a time value in `k_timeout_t`?

I have checked issue https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/49858 but I have to admit I did not understand the reasons why doing math using ticks as suggested would not work.

I would appreciate if anyone could help me understanding why that feature request failed and what would be the right way to do these operations or good alternatives. Thanks!

### Describe the solution you'd like

My expectations when I started looking into the API was to see at least one of these options supported:

1) Having a macro for getting the underlying ticks value that defines a `k_timeout_t` (or equivalent value if it ever changes to something other than ticks).

2) Having macros for arithmetic operations on the ticks value such as addition and subtraction of two `k_timeout_t` values, multiplication and division of a `k_timeout_t` value by a number.

I'd love to help out if this was possible.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I see `k_timepoint_t` with `sys_timepoint_calc()` and `sys_timepoint_timeout()` cover the specific case of adjusting a timeout based on time passed, but it has limitations and it feels like it could be more straightforward and readable.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102133.title: Arithmetic operations on k_timeout_t or safe access to ticks value

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102135.body: ### Describe the bug On the Silicon Labs SiWx917 DK2605A development kit, the user LEDs are wired active-low per the board schematic, but the Zephyr devicetree configuration defines them as GPIO_ACTIVE_HIGH. As a result, Zephyr sample applications such as blinky appear to behave incorrectly: the LED either does not turn on as expected or only flashes briefly, even though the application logic is correct and running. After debugging, the issue appears to be a mismatch between: • the hardware schematic • the SoC GPIO port mapping • and the devicetree LED polarity configuration ### Regression - [ ] This is a regression. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Set up a Zephyr workspace with Silicon Labs support enabled. 2. Build and flash the blinky sample for board siwx917_dk2605a: west build -b siwx917_dk2605a samples/basic/blinky west flash 3. Observe LED behavior on the board. 4. Compare against the board schematic and devicetree definitions in: zephyr/boards/silabs/dev_kits/siwx917_dk2605a/siwx917_dk2605a.dts Expected behavior The LED should turn on solid when driven logically “on” by the GPIO API, matching the behavior described in the blinky sample and documentation. Actual behavior The LED appears off or only briefly flashes, even though: • the application is running • GPIO configuration calls succeed • breakpoints confirm execution flow is correct Manually inverting the GPIO polarity in software causes the LED to behave correctly. ### Relevant log output ```shell ``` ### Impact Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality. ### Environment • OS: macOS (Apple Silicon) • Zephyr version: v4.3.0 (HEAD at time of report) • Toolchain: Zephyr SDK 0.17.4 • Board: SiWx917 DK2605A • Debugger: On-board J-Link OB • Runner: silabs_commander ### Additional Context • The board schematic shows LEDs wired active-low. • The GPIO port mapping in the SiWx917 datasheet confirms the pin assignments. • The devicetree currently defines: `gpios = <&gpioa 15 GPIO_ACTIVE_HIGH>;` which does not reflect the hardware behavior. • This was confirmed by stepping through the code in GDB and cross-referencing the schematic and datasheet. • A polarity correction (GPIO_ACTIVE_LOW) resolves the issue locally. If maintainers agree, I’m happy to submit a pull request correcting the devicetree LED definitions.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102135.title: siwx917_dk2605a: LED polarity in gpio-leds DTS appears inconsistent with board schematic

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102142.body:
### Describe the bug

After setting `CONFIG_SHELL_BACKEND_TELNET=y` inside prj.conf, the previous working network related procedure will be stuck at waiting for the interface to come up. The related logs are shown below:

When `CONFIG_SHELL_BACKEND_TELNET=y` is setting, `net_config_init_by_iface` will be blocked at [init.c line 434](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/v4.2-branch/subsys/net/lib/config/init.c#L434) because no NET_EVENT_IF_UP is caught and therefore waiter is never released([init.c L358](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/v4.2-branch/subsys/net/lib/config/init.c#L358)).  This really blocked my project because we want to have the telnet feature.

My prj.conf is here:
```
# Disable multithreading
CONFIG_MULTITHREADING=y

# No timer support in the kernel
CONFIG_SYS_CLOCK_EXISTS=y

# Optimize drivers and peripherals
CONFIG_PINCTRL=y
CONFIG_GPIO=y

# Optimize boot
CONFIG_BOOT_BANNER=y
CONFIG_BOOT_DELAY=0

# Optimize console
CONFIG_CONSOLE=y
CONFIG_UART_CONSOLE=y
CONFIG_PRINTK=y

CONFIG_THREAD_MONITOR=y
CONFIG_INIT_STACKS=y
CONFIG_THREAD_NAME=y

# Configure logging
CONFIG_LOG=y
CONFIG_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE=4096
CONFIG_LOG_DEFAULT_LEVEL=3
CONFIG_LOG_BACKEND_SHOW_COLOR=n  # not all terminals support colors

# SPI support
CONFIG_SPI=y
CONFIG_SPI_NXP_LPSPI_DMA=n

# Serial connection
CONFIG_SERIAL=y
CONFIG_UART_INTERRUPT_DRIVEN=y

CONFIG_POSIX_API=y

# # Networking config
CONFIG_NETWORKING=y
CONFIG_NET_ARP=y
CONFIG_NET_L2_ETHERNET=y
CONFIG_NET_L2_ETHERNET_MGMT=y
CONFIG_NET_IPV4=y
CONFIG_NET_IPV6=n
CONFIG_NET_CONFIG_AUTO_INIT=n
CONFIG_NET_TCP=y
CONFIG_NET_UDP=y

# Network driver config
CONFIG_TEST_RANDOM_GENERATOR=y

# Network address config
CONFIG_NET_CONFIG_SETTINGS=y

# Network socket support
CONFIG_NET_SOCKETS_PACKET=y

# Activate shell module that provides network commands like ping to the console.
# Note: This is must, otherwise you can't ping and TCP connects to the device
CONFIG_NET_SHELL=y

# Other configs needed
CONFIG_NET_IF_UNICAST_IPV4_ADDR_COUNT=1
CONFIG_NET_MAX_CONTEXTS=4

CONFIG_NET_LOG=y

CONFIG_SHELL_BACKEND_TELNET=y
```

BTW, I pass 30 seconds to the parameter timeout of `net_config_init_by_iface` but the process have been blocked for more than one hour(maybe just block forever). It's this also a bug?

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Setting `CONFIG_SHELL_BACKEND_TELNET=y` in prj.conf
2. west build -p -b mimxrt1180_evk/mimxrt1189/cm33 {your_app} && west flash -r jlink
3. hardcode to find a ethernet device(e.g. switch_port0) and its corresponding struct net_if *iface
4. call net_config_init_by_iface

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102157.body:
### Describe the bug

Commit https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/commit/a713f52fe4f3292894e0d58083b9373ede46cc6b introduced a regression on C33 USB device (HS controller).

Related `hal_renesas` commit: https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/hal_renesas/commit/0164f2f515ad196674103f33cab10a7d547ce3cf

The enumeration succeeedes after 3 retries, but control packets (like USBD_MSG_CDC_ACM_LINE_CODING) are out-of-sync (the callback has data from previous transaction)

Reverting the commit fixes the issue.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

```west build  --board arduino_portenta_c33  samples/subsys/usb/cdc_acm/```

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102239.body:
### Describe the bug

In intc_gicv3.c there is:
```
#ifdef CONFIG_GIC_SINGLE_SECURITY_STATE
	/*
	 * Before configuration, we need to check whether
	 * the GIC single security state mode is supported.
	 * Make sure GICD_CTRL_NS is 1.
	 */
	sys_set_bit(GICD_CTLR, GICD_CTRL_NS);
	__ASSERT(sys_test_bit(GICD_CTLR, GICD_CTRL_NS),
		 "Current GIC does not support single security state");
#endif 
```
However I suspect GICD_CTRL_NS cannot be set here when also ARMV8_A_NS is configured.
Pehaps move sys_set_bit(GICD_CTLR, GICD_CTRL_NS) to EL3 and just keep the assert?

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102242.body:
### Problem Description

The UART transport for MCUmgr (note: not shell) depends on some Kconfig options, however it also selects a Kconfig option - which is a driver, as per https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/build/kconfig/tips.html#select-pitfalls select has issues and a subsystem should certainly not be selecting a driver. It also presents problems to have this as `select` for another PR which introduces an alternate transport for UART which if unchanged, would throw into a Kconfig dependency loop error and projects would be unconfigurable.

### Proposed Change (Summary)

The proposed change is to change the following:
```
menuconfig MCUMGR_TRANSPORT_UART
        bool "UART mcumgr SMP transport"
        depends on CONSOLE
        depends on BASE64
        depends on CRC
        select UART_MCUMGR
```
to the following:
```
menuconfig MCUMGR_TRANSPORT_UART
        bool "UART mcumgr SMP transport"
        depends on CONSOLE
        depends on BASE64
        depends on CRC
        depends on UART_MCUMGR
```
Users that use this transport would then need to add the following to their `prj.conf` (or other) file:
```
CONFIG_UART_MCUMGR=y
```

### Proposed Change (Detailed)

As above, with the Kconfig added to the `prj.conf` file, the application will function exactly as it did before

### Dependencies

_No response_

### Concerns and Unresolved Questions

_No response_

### Alternatives Considered

None, not making this change makes it impossible to add an alternate transport as it would mean invalid and incompatible selections could be made, which likely would compile but would lead to non-functional applications
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102242.title: RFC: Breaking API Change: mgmt: mcumgr: transport: uart: Change Kconfig to depends on

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102352.body:
### Describe the bug

In [this pull](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/100128) I am attempting to add a SAMD21E18A board with RTC like so:
```dts
&rtc {
	status = "okay";
	counter-mode = "clock";
	prescaler = <1024>;
};
```
 When running the CI tests, I am getting an error in this function:
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/32823f39fbf2663fa1bbb75e60e5743cd6d91579/soc/atmel/sam0/common/soc_samd2x.c#L235-L242
```
 /__w/zephyr/zephyr/soc/atmel/sam0/common/soc_samd2x.c: In function 'gclk_rtc_configure':
/__w/zephyr/zephyr/soc/atmel/sam0/common/soc_samd2x.c:240:51: error: 'CONFIG_SOC_ATMEL_SAMD_XOSC32K_PRESCALER' undeclared (first use in this function); did you mean 'CONFIG_SOC_ATMEL_SAMD_OSC8M_AS_MAIN'?
  240 |         gclk_connect(4, GCLK_GENCTRL_SRC_XOSC32K, CONFIG_SOC_ATMEL_SAMD_XOSC32K_PRESCALER, 0);
      |                                                   ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |                                                   CONFIG_SOC_ATMEL_SAMD_OSC8M_AS_MAIN
```
The above reference to `CONFIG_SOC_ATMEL_SAMD_XOSC32K_PRESCALER` is the only one I can find in this repo or hal_atmel.

Note this should just be using the `GCLK` by default to run the RTC as defined here (I don't think I am missing any required DTS parts):
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/32823f39fbf2663fa1bbb75e60e5743cd6d91579/dts/arm/atmel/samd21.dtsi#L101-L106

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

### Environment

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

### Describe the bug

after below commit
commit 1b7810f0cb74bae33f074573c1b7b6f3d3d2cbf3 (HEAD -> b1b7810f0cb74bae33f074573c1b7b6f3d3d2cbf3, refs/bisect/bad)
Author: Grzegorz Chwierut <grzegorz.chwierut@nordicsemi.no>
Date:   Mon Nov 24 17:13:25 2025 +0100

    scripts: twister: Support multiple ports in pytest-twister-harness

    Add support for multiple connections (e.g. UART) from one device,
    enabling communication with second core UARTs. Feature implemented
    by extracting connection logic from DeviceAdapter to new
    DeviceConnection class with specialized implementations:
    - SerialConnection for hardware UART ports
    - ProcessConnection for native simulation
    - FifoConnection for QEMU communication
    Each connection maintains separate log files (handler.log,
    handler_1.log, etc.) and can be accessed via connection_index
    parameter in device methods like readline() and write().
    This enables testing multi-core applications where different
    cores communicate through separate UART interfaces.

    Signed-off-by: Grzegorz Chwierut <grzegorz.chwierut@nordicsemi.no>

the post-scripts run sequence is wrong.

### Regression

- [x] This is a regression.

add any post_script in map file.

Modify the as below

@@ -178,6 +178,7 @@ class HardwareAdapter(DeviceAdapter):

     def _close_device(self) -> None:
         if self.device_config.post_script:
+            asdfsfsdf

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102390.body:
### Describe the bug

When using LWM2M with senml/json, opaque values are send base64 encoded. Not all send values are accepted by the leshan-server. The leshan-server seems to expect base64 urlsafe-encoded with padding omitted. 
This seems to be the intended way, as described here:
https://github.com/OpenMobileAlliance/OMA_LwM2M_for_Developers/issues/553
Currently zephyr sends the opaque values in senml/json as base64 encoded (not urlsafe) with padding.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

- Add an resource with opaque value to the lwm2m client sample
	- For this test, I added a binary data-object to the sample. (shown here: https://github.com/jbr-ia/zephyr/commit/907c5fb92afcaa3af2531ddf3e4872cfd521ca49)
- Compile and run ```west build -b qemu_cortex_a53 -- -DOVERLAY_CONFIG="overlay-data-cache.conf;overlay-dtls.conf;overlay-lwm2m-1.1.conf;overlay-lwm2m-1.1-core-interop.conf;overlay-queue.conf;overlay-tickless.conf"```
- run the sample ```west build -t run```
- On the leshan-server, set single and multi-value to senml_json 
- On the leshan server, Enable observe for the binary data object data, resource 19/0/0
- in the zephyr-terminal, write a value to the resource
  - ```lwm2m write /19/0/0 -s "123"``` and see the value changes in leshan
  - ```lwm2m write /19/0/0 -s "1"``` and see the value does not change in leshan (because there is padding)
  - ```lwm2m write /19/0/0 -s "???"``` and see the value does not change in leshan (because there is a '/' in the base64 encoded string which should not be in the base64 urlsafe variant)
  - ```lwm2m write /19/0/0 -s "~~~"```and see the value does not change in leshan (because there is a '+' in the base64 encoded string which should not be in the base64 urlsafe variant)
  - ```lwm2m write /19/0/0 -s "123456"``` and see communication still works (because with this string there is no difference between base64 and base64 url-safe encoding)


If I change the encoded value to urlsafe without padding before sending (as done here: https://github.com/jbr-ia/zephyr/commit/28e71cc2260ce7a1c1cdea62dc0ec517e5e06280) , than all values seem to be received correctly on leshan.


### Relevant log output

```shell
the leshan-log shows output like:

Caused by: org.eclipse.leshan.core.util.json.JsonException: Node vd with value 'Pz8/' is not in valid Base64 format.
	at .....
```

### Impact

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

### Environment

OS: Ubuntu 24.04 (in WSL)
Version used: main (commit 7e3c5ab2bb2a9f4bf1b0afd43c872e6f19461777) with some changes as described in 'steps to reproduce'

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102390.title: Some opaque values are not accepted by leshan when using LWM2M with senml json

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102393.body:
### Describe the bug

In Zephyr 4.2 in [gen_prov_start](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/64e98eb5278d790f3b3e5279ca8b39b45097bbc0/subsys/bluetooth/mesh/pb_adv.c#L581C13-L581C27) function, there is missing length check of provisioning PDU. Due to this, it can happen that mesh stack can write outside of the buffer.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Major – Severely degrades functionality; workaround is difficult or unavailable.

### Environment

Zephyr 4.2.0, 4.2.1

### Additional Context

_No response_

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102504.body:
### Describe the bug

Currently we have 3 modules which add their directories unconditionally to the zephyr/global include path.
This creates noise, slows down builds, and in general creates unnecessary side-effects.

* [x] modules/hal/microchip
  * https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/hal_microchip/blob/master/CMakeLists.txt#L7
  * https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/102770
* [x] modules/hal/st
  * https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/hal_st/blob/master/CMakeLists.txt#L45 
  * https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/103483
* [x] modules/hal/ti
  * https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/hal_ti/blob/master/mspm0/CMakeLists.txt#L1
  * https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/hal_ti/blob/master/mspm0/source/ti/devices/msp/CMakeLists.txt
  * https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/hal_ti/pull/81

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

1. mkdir build && cd build
2. cmake -GNinja -DBOARD=qemu_x86 ../samples/hello_world/
3. grep INCLUDES build.ninja | head -n 1 | sed 's/-I/\n/g'
4. see result

### Relevant log output

```shell
INCLUDES = 
/scratch/zephyrproject/zephyr/build/zephyr/include/generated/zephyr 
/scratch/zephyrproject/zephyr/include 
/scratch/zephyrproject/zephyr/build/zephyr/include/generated 
/scratch/zephyrproject/zephyr/soc/intel/atom 
/scratch/zephyrproject/zephyr/lib/libc/picolibc/include 
/scratch/zephyrproject/zephyr/lib/posix/c_lib_ext/getopt 
/scratch/zephyrproject/zephyr/soc/intel/atom/. 
/scratch/zephyrproject/modules/hal/microchip/include

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102544.body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

**Describe the request:**
When running hardware tests with Twister, there is currently no way to perform 
a hardware reset of the DUT during test execution without 
reflashing. This limitation prevents testing important scenarios such as:

1. **BLE bonding persistence** - Verifying that bonded device information 
   survives a reset and connections can be re-established
2. **Non-volatile storage recovery** - Testing that settings/data persist 
   across power cycles
3. **Proper initialization sequences** - Ensuring the device initializes 
   correctly after reset
4. **Test isolation** - Resetting device state between test cases without 
   full reflashing

Currently, the J-Link runner (scripts/west_commands/runners/jlink.py) only 
supports reset as part of the flash operation, not as a standalone action.


**Platform**: All platforms using J-Link debugger

### Describe the solution you'd like

Extend the J-Link runner to support a standalone reset command that can be:

1. Called independently: `west reset --runner jlink`
2. Integrated into pytest test cases via Twister fixtures
3. Used programmatically in hardware test scripts

Example usage in pytest:

```python
def test_ble_bonding_persistence(dut, reset_device):
    # Pair and bond with device
    dut.pair_and_bond()
    assert dut.is_bonded()
    
    # Reset the device
    reset_device()
    
    # Verify bonding persists
    assert dut.is_bonded()
    assert dut.can_reconnect()


### Describe alternatives you've considered

Current workarounds:

Power cycling manually - Not automated, unreliable for CI/CD
Reflashing - Slow, erases all persistent storage, not testing reset behavior
External hardware - Requires additional equipment like USB switches
Software reset via shell - Requires shell to be enabled, may not test hardware reset paths
None of these alternatives properly test hardware reset behavior in an automated way.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102544.title: Add J-Link hardware reset support for Twister hardware testing

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102556.body:
### Describe the bug

Summary

Under delete-only or delete-heavy workloads, the NVS allocation table entry (ATE) write pointer may underflow across a sector boundary, causing ATEs to be written into the previous sector.

⸻

Description

In NVS, data entries grow forward within a sector, while allocation table entries (ATEs) grow backward from the end of the sector.
Under normal workloads, data and ATEs coexist and naturally limit each other.

However, in delete-only or delete-heavy scenarios, a sector may contain only delete ATEs and no data entries. In this case, the ATE write pointer (ate_wra) continues to decrement toward the sector start.

Without an explicit boundary check, ATE writes may occur when the write pointer reaches offset 0 of the current sector. After such a write, the pointer is decremented further, allowing subsequent ATE writes to underflow into the previous sector.

This may corrupt unrelated data or metadata belonging to adjacent sectors and lead to inconsistent NVS state after reboot.

⸻

Steps to reproduce
	1.	Initialize an NVS instance with a fixed number of sectors.
	2.	Repeatedly write and delete the same key so that only delete ATEs are generated.
	3.	Ensure that no data entries are written into the active sector.
	4.	Continue delete operations until the active sector is nearly filled by ATEs.
	5.	Observe that the ATE write pointer reaches the sector boundary and may cross into the previous sector.

⸻

Expected behavior

ATE writes must remain confined to the current sector.
When the ATE write pointer reaches the sector boundary, further ATE writes should be prevented to avoid crossing into adjacent sectors.

⸻

Actual behavior

ATE writes are allowed at offset 0 of the current sector.
After such a write, the ATE write pointer may underflow into the previous sector, resulting in out-of-bounds writes.

⸻

Root cause analysis

The current logic checks only for overlap between data and ATE regions, but does not explicitly prevent ATE writes at the sector boundary.
As a result, backward-growing ATEs are not constrained to remain strictly within the current sector under delete-only workloads.

⸻

Proposed fix

Disallow ATE writes when the write pointer is at offset 0 of the current sector.
This ensures that ATE writes remain within the sector boundary and prevents pointer underflow into adjacent sectors, without altering normal NVS semantics.

A minimal boundary check based on the sector offset is sufficient to address this issue.

⸻


### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.

### Environment

_No response_

### Additional Context

Tagged affects active releases 3.7, 4.2, 4.3 and 4.4.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102556.title: NVS: ATE write pointer may underflow across sector boundary in delete-only scenarios

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102586.body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

It's currently not possible to request user input when running a shell command. For example to ask confirmation before a destructive command:

```shell
uart:~$ otp program ocotp@401f4000 0x98 0 1 2 3
Programming 4 bytes onto OTP, offset 0x98:
00000000: 00 01 02 03                                      |....             |
Proceed? [y/N]:
```

### Describe the solution you'd like

I would like to see a function with the following definition or similar:

```c
/**
 * @brief Read a line of input from the shell.
 *
 * This function reads from the shell transport until a newline character is
 * received, storing the data in the provided buffer. The newline character is
 * not included in the buffer. The buffer is null-terminated on success.
 *
 * @param[in]  sh      Shell instance.
 * @param[out] buf     Buffer to store the input line.
 * @param[in]  len     Maximum buffer size (including null terminator).
 * @param[in]  timeout Maximum time to wait for a complete line.
 *
 * @return Number of bytes read (excluding null terminator) on success.
 * @retval -ETIMEDOUT If timeout occurred before newline was received.
 * @retval -ENOBUFS If input exceeds buffer size.
 * @retval -ECANCELED If CTRL+C was pressed.
 */
int shell_readline(const struct shell *sh, uint8_t *buf, size_t len, k_timeout_t timeout);
```

That can be called from inside shell command handler, and captures the input from the user.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102586.title: shell: Interactive mode with user input

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102635.body:
### Describe the bug

When configuring an interrupt for the MCUX GPIO driver, the `base->ICR1` or `base->ICR2` register is written without an IRQ lock active. This can result in the interrupt starting to fire infinitely.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create an application that re-configures a GPIO interrupt (for example an application after MCUBOOT)
2. Reboot until hangs
3. Attaching a debugger shows that the interrupt is always set.

### Relevant log output

```shell
─── Stack ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[1] from 0x700b39ea in gpio_fire_callbacks+20 at /home/pdgendt/zephyrproject/zephyr/include/zephyr/drivers/gpio/gpio_utils.h:118

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102678.body:
### Describe the bug

When exiting from a low power mode if a recoverable fault happens (not hard-fault) during the wakeup sequence, the function **mcux_flexcomm_poll_out()** tries to send information about the reset reason using the uart. 
The main problem is that the **mcux_flexcomm_poll_out()** is trying to read some status registers from the uart but the clocks haven't been reconfigured **causing an infinite wait** while reading the register in this line:

while (!(USART_GetStatusFlags(config->base) & kUSART_TxFifoNotFullFlag)) {}

while (!(USART_GetStatusFlags(config->base) & kUSART_TxFifoEmptyFlag)) {}

A time-out should be added to this conditions to interrupt the infinite wait or check if the clocks are configured previous to check for the uart registers

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

### Environment

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102707.body:
### Summary

In C literal character values like `'a'`, `\0`, `'5'`, etc. are considered `int`s. This means that `sizeof('a') == sizeof(int)` (typically 4), which may not be known by all, and is an easy mistake to make if calculating the size of a buffer. 

E.g. if I have 2 strings that I want to concatenate with a `/` between them, I may define the resulting buffer as `strlen(str_a) + sizeof('/') + strlen(str_b) + sizeof('\0')`, but unfortunately the will make the buffer 2 x 3 (4 - 1) => 6 bytes larger than expected. 

However doing `strlen(str_a) + sizeof((char)'/') + strlen(str_b) + sizeof((char)'\0')` works perfectly fine. Thus `sizeof` should probably never be used with a literal character without a cast. 

### Describe the solution you'd like

Not sure where in our CI this fits, but it should be possible to reject anything that has `sizeof('`, and instead suggest `sizeof((char)'`

### Alternatives

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102707.title: CI: Add check for and reject uses of `sizeof` literal `char`s without cast

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102711.body:
### Summary

We have using the zephyr in our project and we are also sync'ing the zephyr versions periodically.
We are observing difference of behavior in the ethernet cable and disconnect scenario for different release versions:

**Test Scenario** : Establish Modbus communication and then remove Ethernet cable and then reconnect. Stop Modbus TCP client communication.
 
1. In the 3.6 version when ethernet cable is disconnected and reconnected the Modbus communication was restarting without needing any reinitialization of network apis. ( No socket close happening. IP address retained). It also works when client is stopped and restarted.

3. In zephyr 3.7.1, for above test scenario, after cable reconnect, communication establishes but when client is stopped, ip address gets lost. Communication stops. Event received NET_EVENT_IPV4_ADDR_DEL.

We observed this particular PR has caused the change of the functionality 
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/commit/f96087e257b81e11225c429e3b87e2e56bc2bc26#diff-9ac8d59b0b68aecce1e4b98cae67d25e5f56a2a53c14316d30850f68f4e6a2f7

 

4. In zephyr 4.3, after cable disconnect, all sockets are closed and IP address is lost. We have to reinitialize all of our network application.

**Reinitializing all the applications is complex activity for us.**

So with is ticket we are suggesting to retain the functionality and behavior which was available in the 3.6.
If you want to keep the new one can you also keep the behavior configurable so that there won't be any impact on product functionality.

Let us know if you need more information.

Test environment
Board : NucleoSTM32H743ZI / 553ZI

### Describe the solution you'd like

So with is ticket we are suggesting to retain the functionality and behavior which was available in the 3.6.
If you want to keep the new one can you also keep the behavior configurable so that there won't be any impact on product functionality.

### Alternatives

 you keep both functionalities configurable

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102711.title: Retain network information even when ethernet cable is disconnected and reconnected

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102718.body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

When using mDNS together with DHCP on Zephyr, the mDNS responder may start before DHCP completes, causing the hostname (e.g. zephyr.local) to be announced using a preconfigured static IP (CONFIG_NET_CONFIG_MY_IPV4_ADDR). Because the hostname is cached by clients due to the configured TTL, this leads to persistent incorrect name resolution.

Motivation / Use Case

I am developing an HTTP server / WebSocket application where users connect to the device via a browser using:

http://zephyr.local


The expected startup sequence is:

1. Device boots
2. DHCP acquires an IPv4 address
3. mDNS announces zephyr.local with the DHCP-assigned IP
4. Browser connects reliably

Currently, step 3 may occur before step 2, which breaks discovery and first-boot UX.

Problem Description

Observed behavior:

mDNS sometimes starts before DHCP is in BOUND state
mDNS announces the hostname using the static fallback:

CONFIG_NET_CONFIG_MY_IPV4_ADDR

Once DHCP later assigns a valid IP address, mDNS does not re-announce
Because CONFIG_MDNS_RESPONDER_TTL is non-zero, the hostname entry is:
Cached by clients (OS, browser, router, multicast cache)
Persistently associated with the wrong IP address

Important Limitation

Manually stopping and restarting mDNS does not solve the issue, because:

The hostname is already cached in the network due to

CONFIG_MDNS_RESPONDER_TTL


Even if the responder is restarted later, clients may continue resolving
zephyr.local to the old IP until the TTL expires

This makes application-level restart workarounds unreliable or ineffective

Expected Behavior

One of the following should occur by default or be configurable:

* mDNS announcements are deferred until a valid DHCP lease exists
* OR mDNS automatically re-announces with the new IP when DHCP assigns it
* OR the stack provides a clean, documented mechanism to synchronize DHCP and mDNS startup

Please Correct me if I am using the Library wrong

Best regards


### Describe the solution you'd like

Any of the following would address the issue cleanly at framework level:

New Kconfig option

CONFIG_MDNS_START_AFTER_DHCP


Ensures mDNS is only started once DHCP is in BOUND state

Automatic re-announcement

Triggered on NET_EVENT_IPV4_ADDR_ADD

When address source is DHCP

Handles TTL implications correctly

Public API / documented workflow

Explicit control over when mDNS starts

Without relying on internal net_mgmt hooks or fragile workarounds

Current Workarounds (Unsatisfactory)

Restarting mDNS manually → ineffective due to TTL caching

Forcing static IP → undesirable in many real networks

Custom net event logic → fragile and application-specific

Environment

Zephyr version: 4.1

Interface: Ethernet

IPv4 + DHCP enabled

mDNS responder enabled

Issue is reproducible on cold boot (timing-dependent)

Impact

This affects:

Web-based configuration portals

Device discovery UX

First-boot and provisioning scenarios

Because TTL caching makes recovery difficult, this issue is best solved inside the Zephyr networking stack, not in applications.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102718.title: Delay mDNS Announcement Until DHCP Lease Is Acquired

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102866.body:
### Describe the bug

When building an application targeting `native_sim` boards, the link step fails with undefined reference errors for `ztest_set_test_args` and `ztest_reset_test_args`, required by `ztest.c`.

For `native_sim` targets, ztest is built using `ztest_posix.c`, which does not provide implementations for these functions. On other boards, `ztest_shell.c` is used instead, which defines the symbols.

As a result, configurations that enable ztest functionality on `native_sim` select an implementation that lacks the required APIs, leading to linker failures.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Enable `ZTEST_SHELL` and select board `native_sim` 
2. Build

### Relevant log output

```shell
> west build -b native_sim example-application/app
[1/2] Building native simulator runner, and linking final executable

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102889.body:
### Describe the bug

See also https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/94916#issuecomment-3790296277 and #94916

This may affect other vendors but I am unable to test due to lack of hardware.

More testing on different STM32 HW should be done as well - I will do it and update this message when time allows.

---

Commit 3c4b4e1ae22c01a1b4a12123db4e49038a8c78a6 introduced a "regression" in the Mass Storage Class when combined with the STM32 UDC driver:
* before the commit:
  * MSC I/O worked fine on Linux with some errors printed in `dmesg`
  * USB3CV `MSC Tests` suite mostly passed on Windows
    * crash when reaching `Command Set Test - Device Configured`
    * all prior tests passed
* after the commit:
  * MSC I/O works fine on Linux with no errors in `dmesg`
  * USB3CV `MSC Tests` suite no longer passes on Windows

---

Main problem: **commit 1243aba8e5a9565e761923a8f51c9a7273b32b6b** breaks MSC completely when combined with the STM32 UDC driver:
* MSC I/O not functional on Linux, errors in `dmesg`
* USB3CV `MSC Tests` error out due to timeout on Windows
  * whereas before, they would fail quickly

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Tested on `stm32f769i_disco`:
```
$ git checkout 81fd57078de4609d7ada72a037120f5e44d68021     # `main` as of writing
# revert usbd_msc.c to a non-broken revision
$ git revert --no-edit 152844a7e0f9edeb143cc8f865a23da7648968c0 7e11bc58172e0ae5804220e7f6e07a9c292c07df 1243aba8e5a9565e761923a8f51c9a7273b32b6b
$ west build ... && west flash
# try to access files on MSC: should work fine

# discard revert of commit that causes issues
$ git reset --hard HEAD~1
$ west build ... && west flash
# try to access files on MSC: should be broken
```

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Major – Severely degrades functionality; workaround is difficult or unavailable.

### Environment

- OS: Linux/Windows
- Toolchain: Zephyr SDK 0.17.4
- Commit SHA or version used: varies

### Additional Context

_No response_

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102904.body:
### Summary

The interrupt enable/disable logic used in the `scmi_send_message_polling()` logic violates the layered architecture of the SCMI support. Specifically, said function directly calls SHMEM/MBOX-related functions even if all of this should be handled by transport layer. The SCMI core shouldn't be aware of the type of transport that's being used.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Move the interrupt handling logic to the transport layer. The core should only be calling `scmi_transport_send_message()` with the polling-related information being encoded in the message structure. From there on, the transport layer should take care of enabling/disabling interrupts based on the encoded information.

### Alternatives

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102904.title: firmware: scmi: core: interrupt enable/disable logic violates layered architecture

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102912.body:
### Problem Description

Although overlays are a reasonable way of configuring applications for different platforms, they use in samples and tests seems to be far too frequent and without any control. A high number of overlays is an indication that sample/test is not platform-agnostic. This is a maintenance issue. Overlays delay PR reviews.

### Proposed Change (Summary)

Strict rules for the use of overlays in Zephyr tree. Driver APIs samples/tests and subsystems should generally be platform-agnostic and not require platform specific overlays.

### Proposed Change (Detailed)

For all non-platform-specific samples and tests:

Single-line configuration overlays should generally not be allowed. If necessary, and there are no other options to configure it on the board/shield/snippet level, the user should pass this line as a build option or place it in the `extra_configs` property of the sample.yaml/testcase.yaml.

There are quite many trivial devicetree overlays in the tree containing just a few lines to define a nodelabel. This kind of devicetree overlays should generally not be allowed. Boards/platforms should either define the nodelabel or use mechanisms like snippets. Driver API or area maintainers should agree on a nodelabels that will be used in all samples and tests belonging to the area in Zephyr tree. See usage of `zephyr_i2c` or `zephyr_udc0`. Project should establish guidelines how to use this kind of "well-known" nodelabels.
Driver APIs/subsystems and their associated tests should be designed so that they do not require specific configuration, but instead provide an API to obtain driver capabilities and act accordingly.

It should be documented that, if a board does not provide a common feature by default (e.g. a button or LED), adding overlays to fake this feature, to a sample or test that requires this feature, is not allowed. For example, faking a button or LED using a free GPIO pin is not allowed.

Adding a board/SoC-specific overlays to a sample designed to be as platform-agnostic as possible (e.g. with dependencies represented by well-known nodelabels zephyr_udc0 and zephyr_i2c) should generally not be allowed.

...

### Dependencies

-

### Concerns and Unresolved Questions

-

### Alternatives Considered

No alternatives.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#102912.title: Reduce number of configuration and devicetree overlays in samples and tests

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#103029.body:
### Describe the bug

Summary

NVS may fail to mount after a power loss occurs while programming the last data ATE（not last ate for delete） in a sector, when the remaining space is only sufficient for an delete-ATE but not for any further data entries.
In this scenario, the sector is logically exhausted but not properly closed, causing nvs_startup() to fail instead of triggering garbage collection.

Description

NVS writes an entry by first programming the data area and then writing the corresponding ATE.
If a power loss occurs after the data has been written but during the last data-ATE programming, and the remaining free space in the sector is only large enough for a single delete-ATE, the following situation can arise:
	•	The ATE may be partially written and therefore invalid.
	•	No erased space remains that is large enough for a data entry.
	•	The sector is logically full, but not explicitly closed.

Example layout near the end of a sector:

``` 
[ data ][ data ][ erase ][ ATE ][ ATE ][ erase ]  (sector end)
                           ^^^

[ data ][ data ][ erase ][ ATE (invalid) ][ ATE ][ erase ]
        ^       ^
     data_wra  ate_wra
```

During startup, nvs_startup() scans the sector and fails to find any erased space usable for data writes.
As a result, the mount process fails, even though the sector should be considered exhausted and eligible for garbage collection.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Steps to Reproduce
	1.	Use NVS with a sector size such that only space for a single ATE remains after the last data write.
	2.	Trigger a power loss:
	•	After the data area is fully written
	•	While the corresponding ATE is being programmed
	3.	Reboot the system and call nvs_mount().

https://github.com/LingaoM/zephyr/commit/19c8f94248d09beb06a1037524e16dd372f6ae06

Please checkout this branch

`west build -b native_sim zephyr/samples/subsys/nvs/ -p auto`
sudo ./build/zephyr/zephyr.exe and then run again, will see below log:

```
(.venv-3.10) ╭─mi@lingao ~/works/github/zephyrproject  
╰─➤  sudo ./build/zephyr/zephyr.exe 
*** Booting Zephyr OS build v4.3.0-4932-g7a218e5db564 ***
[00:00:00.000,000] <dbg> fs_nvs: nvs_recover_last_ate: Recovering last ate from sector 0
[00:00:00.000,000] <inf> fs_nvs: 3 Sectors of 4096 bytes
[00:00:00.000,000] <inf> fs_nvs: alloc wra: 0, fd8
[00:00:00.000,000] <inf> fs_nvs: data wra: 0, fd8
[00:00:00.000,000] <dbg> fs_nvs: nvs_flash_erase_sector: Erasing flash at fc000, len 4096
[00:00:00.000,000] <dbg> fs_nvs: nvs_flash_erase_sector: Erasing flash at fd000, len 4096
[00:00:00.000,000] <dbg> fs_nvs: nvs_flash_erase_sector: Erasing flash at fe000, len 4096
[00:00:00.000,000] <dbg> fs_nvs: nvs_recover_last_ate: Recovering last ate from sector 0
[00:00:00.000,000] <dbg> fs_nvs: nvs_add_gc_done_ate: Adding gc done ate at ff0
[00:00:00.000,000] <inf> fs_nvs: 3 Sectors of 4096 bytes
[00:00:00.000,000] <inf> fs_nvs: alloc wra: 0, fe8
[00:00:00.000,000] <inf> fs_nvs: data wra: 0, 0
^C
Stopped at 1.290s
(.venv-3.10) ╭─mi@lingao ~/works/github/zephyrproject  
╰─➤  sudo ./build/zephyr/zephyr.exe 
*** Booting Zephyr OS build v4.3.0-4932-g7a218e5db564 ***
[00:00:00.000,000] <dbg> fs_nvs: nvs_recover_last_ate: Recovering last ate from sector 0
Flash Init failed, rc=1
^C
Stopped at 5.090s
```

Expected Behavior
	•	During startup, NVS should detect that:
	•	The last ATE is invalid or incomplete.
	•	No erased space remains after the last valid ATE.
	•	The sector should be explicitly closed.
	•	Garbage collection should be triggered.
	•	nvs_mount() should succeed and provide a writable sector.

Actual Behavior
	•	nvs_startup() fails to find erased space suitable for data writes.
	•	The sector is not closed.
	•	Garbage collection is not triggered.
	•	nvs_mount() fails, even though the sector is logically full.

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.

### Environment

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#103042.body: ### Describe the bug When calling `uart_tx_abort` API with stm32u5 MCU, the call might get stuck forever into loop in `dma_stm32_suspend` waiting `SUSP` flag to be set. This one: https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/935373e9186139f326bf07865f17d3925049a173/drivers/dma/dma_stm32u5.c#L660-L662 Backtrace: My application does the following: 1. In its own task, data is sent periodically using UART async API. 2. Other task sends message to the communication task asking communication to stop. The communication task then calls `uart_tx_abort` unconditionally to stop the current transfer. Usually this works fine, but when this is repeated hundreds of times, then sometimes `dma_suspend` does not exit. ### Regression - [ ] This is a regression. ### Steps to reproduce _No response_ ### Relevant log output ```shell ``` ### Impact Intermittent – Occurs occasionally; hard to reproduce. ### Environment Zephyr 4.3, SDK 0.17.4, stm32u585 based custom board. ### Additional Context AI explanation (no guarantees of quality of it) is that this is due to a race situation. When DMA is idle just after completing tx, then writing the suspend register does not set the suspend flag: AI suggested fix is to remove the dma_suspend call completely: I tried that, and the function does not get into stuck anymore. I've been running the code for 12 hours without problems.

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#103076.body:
### Describe the bug

The `kernel.timer.timer` test (timer_behavior) fails on all ARC hardware boards that have `CONFIG_SYS_CLOCK_TICKS_PER_SEC=100` explicitly set in their defconfig, such as (`hsdk`, `hsdk/arc_hsdk/2cores`,  `hsdk4xd` and `emsdp`).

The test uses `CONFIG_TIMER_TEST_PERIOD=1000` (1 ms timer period), but when `CONFIG_SYS_CLOCK_TICKS_PER_SEC=100`:
- **System tick period = 10 ms** (1/100 Hz)
- **Test timer period = 1 ms**

Since the requested timer period (1 ms) is **smaller than the system tick period (10 ms)**, the kernel cannot schedule timers at the requested resolution. The timer is rounded up to the next tick boundary.

**Note:** The test passes on `iotdk` because it does **not** have this setting and uses the default tick rate. and i see the commit b142033b91722b4e03545eab2e698b3c25ff2e21 removed CONFIG_SYS_CLOCK_TICKS_PER_SEC=100 from several ARC boards including `iotdk`, `em_starterkit` and others. 
However, `hsdk`, `hsdk4xd`, and `emsdp` boards were added later and **incorrectly included** `CONFIG_SYS_CLOCK_TICKS_PER_SEC=100`.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

west twister -p hsdk/arc_hsdk -s kernel.timer.timer

### Relevant log output

```shell
*** Booting Zephyr OS build v4.3.0-4808-ga5d4626b8c16 ***
Running TESTSUITE timer_jitter_drift
===================================================================
START - test_jitter_drift_timer_period
periodic timer behavior test using built-in restart mechanism
collecting time samples for approx 100 seconds
```

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.

### Environment

OS: Linux
Toolchain: Zephyr SDK
Commit SHA or Version used: Main e7ae93b39fcb04661e0b03d6c9b86ebc968403d9

### Additional Context

_No response_

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#103135.body:
### Describe the bug

The `C Language Support` page indicates:
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/9bd3e3f32dfe9a054d1110f4a3498032ed2efa0b/doc/develop/languages/c/index.rst?#L15-L18

Emphasis mine:
> Applications must return zero (0) from main. All non-zero return
values are reserved.

This requirement is not documented in the `C++ Language Support` page:
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/9bd3e3f32dfe9a054d1110f4a3498032ed2efa0b/doc/develop/languages/cpp/index.rst?#L33-L37


The two pages should be harmonized such that either:
1. the requirement is dropped from the `C Language Support` page
2. the requirement is added to the `C++ Language Support` page

(sidenote: remark that `CONFIG_BOOTARGS` is not documented in the `C Language Support` page, only the `C++ Language Support` one as well as [a more generic "system" page](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/9bd3e3f32dfe9a054d1110f4a3498032ed2efa0b/doc/kernel/services/threads/system_threads.rst))

On a related note, there are several in-tree samples which don't comply with the current requirements:
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/9bd3e3f32dfe9a054d1110f4a3498032ed2efa0b/samples/drivers/i2s/output/src/main.c#L59-L70
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/9bd3e3f32dfe9a054d1110f4a3498032ed2efa0b/samples/boards/espressif/xt_wdt/src/main.c#L32-L36

Depending on the approach chosen (1/2), these samples may have to be updated.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

N/A

N/A
```

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#103140.body:
### Describe the bug

We are facing issues with the TCAN4x5x drivers latest addition of power management features. This change in commit 

https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/commit/631dbd98b436c659fa6956a2cacd78a7f6817fc7

slightly changed the initialization procedure. Before that change, the crystal frequency as well as the desired operation mode were set in a single transaction(write to the MODE_CONFIG register, 0x0800). Now, the mode is set first(normal mode, 0x02) and the crystal clock frequency configuration(bit CLKREF set for 40MHz, default =1 on powerup) and the voltage configuration for nWKRQ(bit nWKRQ_VOLTAGE is set, default =0 on powerup) is done afterwards in two individual transactions. This leads to a stall of the following MCAN initialization.

I cross-checked by removing the first write to MODE_CONFIG and writing the final setting with all bits as desired only once in the end before doing the MCAN init, this works as well as in previous versions. I also cross-checked by just writing the same value twice(keep crystal and nWKRQ voltage settings as read before), this also stalls the init.


### Regression

- [x] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### Relevant log output

```shell
Zephyr v4.3 version:

[00:00:39.034,271] <dbg> can_tcan4x5x: tcan4x5x_init: TCAN4550, SPI 2 rev. 0, device rev. ID 2.1
[00:00:39.034,362] <inf> can_tcan4x5x: Entering normal mode 841
[00:00:39.034,576] <dbg> can_tcan4x5x: tcan4x5x_set_config_mode_sel: read MODE_CONFIG reg: 0xc8000468
[00:00:39.034,606] <dbg> can_tcan4x5x: tcan4x5x_set_config_mode_sel: current mode 1, new mode 2
[00:00:39.034,881] <dbg> can_tcan4x5x: tcan4x5x_init_normal_mode: set normal mode in MODE_CONFIG reg: 0xc80004a8
[00:00:39.035,095] <dbg> can_tcan4x5x: tcan4x5x_init_normal_mode: wrote MODE_CONFIG reg: 0xc80804a8
[00:00:39.040,222] <dbg> can_mcan: can_mcan_init: IP rel: 3.2.1 05.3.32
[00:00:39.041,381] <dbg> can_mcan: can_mcan_init: Presc: 2, TS1: 139, TS2: 20
