zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105041.body:
### Describe the bug

Enabling both DMIC and FlexSPI on MIMXRT595S fails to build because MCUX_DMIC_CLK and MCUX_FLEXSPI_CLK both expand to MCUX_LPC_CLK_ID(0x0A, 0x00), producing a duplicate case value in clock_control_mcux_syscon.c.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Board: mimxrt595_evk/mimxrt595s/cm33
Zephyr: v4.3.0
Enable CONFIG_AUDIO_DMIC=y alongside any FlexSPI usage (e.g. CONFIG_FLASH=y)
Build fails

### Relevant log output

```shell
`clock_control_mcux_syscon.c:469:9: error: duplicate case value
  469 |     case MCUX_FLEXSPI_CLK:
      |     ^~~~
clock_control_mcux_syscon.c:464:9: note: previously used here
  464 |     case MCUX_DMIC_CLK:
      |     ^~~~`
```

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.

### Environment

Board: mimxrt595_evk/mimxrt595s/cm33
Zephyr: v4.3.0
Enable CONFIG_AUDIO_DMIC=y alongside any FlexSPI usage (e.g. CONFIG_FLASH=y)
Build fails

### Additional Context

Root cause
In the clock ID header, both are defined with the same subsystem/offset pair. The DMIC clock needs a distinct offset value.
Workaround
Changed MCUX_FLEXSPI_CLK to MCUX_LPC_CLK_ID(0x0A, 0x02) in an out-of-tree shadowed header.
Environment

Zephyr v4.3.0
NXP MIMXRT595S (CM33)
Toolchain: Zephyr SDK / GCC
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105041.title: clock: mcux_syscon: MCUX_DMIC_CLK collides with MCUX_FLEXSPI_CLK — build failure on RT595

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105101.body:
### Describe the bug

Attempts to query the SNTP time with `CONFIG_SNTP_UNCERTAINTY=y` now always fail with `-EINVAL`.

PR #97688 fixed the transmit timestamp by switching from device uptime to `SYS_CLOCK_REALTIME`, but inadvertently broke the timestamp comparison used to validate server responses.
https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/fb350217cc199e7e6c5a40b4e50a04adbf038f8b/subsys/net/lib/sntp/sntp.c#L100-L110
`dest_ts_us` and `orig_ts_us` are now in different time bases, so the naive comparison is invalid.

### Regression

- [x] This is a regression.

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105101.title: SNTP: response parsing fails with `SNTP_UNCERTAINTY=y`

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105106.body:
### Describe the bug

The [arc_core_mpu_buffer_validate()](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/d0c2f51f811edd878165f9db5514dd4a333e6990/arch/arc/core/mpu/arc_mpu_common_internal.h#L210) function for ARC MPUv6 has a race condition that can cause spurious memory access denials during syscall validation. This occurs because the function iterates through MPU regions using bank selection without interrupt protection.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

west build -b nsim/nsim_hs/mpuv6 tests/kernel/mem_protect/syscalls -T kernel.memory_protection.syscalls.timeslicing -p
west flash

### Relevant log output

```shell

Running syscall switch stress test with 4 threads on 1 cpu(s)

    Assertion failed at WEST_TOPDIR/zephyr/tests/kernel/mem_protect/syscalls/src/main.c:417: syscall_switch_stress: (ret not equal to 0)
stress: string_copy: string should have matched (14)
E: ***** Exception vector: 0x7, cause code: 0x0, parameter 0x0
E: Address 0x670c
E: EV_PrivilegeV
E: Privilege violation
E:  r0: 0x0  r1: 0x47  r2: 0xa00  r3: 0x0
E:  r4: 0x0  r5: 0xcac0  r6: 0x47  r7: 0x0
E:  r8: 0x0  r9: 0x0 r10: 0x0 r11: 0x0
E: r12: 0xffffffff r13: 0x0  pc: 0x670c
E:  blink: 0x3fac status32: 0x80186a82
E: lp_end: 0x0 lp_start: 0x0 lp_count: 0xc
E: >>> ZEPHYR FATAL ERROR 0: CPU exception on CPU 0
E: Current thread: 0x80000f30 (unknown)
Caught system error -- reason 0
Unexpected fault during test
===================================================================
PROJECT EXECUTION FAILED
```

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.

### Environment

OS: Linux
Toolchain: Zephyr SDK
Commit SHA or Version used: Main d0c2f51f811edd878165f9db5514dd4a333e6990

### Additional Context

Similar to: https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/22290

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105162.body:
### Describe the bug

Using a renesas ra4m2, connected a uart_bridge between cdc-acm and uart2.  Whenever data is received from cdc, duplicate characters are being transmitted to the uart2.  The issue seems to be that:
 
1. uart_bridge calls irq_tx_enable() on uart2 whenever data is received from cdc-acm.
2. uart_ra_sci_irq_tx_enable() clears the Transmit Data End and Transmit Data Empty flags from the status register in addition to enabling the transmit interrupts.  clearing the TDRE and/or TEND status flags seems to cause the uart peripheral to re-send the last value that was written to the TDR register.

So it seems that calling irq_tx_enable() causes the uart to resend the last transmitted byte, and uart_bridge calls irq_tx_enable() every time data is received from the cdc end of the bridge.

I am not sure what the purpose of clearing the TDRE and TEND flags is in tx_enable, it seems like these can be removed.  After removing that part, uart_bridge is working as intended for me.


### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create uart_bridge between cdc_acm_uart0 and uart2:
```
    uart_bridge0: uart_bridge0 {
        compatible = "zephyr,uart-bridge";
        peers = <&cdc_acm_uart0 &uart2>;
    };
```
2. Send data on /dev/ttyACM0
3. Receive duplicate data from uart2

In my case, uart2 was connected to the console tty of another linux system.  Attempting to enter "root" at the login: prompt transmitted "Rrroott" where "R" was the last byte of the previous ANSI sequence transmitted.


### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

### Environment

- Zephyr 4.3.0
- OS: Ubuntu 24.04, `tio` connected to /dev/ttyACM0
- zephyr SDK 0.17.4

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105162.title: drivers: serial: uart_renesas_ra_sci.c transmits duplicate charactes

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105216.body:
The driver requires nodelabel `stm32_lp_tick_source` to be attached on an LPTIM to work properly. If not done, a build failure occurs - see #105121 for details.

Add missing nodelabel to a board which lacked it and harden Kconfig to prevent compiling the driver when the nodelabel is missing, ensuring the build error cannot occur again.

Fixes #105121

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105244.body: ### Describe the bug Problem seems to be that the reads for the fifo streams are too long. It seems to be this way because the fifo reads are gotten from buf_avail and not the req_len This is when [Problematic line](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/drivers/sensor/st/lsm6dsvxxx/lsm6dsvxxx_stream.c#L311) ### evidence So here i changed the watermark levels from 4 and 64 to see how it would effect the buffer lens. To see if it was just because i was using using a fairly small watermark. But because the amount of data read from the fifo is always related to the buffer length and not the actual size of the needed read. It's always off by a large margin. Setting watermark 4 [00:00:03.254,000] LSM6DSVXXX: fifo count 4, **size 28**, size fifo data 19, **buffer len 256** Setting watermark 64 [00:00:26.922,000] LSM6DSVXXX: fifo count 64, **size 448**, size fifo data 19, **buffer len 512** ### Regression - [ ] This is a regression. ### Steps to reproduce 1. west build -p -b nucleo_n657x0_q app 2. west flash The code i was using to test this wasn't inside the zephyr repo. But i can provide snippets if need to reproduce it. ### Relevant log output ```shell ``` ### Impact Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable. ### Environment - OS = Linux - zephyr version - v4.3.0-4645-g1bc469bcb454 - Git hash - 1bc469bcb454f307b2ef73e1059fa2e341bc00a1 ### Additional Context ## board * nucleo_n657x0_q #### Dts file ~~~dts &i2c4 { status = "okay"; clock-frequency = ; // I2C_BITRATE_STANDARD I2C_BITRATE_FAST I2C_BITRATE_FAST_PLUS lsm6dsv320x: lsm6dsv320x@6b { status = "okay"; compatible = "st,lsm6dsv320x"; reg = <0x6b>; int1-gpios = <&gpiof 5 GPIO_ACTIVE_HIGH>; // INT1 on LSM6DSV320X connected to STMod+ //fifo settings gyro-range = <0x5>; accel-range = <4>; //8G fifo-watermark = <4>; gyro-odr = <0x7>; accel-odr = <0x7>; //240 accel-fifo-batch-rate = <0x7>; //240 gyro-fifo-batch-rate = <0x7>; //240 temp-fifo-batch-rate = <0x0>; sflp-odr = <0x4>; //240 sflp-fifo-enable = <0x0>; // 1 == Game rotation }; } ~~~ ~~~c //Using the RTIO part of the driver //setting fifo trigger mode for watermark SENSOR_DT_STREAM_IODEV(imu_stream, DT_ALIAS(imu_device), {SENSOR_TRIG_FIFO_WATERMARK, SENSOR_STREAM_DATA_INCLUDE}, ); ~~~ ### Current capture length In the capture you can see that whats suppose to be around 28 bytes because i set the watermark to 4 is way larger. Also after it reaches the normal amount of data and you start to read beyond what your fifo's set to. It seems to just repeat the same 7 values. ### Reduce changed to req_len - seizeof(hdr) Example of just reducing the buf_avail to the req_len - seizeof(hdr). So you only grab the amount of data that you want from the fifo.

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105266.body:
### Describe the bug

The [`rpi_4b`](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/boards/raspberrypi/rpi_4b/doc/index.rst) [documentation](https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/boards/raspberrypi/rpi_4b/doc/index.html#:~:text=bootcode.bin) instructs you to
> 1. Download and place these firmware files:
> * [bcm2711-rpi-4-b.dtb](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/raspberrypi/firmware/master/boot/bcm2711-rpi-4-b.dtb)
> * [bootcode.bin](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/raspberrypi/firmware/master/boot/bootcode.bin)
> * [start4.elf](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/raspberrypi/firmware/master/boot/start4.elf)

But as the Raspberry Pi documentation states [here](https://www.raspberrypi.com/documentation/computers/configuration.html#bootcode-bin)
> The Raspberry Pi 4 and 5 do not use bootcode.bin. It has been replaced by boot code in the onboard EEPROM.

and [here](https://www.raspberrypi.com/documentation/computers/raspberry-pi.html#special-bootcode-bin-only-boot-mode)
> Since Raspberry Pi 4, flagship devices do not use the bootcode.bin file. Instead, these devices use a bootloader located in an on-board EEPROM chip.

However, there is another file [fixup4.dat](https://www.raspberrypi.com/documentation/computers/configuration.html#fixup-dat) recommended for the Pi 4B that [relocates start4.elf](https://www.raspberrypi.com/documentation/computers/config_txt.html#start_file-fixup_file) according to the [`gpu_mem`](https://www.raspberrypi.com/documentation/computers/legacy_config_txt.html#gpu_mem) split (see [likely explanation](https://waldorf.waveform.org.uk/2025/pull-yourself-up-by-your-bootstraps.html#afaik)).

Therefore I believe the aforementioned list of recommended boot files should be amended to
* [bcm2711-rpi-4-b.dtb](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/raspberrypi/firmware/master/boot/bcm2711-rpi-4-b.dtb)
* [start4.elf](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/raspberrypi/firmware/master/boot/start4.elf)
* [fixup4.dat](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/raspberrypi/firmware/master/boot/fixup4.dat)

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Visit https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/boards/raspberrypi/rpi_4b/doc/index.html#:~:text=bootcode.bin or https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/boards/raspberrypi/rpi_4b/doc/index.rst?plain=1#L48

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.

### Environment

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105291.body:
### Describe the bug

When Zephyr's L2 PPP driver is used against PPPD implementation from Linux, it fails, unless option `asyncmap 0xffffffff` is specified.

When peer sends the Async-Control-Character-Map option on LCP
phase, Zephyr correctly responsed with Configure-Ack.
But the LCP negotiation phase have not been finnished yet,
so the remote end may not expect us to immediately
update the value.

This is seen when trying to use Zephyr's PPP stack against
PPPD from Linux, where default asyncmap option is zero.


### Regression

- [x] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### Relevant log output

```shell
Connect: ppp0 <--> /dev/ttyACM0
sent [LCP ConfReq id=0x1 <asyncmap 0x0> <magic 0x42717a60> <pcomp> <accomp>]
rcvd [LCP ConfReq id=0x1 <mru 1464> <asyncmap 0xffffffff>]
sent [LCP ConfAck id=0x1 <mru 1464> <asyncmap 0xffffffff>]
sent [LCP ConfReq id=0x1 <asyncmap 0x0> <magic 0x42717a60> <pcomp> <accomp>]
rcvd [LCP ConfRej id=0x1 <magic 0x42717a60> <pcomp> <accomp>]
sent [LCP ConfReq id=0x2 <asyncmap 0x0>]
sent [LCP ConfReq id=0x2 <asyncmap 0x0>]
sent [LCP ConfReq id=0x2 <asyncmap 0x0>]
sent [LCP ConfReq id=0x2 <asyncmap 0x0>]
```

### Impact

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- 

### Additional Context

This looks like a regression when these two PRs are combined:
* https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/97963
* https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/89580
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105291.title: net: l2: ppp: PPP negotiation fails against Linux PPPD without asyncmap 0xffffffff

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105374.body:
### Describe the bug

The ISR in drivers/gpio/gpio_sam.c reads PIO_ISR and passes the raw value to gpio_fire_callbacks() without masking against PIO_IMR. PIO_ISR records if there's been any pin transitions since the last time it has been read, regardless of whether the associated interrupt was enabled or not. Therefore, when another enabled interrupt fires on the controller, it can result in callbacks  for pins whose interrupts have been explicitly disabled with GPIO_INT_DISABLE.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Install two interrupts A and B with callbacks on the same PIO controller, disable A, enable B.
2. Change pin to cause interrupt for A (if it were enabled) -> nothing happens
3. Change pin to cause interrupt for B -> interrupt fires, callbacks occur for both A and B

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.

### Environment

- Ubuntu 24.04
- zephyr SDK 0.16.8
- zephyr commit b43e96baa55011c533d2b7fbc2c11531f1c48ae2

### Additional Context

_No response_

### Summary

The current JSON lib implementation hard codes the float and double precision at 9 and 16 respectively. This can create larger than needed JSON objects and inflate size of data being transmitted in communications.

### Describe the solution you'd like

I would like to create Kconfig options for setting the applications desired precision for JSON encoding of float and double values.

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105506.body:
### Summary

[uart_nrfx_uarte.c, uarte_pm_suspend](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/drivers/serial/uart_nrfx_uarte.c#L2976-L2977) checks some preconditions with __ASSERT_NO_MSG. 

In my experience that causes unintended and not required crashes.

### Describe the solution you'd like

If the precondition is not full-filled, returning -EAGAIN would be a better choice.

### Alternatives

Try to ensure, that the preconditions are full filled before calling suspend. 
But that seem to be much harder than just return an error code instead of using an ASSERT. And if it fails, the device crashes.

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105506.title: drivers: nrfx_uarte: uarte_pm_suspend, please return -EAGAIN instead of kill the device with ASSERT

Adding support for this memory in the flash driver. If this memory is used and the support is not in there, the driver will set the memory into 4bytes address mode which will cause issues with the bootROM.

Add the support for the memory winbond 25Q256JV in the flash driver when the JEDEC-ID is read from the memory at the function **flash_flexspi_nor_check_jedec**

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105644.body:
### Describe the bug

SNTP is unable to perform a proper query when `CONFIG_SNTP_UNCERTAINTY=y`. I believe it's because we're mixing `CLOCK_REALTIME` and `k_uptime_ticks()`. Invalid lines:

```c
if (rx_ts_us > tx_ts_us || orig_ts_us > dest_ts_us) {
    NET_DBG("Invalid timestamps from SNTP server");
    return -EINVAL;
}
```
I believe the faulty commit is `98e0ddecc0621185225f5aad833a6989a98457ed`.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Steps to reproduce are quite simple (in my case, I've used `esp32s3_devkitc/esp32s3/procpu`. Sample used is `zephyr/samples/net/sockets/sntp_client`. To the `prj.conf` add:
```
CONFIG_SNTP_LOG_LEVEL_DBG=y
CONFIG_SNTP_UNCERTAINTY=y
```
In this directory:

- run build with e.g. `west build -p auto -S wifi-ipv4 -b esp32s3_devkitc/esp32s3/procpu --no-sysbuild`
- flash and open serial logs with e.g. `west flash --no-rebuild && west espressif monitor -b 115200`
- provide necessary credentials for connectivity if needed
- check the logs


### Relevant log output


zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105673.body:
### Describe the bug

In `drivers/pinctrl/Kconfig.imx`, a dependency is missing in `config PINCTRL_IMX_SCMI` entry.

Here is what it looks like currently:
```
config PINCTRL_IMX_SCMI
	bool "Pin controller SCMI-based driver for i.MX SoCs"
	depends on ARM_SCMI_PINCTRL_HELPERS
	default y
	help
```

As a result, the "pin controller SCMI-based driver for i.MX SoCs" is built (enabled at least, even if the build fails) **mistakenly** when another SoC enables "helper functions for SCMI pinctrl protocol" (CONFIG_ARM_SCMI_PINCTRL_HELPERS).

Not sure what the relevant dependency should be here (adding `depends on PINCTRL_IMX` was sufficient to get rid of the issue with my SoC but it was just a quick fix). Any suggestion ?

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.
(...but blocking for any other SoC using ARM SCMI pinctrl protocol !) 

### Environment

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105687.body:
## Summary

The USB-C stack does not notify the Device Policy Manager (DPM) when a port transitions from attached to unattached. This makes it impossible for an application to know when to reduce current consumption back to the default level after a detach event.

## Expected Behavior

A `usbc_policy_notify_t` notification should be sent to the DPM callback when the port detaches, allowing the application to respond (e.g. reduce current draw, disable power rails).

## Actual Behavior

No notification is sent to the DPM upon detach. The following sequence is observed when attaching and then detaching a PD charger:

```c
// Attach
[00:00:20.645,000] <inf> main: NOTIFY:POWER_ROLE_IS_SINK
[00:00:20.645,000] <inf> main: NOTIFY:NOT_PD_CONNECTED
[00:00:20.667,000] <inf> main: NOTIFY:POWER_CHANGE_3A0
[00:00:20.733,000] <inf> main: NOTIFY:SOURCE_CAPABILITIES_RECEIVED
[00:00:20.733,000] <inf> main: NOTIFY:PD_CONNECTED
[00:00:20.868,000] <inf> main: NOTIFY:TRANSITION_PS

// Detach
// No notifications to DPM
```

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Build and flash the `samples/subsys/usb_c/sink` sample to a board with a USB-C port and TCPC (e.g. `stm32g081b_eval`).
2. In `port0_notify()`, add a log for every `usbc_policy_notify_t` value so all notifications are visible on the serial console (or use the updated sink sample which already does this).
3. Open a serial terminal connected to the board.
4. Connect a PD charger. 
5. Disconnect the PD charger.

Observe: No notifications are printed. The DPM callback is never called on detach.

### Relevant log output

```shell
// Attach
[00:00:20.645,000] <inf> main: NOTIFY:POWER_ROLE_IS_SINK
[00:00:20.645,000] <inf> main: NOTIFY:NOT_PD_CONNECTED
[00:00:20.667,000] <inf> main: NOTIFY:POWER_CHANGE_3A0
[00:00:20.733,000] <inf> main: NOTIFY:SOURCE_CAPABILITIES_RECEIVED
[00:00:20.733,000] <inf> main: NOTIFY:PD_CONNECTED
[00:00:20.868,000] <inf> main: NOTIFY:TRANSITION_PS

// Detach
// No notifications to DPM
```

### Impact

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

### Environment

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105687.title: usb_c: No DPM notification on port detach

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105867.body:
### Describe the bug

The issue was encountered while testing a Matter MCXW72 Zephyr POC and it manifests as an assert trigger in [SubMac::BeginTransmit](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/openthread/blob/e4d97681c53ec1cc34af1404ad2960adda4ba691/src/core/mac/sub_mac.cpp#L522). 

The assert is caused by [tx_job work item still being busy](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/b7d49490136bcf5bd13d0570593205b05a36e186/modules/openthread/platform/radio.c#L629) even though the packet is sent and the event `PENDING_EVENT_TX_DONE` is processed.

Seems like internal state of the work item (tx_job) is not set fast enough. Because the `PENDING_EVENT_TX_DONE` event is already processed, the openthread thread tries sending packets again but it fails.

I made the tx_job work item global and checked it into [handle_tx_done](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/b7d49490136bcf5bd13d0570593205b05a36e186/modules/openthread/platform/radio.c#L481C11-L481C12) and most of the times is still busy even though [transmit_message](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/b7d49490136bcf5bd13d0570593205b05a36e186/modules/openthread/platform/radio.c#L382) finished. 

I'm not sure why this issue wasn't encountered in Zephyr examples. My guess is that the matter example is more complex.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

The matter zephyr example is a work in progress and not yet available.

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Functional Limitation – Some features not working as expected, but system usable.

### Environment

Build on Ubuntu 24.04 with Matter v1.6 and Zephyr 4.3

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#105867.title: modules: openthread: Race condition in radio.c

### Describe the bug

When BLE sample on stm32wba is compiled with option CONFIG_FLASH enabled, compilation error "in function `FM_ProcessRequest': zephyr/drivers/flash/flash_stm32wba_fm.c:52:(.text.FM_ProcessRequest+0xc): undefined reference to `ble_ctrl_work_q''" is reported.

The problem is due to the fact that ble_ctrl_work_q is declared in extern in the flash_stm32wba_fm.c
The ble_ctrl_work_q was previously declared in \zephyr\soc\st\stm32\stm32wbax\hci_if\host_stack_if.c but a commit has changed the name of this k_work_q to ble_ctlr_work_q and this variable is declared in static

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#106076.body:
### Describe the bug

The problem is when the interval is set its in (Nanoseconds/100) and when you go to get the interval its in (Microseconds * 100).  

* [setting interval](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/subsys/usb/device_next/class/usbd_uvc.c#L1330)
* [getting interval](https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/blob/main/subsys/usb/device_next/class/usbd_uvc.c#L573)


So your intervals end up being 10 times to small.  

EX 60 fps
* frame interval 166666/100000000 == 0.00166666


Purposed solution
* Is there any harm in keeping it in Nano seconds or even making the default Micro seconds?

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

```shell

```

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#106185.body:
### Describe the bug

While trying to run the Zephyr OpenThread Border Router sample, I have found out that application was consistently rebooting during initialization. Application main() function was never reached.
While debugging, I have found out that application reset was caused by a failure in socket_service_thread(), after `STRUCT_SECTION_FOREACH(net_socket_service_desc, svc)` was called, svc->pev_len contained huge values, totally different from what the application defined.
After investigating, I have discovered that struct net_socket_service_desc has a conditionally compiled field, `const char *owner`.
It seems that starting from this PR https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/commit/d74c1ab7c835c1ba2423bc0f65f746168421cff3, commit ID d74c1ab7c8, net_core.h header is not including net_log.h anymore. net_log.h included logging/log.h which in turn included logging/log_core.h which defined LOG_LEVEL_DBG.

Probably inconsistent include ordering caused structure size mismatch and therefore issues within the application.

Adding `#include <zephyr/logging/log.h>` in socket_service.h seems to solve the issue

### Regression

- [x] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Build OpenThread Border Router sample for RW612 
west build -p auto -b frdm_rw612 samples/net/openthread/border_router/ -- -DEXTRA_CONF_FILE='overlay-ot-rcp-host-eth-nxp.conf'

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Major – Severely degrades functionality; workaround is difficult or unavailable.

### Environment

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#106185.title: net_socket_service_desc structure size missmatch

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#106232.body:
### Describe the bug

Attempting to erase a region of flash storage from a PSOC Edge CM33 non-secure application that executes out of flash causes a fault in (or shortly after) `Cy_SMIF_MemEraseSector`. This is because the erase operation times out too early, causing the function to reenable interrupts and return before the flash device is ready to continue executing code from flash.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.


zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#106238.body:
### Describe the bug

When CONFIG_CAN_FD_MODE=y is enabled, flexCAN initialization failed.

The issue is introduced by the following commit:

0c4abcdd29fcb828bba7a82fee9e60f0ec377a67 drivers: can: mcux: flexcan: support Enhanced CAN Bit Timing registers

this commit updated the timing of flexcan, but it is not compatible with CAN FD on imx93/imx95/imx943 these platforms, so caused driver initialization failed.

The exthdr_len was previously validated against the total packet length, which did not account for the header's offset. This could allow a crafted packet to cause an out-of-bounds read by claiming a length that exceeds the remaining buffer.

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#106337.body:
### Describe the bug

`airoc_mgmt_scan` does not have a null check for `params`. Thus, performing a WiFi scan without specifying parameters causes a null pointer deferences. Other WiFi drivers do not require `params` to be non-null, causing misbehavior or faults when porting code tested against another WiFi module.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Request a WiFi scan with no parameters:

```c
net_mgmt(NET_REQUEST_WIFI_SCAN, iface, NULL, 0);
```


### Impact

Major – Severely degrades functionality; workaround is difficult or unavailable.

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#106375.body:
### Describe the bug

In net_context_get(), when an ephemeral port is allocated for a new socket, the sa_family field of the local address is not set before calling find_available_port(). The preceding memset() zeroes the entire contexts[i].local structure, leaving sa_family = 0.
This causes check_used_port() to skip both the IPv6 (sa_family == AF_INET6) and IPv4 (sa_family == AF_INET) collision-detection branches, making it always report the port as available - even when it is already in use by another context.
As a result, multiple sockets can be assigned the same ephemeral port. When listen() is later called, net_conn_register() detects the duplicate via conn_find_handler() and returns -EADDRINUSE (errno 112):

`<err> net_conn: Identical connection handler 0x20015838 already found.`

Fix:
Set addr6->sin6_family = AF_INET6 (and addr->sin_family = AF_INET for IPv4) immediately after the cast and before calling find_available_port(), so that check_used_port() enters the correct address-family branch and properly detects port collisions.

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

### Steps to reproduce

Reproduction:
Create multiple server sockets with dynamic port assignment (bind(port=0)) in rapid succession. On rare occasions (dependent on PRNG output), two sockets receive the same ephemeral port and the second listen() fails with EADDRINUSE.

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

### Impact

Annoyance – Minor irritation; no significant impact on usability or functionality.

### Environment

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#106375.title: net: context: Missing sa_family initialization causes ephemeral port collision

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#106381.body:
### Describe the bug

When using DHCPv6 without a static IPv6 address configured (CONFIG_NET_CONFIG_MY_IPV6_ADDR is empty), the IPv6 address and lifetime are never printed to the console, even though the equivalent DHCPv4 flow works correctly.
Additionally, the DHCPv6 client (dhcpv6.c) has no NET_INFO log when an address is received, unlike the DHCPv4 client which prints Received: <addr>.
Root cause
In subsys/net/lib/config/init.c, the setup_ipv6() function registers the ipv6_event_handler callback after the early exit for an empty CONFIG_NET_CONFIG_MY_IPV6_ADDR.

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#106438.body:
### Describe the bug

Mbed TLS libraries are created via `add_library()` / `add_subdirectory()` in `modules/mbedtls/CMakeLists.txt` and linked against `zephyr_interface` for include paths and compile options.

Unlike `zephyr_library()` targets (which get an explicit `add_dependencies(${lib} zephyr_generated_headers)` at `CMakeLists.txt:1093`), these external CMake libraries have no build ordering dependency on `zephyr_generated_headers`. Generated headers like `heap_constants.h` may not exist yet when Mbed TLS sources compile.

When `x509_crt.c` is compiled for a cross-compile target with `CONFIG_POSIX_API=y`, Zephyr's POSIX `<sys/socket.h>` shim is on the include path. The `__has_include(<sys/socket.h>)` check at `x509_crt.c:2674` succeeds, pulling in `zephyr/posix/sys/socket.h` -> `zephyr/net/socket.h` -> `zephyr/kernel.h` -> `zephyr/heap_constants.h`, which is not generated yet.

The root `CMakeLists.txt` already has a TODO about this at line 1092:
```
// TODO: Could this become an INTERFACE property of zephyr_interface?
```

### Regression

- [ ] This is a regression.

Introduced by commit `dee349919bd` in https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/104999 ("lib: heap: compute heap sizing constants from actual struct layouts"). The missing dependency existed before, but was latent — `kernel.h` did not include any generated constant headers beyond `offsets.h`.

### Steps to reproduce

```
west build -p -b qemu_x86 samples/hello_world -- \
  -DCONFIG_NETWORKING=y -DCONFIG_NET_UDP=y -DCONFIG_NET_TCP=y \
  -DCONFIG_NET_IPV6=y -DCONFIG_NET_SOCKETS=y -DCONFIG_NET_L2_OPENTHREAD=y \
  -DCONFIG_OPENTHREAD_SRP_CLIENT=y -DCONFIG_POSIX_API=y
```

Fails deterministically on every clean build.

### Relevant log output

```
In file included from zephyr/include/zephyr/net/socket.h:29,
                 from zephyr/include/zephyr/posix/sys/socket.h:13,
                 from modules/crypto/mbedtls/library/x509_crt.c:2675:
zephyr/include/zephyr/kernel.h:6247:10: fatal error: zephyr/heap_constants.h: No such file or directory
 6247 | #include <zephyr/heap_constants.h>
```

### Impact

Showstopper – Prevents release or major functionality; system unusable.

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Toolchain: Zephyr SDK 1.0.0
- Commit SHA: main branch (v4.4.0-rc1+)
- Regression commit: dee349919bd

### Additional Context

**Targeted fix** — add explicit dependency in `modules/mbedtls/CMakeLists.txt`:
```cmake
foreach(lib mbedtls mbedx509 tfpsacrypto builtin p256-m everest pqcp extras platform utilities)
  add_dependencies(${lib} zephyr_generated_headers)
endforeach()
```

**System-wide fix** (resolves existing TODO at `CMakeLists.txt:1092`):
```cmake
target_link_libraries(zephyr_interface INTERFACE zephyr_generated_headers)
```
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#106438.title: mbedtls: missing build dependency on zephyr_generated_headers

zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#106594.body: The RW610 RESET handler `POWER_GetResetCause()` returns a bitmask allowing multiple reset at once. However `z_impl_hwinfo_get_reset_cause()` uses a switch statement, which assumes a single value. If multiple bits are set, the logic may fall to RESET_UNKNOWN.
zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr#106594.title: RW610 multiple reset sources not detected
